titulo: TikTok considers London and other locations for headquarters
resumo: TikTok has been in discussions with the UK government over the past few months to locate its headquarters in London, a source familiar with the matter said, as part of a strategy to distance itself from its Chinese ownership.

titulo: Disney cuts ad spending on Facebook amid growing boycott: WSJ
resumo: Walt Disney  has become the latest company to slash its advertising spending on Facebook Inc  as the social media giant faces an ad boycott over its handling of hate speech and controversial content, the Wall Street Journal reported on Saturday, citing people familiar with the situation.

titulo: Trail of missing Wirecard executive leads to Belarus, Der Spiegel reports
resumo: Former Wirecard  chief operating officer Jan Marsalek travelled to Minsk soon after he was suspended and may still be in Belarus or Russia, a German magazine reported on Saturday.

titulo: Twitter says attackers downloaded data from up to eight non-verified accounts
resumo: Twitter Inc said on Saturday that hackers were able to download account information for up to eight accounts involved in the hack of its systems this week, but said none of them were verified accounts.

titulo: U.S. Republicans seek liability protections as coronavirus aid battle looms
resumo: A battle in the U.S. Congress over a new coronavirus-aid bill began on Friday as Republicans were putting the finishing touches on provisions granting liability protections for a wide range of entities resuming operations amid the pandemic.

titulo: Wall Street Week Ahead: Fund managers navigate 'Night of the Living Dead' in small caps
resumo: Investors are searching for bargains in the world of U.S. small-caps, as the beaten-down asset class prepares for what may be the worst earnings season in its history amid a resurgent coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Take Five: Hoping for that V-shape in earnings
resumo: We knew Q2 earnings would be dire but the hope was a) they might turn out to be better than feared and b) companies could offer guidance on the outlook. So while expectations are for European Q2 earnings to slump 50%-plus, there's margin for error.

titulo: Evictions nearly back to pre-pandemic levels in some U.S. areas: Cleveland Fed study
resumo: Landlords in some areas of the United States are filing to evict rent-delinquent tenants at roughly the same rate they were before the coronavirus pandemic as eviction bans across the country begin to expire, research from the Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland showed on Friday.

titulo: Google bans ads on coronavirus conspiracy theory content
resumo: Alphabet Inc's Google said on Friday it would prohibit websites and apps that use its advertising technology from running ads on "dangerous content" that goes against scientific consensus during the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Flight to suburbs boosts U.S. homebuilding, but COVID-19 surge erodes consumer sentiment
resumo: U.S. homebuilding increased in June by the most in nearly four years amid reports of rising demand for housing in suburbs and rural areas as companies allow employees to work from home during the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Delta will avoid pilot furloughs if they agree to reduced minimum pay: memo
resumo: Delta Air Lines told pilots on Friday it would avoid furloughs if they agreed to reduced guaranteed minimum pay, according to a memo seen by Reuters.

titulo: S&P 500 ends higher as traders weigh stimulus and virus worries
resumo: The S&P 500 ended higher on Friday as investors weighed the prospect of more fiscal stimulus against fears of further business disruptions due to a record rise in COVID-19 cases.

titulo: FTC considering deposing top Facebook executives in antitrust probe: WSJ
resumo: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission is considering taking sworn testimony from Facebook Inc's  top executives Mark Zuckerberg and Sheryl Sandberg as a part of its probe into whether the company has engaged in unlawful monopolistic practices, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday.

titulo: Where did all the bulls go? Rallies stall as EU summit begins
resumo: Global equity benchmarks treaded water on Friday and government bond yields edged lower as investors waited on the European Union to iron out details of an expected 750 billion-euro recovery fund that could provide another round of stimulus as the world economy reels from the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: BlackRock profit beats Street estimates as markets rebound
resumo: BlackRock Inc's  results topped Wall Street estimates on Friday, helped by investors flocking to the world's largest asset manager's bond funds in the second quarter as global financial markets rebounded strongly from a COVID-sparked brutal selloff in March.

titulo: Dollar falls, euro hovers at four-month high as EU negotiates rescue fund
resumo: The dollar was broadly lower on Friday as the euro rose to just under a four-month high, with negotiations underway between European Union leaders on a recovery fund that could lift the bloc out of the current recession.

titulo: Boohoo tells UK Home Secretary it is probing charges of malpractice in supply chain
resumo: Fashion retailer Boohoo Group Plc  wrote a letter to UK Home Secretary Priti Patel, saying that the company is taking action to probe allegations of malpractice in its supply chain and that it asks for the government to take action as well.

titulo: Exclusive: EU in talks with Moderna, BioNtech, CureVac to secure possible COVID vaccines
resumo: The European Union is negotiating advance purchase deals of potential COVID-19 vaccines with drugmakers Moderna , Sanofi  and Johnson & Johnson  as well as biotech firms BioNtech  and CureVac, two EU sources told Reuters.

titulo: Former Pemex boss arrives back in Mexico for graft trial, enters hospital
resumo: A former boss of Mexico's state oil company Petroleos Mexicanos facing corruption charges that could envelop leaders of the last government was taken to a hospital early on Friday shortly after his overnight extradition to Mexico from Spain.

titulo: Twitter says about 130 accounts were targeted in cyber attack this week
resumo: Twitter Inc disclosed late on Thursday that hackers targeted about 130 accounts during the cyber attack this week, an incident in which profiles of many prominent personalities and organizations were compromised.

titulo: Senator asks Twitter about claim worker was paid to help with hack
resumo: Senator Josh Hawley, a Republican who closely follows tech issues, pressed Twitter Chief Executive Jack Dorsey on Friday on whether a company employee had been paid to assist with a hack of high-profile accounts this week aimed at scamming readers.

titulo: Treasury's Mnuchin open to blanket forgiveness for smaller business relief loans
resumo: U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin said Friday policymakers should consider blanket forgiveness for all smaller businesses that received "Paycheck Protection Program" loans.

titulo: On eve of bankruptcy, U.S. firms shower execs with bonuses

titulo: Rival parties take aim at Germany's Scholz over Wirecard scandal
resumo: Germany's Social Democrat Finance Minister Olaf Scholz is facing calls from rival parties to account for the regulatory failures that led to the collapse of Wirecard after it emerged he knew of concerns about the company 18 months ago.

titulo: U.S. economy faces significant risks, long road to recovery: IMF staff
resumo: The U.S. economy is forecast to shrink by 6.6% in 2020 due to the shock of the coronavirus pandemic, but a resurgence in coronavirus infections and a systemic increase in poverty could worsen that outlook, the staff of the International Monetary Fund warned on Friday.

titulo: Euro zone shares flat as EU recovery plan awaited; Ericsson soars
resumo: Euro zone shares were little changed on Friday as investors awaited the outcome of an European Union summit where leaders are expected to hammer out details of a 750-billion-euro ($858 billion) recovery fund.

titulo: Cohen's Point72 to open Warsaw office for some back office teams
resumo: Billionaire investor Steven A. Cohen's Point72 Asset Management is opening the $16.2 billion firm's latest satellite location in Warsaw and moving some back office operations to lower-cost Poland.

titulo: In Utah, a debate stirs over Estonian radioactive waste
resumo: (This July 16 story has been refiled to fix spelling of 'whether' in paragraph 2)

titulo: End of the jumbo: British Airways retires 747 early due to coronavirus crisis
resumo: British Airways, the world's largest operator of Boeing 747s, will retire its entire jumbo jet fleet with immediate effect after the COVID-19 pandemic sent air travel into freefall.

titulo: Fed opens 'Main Street' loan program to nonprofits, eases terms
resumo: The U.S. Federal Reserve on Friday opened its "Main Street" loan program to nonprofits, allowing education, health, social service and other groups with as few as 10 employees to tap central bank funding.

resumo: Major U.S. bank executives this week said they extended forbearance programs to millions of credit card, auto loan and mortgage customers who were financially hard hit by the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: UBS, Morgan Stanley expected to lead Vodafone Tower IPO: sources

titulo: Exclusive: Thyssenkrupp to explore further options for boosting finances - memo

titulo: Economic recovery further away than markets think: BlackRock CEO Fink
resumo: The U.S. economy is going to take longer to recover from the hit from the coronavirus pandemic than the market expects, and more stimulus is critical, BlackRock Inc  Chief Executive Larry Fink said on Wednesday.

titulo: EasyJet pilots say they have no confidence in COO in union vote

titulo: Exclusive: HSBC sheds top talent as global equities boss leaves - sources
resumo: HSBC's  global equities chief Hossein Zaimi is leaving the bank, two sources with direct knowledge of the matter said on Friday, in the latest revamp of the lender's troubled investment banking operations.

titulo: Lloyds Bank sets target to increase number of senior Black staff
resumo: Britain's biggest domestic bank Lloyds  has set a target to increase the number of Black staff in senior roles to at least 3% by 2025 from just 0.6% now.

titulo: Wall Street analysts look past Netflix's weak subscriber forecast

titulo: Exclusive: Pact to aid poor cocoa farmers in peril as COVID-19 hits demand

titulo: Italy plans listing for Autostrade by early 2021: sources

titulo: Former Pemex boss Lozoya taken to hospital after arrival in Mexico

titulo: Bored bank customers flock to day-trading platforms during pandemic
resumo: Retail investing is having a moment. Major U.S. brokerages that reported quarterly results this week cheered the self-directed day trading happening on their platforms as individuals with a bit of extra money and time on their hands during the coronavirus pandemic have been engaging more in markets.

titulo: Stripe wins business following Wirecard's collapse
resumo: The U.S. payments company Stripe is picking up some former Wirecard  customers, an executive told Reuters.

titulo: TUI to offer free coronavirus insurance to lure travellers: report

titulo: With shift toward merit-based pay, Japan's Hitachi to drop old ways

resumo: The chairman of the Senate Commerce Committee asked Twitter Inc  to brief committee staff by next week on the hacking of high-profile accounts on Wednesday.

resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump will not stop using Twitter and his account was secure during a hack of the social media platform, a White House spokeswoman said on Thursday.

titulo: Negative U.S. rate bets persist, but seen unlikely to happen
resumo: Negative U.S. interest rates could be the norm next year and all through to 2023 if fed funds futures are any indication. Or it could just be traders hedging their bets.

titulo: Explainer: U.S. Congress readies $740 billion defense bill covering far more than bullets
resumo: The National Defense Authorization Act, which sets policy for the U.S. Defense Department, is one of the only major bills to reliably pass Congress and be signed into law every year.

resumo: Investors are suing Deutsche Bank and its CEO Christian Sewing, alleging the bank made false and misleading statements before it agreed to pay a $150 million fine for compliance failures linked to disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein.

titulo: Morgan Stanley posts record profit on trading boom
resumo: Morgan Stanley posted a record quarterly profit on Thursday that blew past analysts' expectations as another of Wall Street's big investment banks gained from huge swings in financial markets due to the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Twitter silences some top accounts after internal systems hacked

titulo: UK ban on Huawei opens door for competitors, although consumer may pick up tab

titulo: Exclusive: J&J in talks with Japan, Gates Foundation to lock in deals on COVID-19 vaccine
resumo: Johnson & Johnson  is in talks with the government of Japan and the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation about locking up allocations of its potential COVID-19 vaccine as it prepares to kick off human trials, the company's Chief Financial Officer Joseph Wolk told Reuters in an interview.

titulo: European stocks end off session lows ahead of EU summit

titulo: Exclusive: Natixis plans shake-up to focus on greener lending

titulo: Apple expands coding education programs to more historically Black schools
resumo: Apple Inc said on Thursday it was working with 10 additional historically Black universities and colleges to become "hubs" that will teach computer programming and app design skills in their regions.

titulo: EU regulators to probe Alexa, Siri and other voice assistants
resumo: EU competition regulators are seeking information from 400 companies to establish if there are problems in the market for voice assistants such as Alexa and Siri and other internet-connected devices that could lead to antitrust cases.

titulo: Irish regulator sees U.S. data transfers decided on 'case by case' basis
resumo: Ireland's Data Protection Commissioner, the lead data regulator for Facebook in the European Union, on Thursday "strongly welcomed" a ruling by Europe's highest court that a transatlantic data transfer deal was invalid.

titulo: Ebay nears $10 billion sale of classified ads unit: sources
resumo: Ebay  has called a board meeting on Friday to select a buyer for its classified ads business in a deal potentially worth up to $10 billion, sources close to the matter told Reuters.

titulo: Twitter hacking spree alarms experts concerned about the platform's security

titulo: Lagarde says ECB expects more grants than loans in EU recovery fund

titulo: J&J says large study of coronavirus vaccine could begin in September

titulo: Americans on COVID-19 jobless benefits spent more than when working, study shows

titulo: Instagram rolls out shopping feature in U.S.

titulo: State-run Air India working to contain costs

titulo: Bank of America profit slumps as heavy loan losses loom

titulo: Lagarde comments at ECB press conference

titulo: U.S. passive stock funds back in demand as investors seek steadier returns
resumo: U.S. passive equity funds have started to witness inflows after a two-month hiatus as investors flock to rising equity markets, but prefer shadowing indexes to picking stocks.

titulo: YouTube not liable for user copyright breaches, EU court adviser says

titulo: Uber acquires public transportation software company in latest transit expansion

resumo: (This June 9 story corrects to remove reference to speaking with Gupta at his office)

Having slumped 35% from Feb. 20 to March 23, they are now within 10% of February's record highs thanks to lashings of fiscal stimulus, interest rates slashed to 0% or below in most major economies, and massive amounts of QE. Borrowing costs for high-grade U.S. companies have in fact fallen below January levels.

titulo: United Airlines expects to raise $3 billion in debt offering
resumo: United Airlines  said on Tuesday it plans to raise $3 billion in debt by issuing senior notes, as U.S. airlines beef up liquidity in the wake of the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: U.S. business sector contraction eases in June
resumo: U.S. business activity contracted for a fifth straight month in June, but the pace of decline eased, supporting views that the recession caused by the COVID-19 crisis was drawing to an end.

titulo: American Airlines expects to raise about $2 billion in upsized stock, notes offering
resumo: American Airlines Group Inc  said on Tuesday it expects to raise about $2 billion through a stock-and-notes offering in an effort to bolster its balance sheet to better deal with a slowdown in travel due to the COVID-19 crisis.

titulo: Wirecard 'disaster' piles on pressure for corporate reform in Germany
resumo: Wirecard's  fall from grace prompted calls for an overhaul of Germany's system of corporate oversight on Tuesday, as the fraud scandal threatened to further undermine the country's once-prized reputation for integrity.

titulo: Explainer: Why COVID-19 can run rife in meatpacking plants
resumo: Meat-processing plants around the world are proving coronavirus infection hotspots, with an outbreak at a factory in Germany leading to Guetersloh becoming on Tuesday the first area in the country to be ordered back into lockdown.

titulo: Central banks hint at pandemic stimulus exit. Markets aren't buying it
resumo: As strict coronavirus lockdowns end, some central bankers have started hinting at another kind of exit -- from emergency stimulus they launched just three months ago. Markets so far appear to be calling their bluff.

titulo: Luckin gets another de-listing notice from Nasdaq, shares slump 18%
resumo: Luckin Coffee Inc  disclosed on Tuesday that it received a de-listing notice from the Nasdaq Inc  last week after it failed to file its annual report, sending the shares of the  Chinese coffee chain down about 18%.

titulo: Prosecutors in Munich say former Wirecard CEO to be released on bail
resumo: Prosecutors in Munich said on Tuesday that former Wirecard  CEO Markus Braun would be released as soon as he has posted 5 million euros ($5.66 million) in bail.

titulo: Fiat Chrysler close to green light for $7.1 billion state-backed loan, bank executive says
resumo: Fiat Chrysler (FCA)  is very close to obtaining the necessary authorisations to receive a 6.3 billion euros ($7.1 billion) state-backed loan from Intesa Sanpaolo , an executive at the bank said on Tuesday.

titulo: Exclusive: German finance minister points to regulatory failures in Wirecard scandal
resumo: German Finance Minister Olaf Scholz said lawmakers need to quickly determine how to tighten regulation in the wake of an accounting scandal at payments company Wirecard that has tarnished the reputation of Germany's financial watchdog.

titulo: SoftBank kicks off $21 billion sale of T-Mobile shares
resumo: SoftBank Group Corp  unveiled a series of transactions on Monday to divest more than $21 billion worth of stock in U.S. wireless carrier T-Mobile US Inc , as it seeks funding for a $41 billion share buyback and debt reduction plan.

titulo: Lufthansa says it has not yet reached agreement with unions on crisis package
resumo: Lufthansa  has not yet reached an agreement with unions on a package to cut staff costs, it said on Tuesday, adding to the pressure on the German airline before a crunch shareholder vote on a 9 billion euro bailout plan to be held on Thursday.

titulo: EU countries, lawmakers agree deal on class action lawsuits
resumo: European Union countries and lawmakers struck a deal late on Monday to pave the way for Europeans to team up and sue companies for defective products but with built-in safeguards to guard against the excesses of U.S. class action lawsuits.

titulo: Siemens, Salesforce aim to sell products for COVID-19 back-to-work plans
resumo: Siemens  and U.S. software developer Salesforce  will work together on products to help companies' employees get back to work while following rules to prevent the spread of the new coronavirus, the firms said on Tuesday.

titulo: ADNOC inks $10 billion deal, keeps tight control of costs amid market downturn
resumo: Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC) said on Tuesday it had signed a $10 billion gas infrastructure deal with a consortium of investors, while its chief executive told Reuters the company would keep a tight lid on costs amid low oil prices.

titulo: Luckin Coffee picks Houlihan Lokey for financial, strategic advice: sources
resumo: Luckin Coffee  has picked investment bank Houlihan Lokey  as an adviser, according to sources close to the matter, following an accounting scandal that has seen the Chinese coffee chain's shares plummet and creditors pursue assets held by the firm's family.

titulo: White House adviser Navarro walks back on comments China trade deal 'over'
resumo: White House trade adviser Peter Navarro on Monday walked back on his earlier remarks that the U.S.-China trade pact was "over", stoking volatility in markets already frazzled by the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: U.S. home sales hit nine-and-a-half-year low; price growth cools
resumo: U.S. home sales dropped to their lowest level in more than 9-1/2 years in May, strengthening expectations for a sharp contraction in housing market activity in the second quarter following disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Volkswagen sees V-shaped recovery lasting into 2022
resumo: Volkswagen expects a V-shaped recovery lasting into 2022, its sales chief said on Tuesday, as businesses try to work out how quickly they will rebound from the coronavirus pandemic which shut large parts of the economy around the world for months.

titulo: Singapore's central bank says received licence application from Wirecard
resumo: The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has received a license application from scandal-hit electronics payments firm Wirecard  under the country's new Payments Services Act, the central bank said on Tuesday.

titulo: Fed's gloomy economic outlook 'about right,' say economists: Reuters poll
resumo: The Federal Reserve struck the right tone in its first pandemic-era economic outlook, suggesting years of extraordinary policy support for an economy facing a slow and long slog back, according to a majority of economists in a Reuters poll.

titulo: U.S. fights bail bid by men accused of helping former Nissan boss escape
resumo: A U.S. prosecutor on Monday urged a judge to keep a former Green Beret and his son locked up as Japan prepares to formally seek their extradition on charges that they helped former Nissan Motor Co boss Carlos Ghosn flee the East Asian country.

titulo: Analysts' View: Take Navarro's China trade deal is 'over' comments with a pinch of salt
resumo: U.S. stock futures fell and the dollar rose after White House  adviser Peter Navarro said that the trade deal with China is "over". But President Donald Trump later tweeted that the deal "is fully intact" and said he hoped Beijing would continue to live up to terms of the agreement.

titulo: Virgin Galactic, NASA to develop program for private missions to space station
resumo: Billionaire Richard Branson's Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc  said on Monday it has signed up with NASA to develop a program to promote private missions to the International Space Station (ISS), sending the shares of the company up about 16%.

titulo: Google faces employee petition to end tech sales to police
resumo: More than 1,600 workers at Alphabet Inc are petitioning its Google unit to stop selling email and other services to police departments, a source familiar with the matter said on Monday.

titulo: Environmental shareholder activism comes to Japan as Mizuho faces climate resolution
resumo: More investors are publicly backing a resolution to curb coal project lending that shareholders of Mizuho Financial Group are expected to consider this week, the first time such a step is to figure at the annual meeting of a Japanese listed company.

titulo: Scores of Volkswagen's Mexico staff test positive for coronavirus
resumo: Volkswagen AG's  Mexican unit on Monday said about 2% of its workers tested for coronavirus had contracted the disease at some point, underlining the challenge faced by automakers in reopening factories before the pandemic has peaked in Mexico.

titulo: Factbox: Who is affected by Trump's new rules on work visas?
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump suspended the entry of certain foreign workers on Monday until the end of the year, a move the White House said would help the coronavirus-battered economy, but which business groups strongly oppose.

titulo: Apple switches to its own chips for Mac computers as it adds features, privacy controls
resumo: Apple Inc  on Monday said it will switch to its own chips for its Mac computers, saying the first machines will ship this year and ending a nearly 15-year reliance on Intel Corp to supply processors for its flagship laptops and desktop.

titulo: Wall Street ends higher with boost from technology stocks
resumo: Wall Street's three major indexes closed higher on Monday with the biggest gains in technology stocks as investors focused on the potential for more government stimulus measures even as they worried about an increase in coronavirus cases in the United States and other countries.

titulo: Spirit AeroSystems asks lenders for relief after deeper Boeing 737 production cut
resumo: Spirit AeroSystems Holdings , Boeing Co's  largest supplier, said on Monday it was seeking relief from its lenders as its finances are stretched by the COVID-19 pandemic and a 737 MAX production halt after deadly crashes.    Shares in Wichita-based Spirit, which builds the fuselage, thrust reversers, engine pylons and wing components for the 737 MAX, fell about 4% in after-hours trading.

titulo: Oil, stocks gain, but rising infection rates spark concerns
resumo: Crude oil prices and a gauge of global equity markets edged higher on Monday as lockdowns eased, with the Nasdaq setting a record closing high, but sentiment remained tenuous as coronavirus infections continued to rise.

titulo: Bayer wins court ruling restricting California's Roundup warning: Bloomberg News
resumo: Bayer AG won a court ruling blocking California from requiring the German-based company to tell consumers that a chemical in its Roundup herbicide is known to cause cancer, Bloomberg News reported on Monday.

titulo: Gilead targets remdesivir supply for two million COVID-19 patients by year-end
resumo: Gilead Sciences Inc  said on Monday it expects to be able to supply enough of its antiviral drug remdesivir by year end to treat more than 2 million COVID-19 patients, more than double its previous target of 1 million.

titulo: Oil up above 2% on tighter supplies, eased lockdowns
resumo: Oil was up 2% on Monday on tighter crude supplies from major producers and as coronavirus lockdowns kept easing despite a record rise in cases globally.

titulo: Dollar dips, Aussie gains as traders bet on pandemic recovery
resumo: The U.S. dollar weakened and higher-risk currencies including the Australian dollar jumped on Monday as investors focused on the prospect for an eventual economic recovery from the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: NYSE makes new push with the SEC for IPO alternative
resumo: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) submitted an amended rule change with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Monday in a bid to enable companies that debut on the stock market through a direct listing to raise capital.

titulo: U.S. Supreme Court rejects challenge to Trump's steel tariffs
resumo: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday turned away a challenge to President Donald Trump's tariffs on imported steel brought by an industry group that had argued that a key part of the law under which he imposed the duties violates the U.S. Constitution.

titulo: U.S. Supreme Court sets limits on SEC's power to recover ill-gotten gains
resumo: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday placed limits on the Securities and Exchange Commission's practice of forcing defendants to surrender profits obtained through fraud as part of its enforcement of investor-protection laws in federal courts.

titulo: U.S. Disneyland workers say proposed July reopening may be too early
resumo: Unions representing 17,000 workers at Walt Disney Co's Disneyland Resort in California have told the state's governor they are not convinced the theme park will be safe enough to reopen by the company's July target date.

titulo: PG&E says bankruptcy court approves its Chapter 11 reorganization plan
resumo: PG&E Corp said its Chapter 11 reorganization plan has been confirmed by the United States Bankruptcy Court for the Northern District of California, bringing the power provider one step closer to emerging from bankruptcy and participate in a state-backed wildfire fund.

titulo: Exclusive: EU in advanced talks with Johnson & Johnson on COVID-19 vaccine deal - sources
resumo: The European Commission is in advanced talks with pharmaceuticals giant Johnson & Johnson to reserve or make an up-front purchase of its COVID-19 vaccine under development, two officials familiar with the talks told Reuters.

titulo: Patagonia joins The North Face in Facebook ad boycott
resumo: Outdoor apparel brand Patagonia Inc will pause its ads on Facebook Inc  and Facebook's photo-sharing app, Instagram, making it the latest company to join a boycott campaign organized by U.S. civil rights groups.

titulo: Dior revives fashion shows - but with no front row
resumo: French luxury label Christian Dior said on Monday it would press ahead with a calendar of fashion shows for this year starting in July with an Italian catwalk display - but without the celebrated front-row audience of A-listers.

titulo: Germany in Lufthansa bailout talks with key shareholder
resumo: The German government held talks with Lufthansa's  biggest shareholder on Monday to persuade him to accept the terms of a 9 billion euro ($10 billion) coronavirus bailout that it has offered the airline group.

titulo: Carnival extends operations pause as cruise industry troubles persist
resumo: Carnival Cruise Line said on Monday it has extended its pause in operations for North American voyages until Sept. 30, as it determines how to safely resume service amid the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: 'Total disaster': Phantom billions plunge Wirecard into chaos
resumo: Wirecard said on Monday that 1.9 billion euros ($2.1 billion) it had booked in its accounts likely never existed, a black hole that threatens to engulf the payments company and tarnish the reputation of Germany's financial watchdog.

titulo: EU warns of competition concerns over LSE's $27 billion bid for Refinitiv
resumo: European Union antitrust regulators on Monday warned about the possible anti-competitive effects of the London Stock Exchange's $27 billion bid for data and analytics company Refinitiv as they launched a four-month investigation into the deal.

titulo: Google's U.S. ad revenue to take a hit due to pandemic: report
resumo: Google Inc's U.S. advertising revenue will drop 5.3% as brands pare spending during the coronavirus pandemic, according to an eMarketer report on Monday, the first decline since 2008 when the research firm began estimating the Alphabet Inc  unit's ad revenues.

titulo: South Africa to oppose Airlink's bid to halt SAA creditors meeting
resumo: South Africa plans to oppose an application by regional airline Airlink to put South African Airways (SAA) into provisional liquidation and prevent a meeting of SAA's creditors to discuss a restructuring plan, the government said on Monday.

titulo: Nigeria's WTO candidate gets regional backing: document
resumo: The West African regional bloc ECOWAS is backing Nigeria's candidate to head the World Trade Organization (WTO) and calls on other African countries to follow, a document showed on Monday, in a possible step towards uniting Africa behind her.

titulo: Who's bidding to be next World Trade Organization chief?
resumo: The World Trade Organization (WTO) began the process this month of selecting a new director-general to replace Brazil's Roberto Azevedo, who is stepping down a year early at the end of August.

titulo: German prosecutors may seek arrest of former Wirecard executives, say sources
resumo: German prosecutors may issue arrest warrants against former Wirecard bosses Markus Braun and Jan Marsalek as they investigate how $2.1 billion went missing from the electronic payments company, two sources said on Monday.

titulo: Credit Suisse says it will delist volatility ETN
resumo: Credit Suisse Group AG  said on Monday that it will delist several exchange-traded notes, including one linked to Wall Street's "fear gauge" that soared during the peak of the sell-off in U.S. stocks.

titulo: Germany's Scholz says Lufthansa talks were good
resumo: The German government on Monday held last-ditch talks about a 9 billion euros Lufthansa  bailout on Monday, Finance Minister Olaf Scholz said, as Berlin politicians seek to resolve a standoff with a key shareholder in the airline.

titulo: Live jewellery auctions resuming but online sales 'here to stay': Sotheby's
resumo: Rare coloured diamonds and historic gems will be seeking new owners at Sotheby's semi-annual sale in Geneva this week, as  live auctions resume after a suspension due to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Nokia to cut a third of jobs at French arm Alcatel-Lucent
resumo: Finnish telecoms equipment maker Nokia Oyj  plans to cut 1,233 jobs at its French subsidiary Alcatel-Lucent International, equivalent to a third of the unit's workforce, the group said on Monday, confirming an earlier Reuters report.

titulo: Unilever to invest 1 billion euros in climate change fund over 10 years
resumo: (This June 14 story corrects to remove reference to 2039 in second bullet, paragraph 7)

titulo: Musk says Tesla 'Battery Day', shareholder meet likely on Sept. 15
resumo: Tesla Inc  Chief Executive Elon Musk has said Sept.15 is the tentative date for the company's shareholder meeting as well as "Battery Day", at which the electric-car maker is expected to reveal significant advances in battery technology.

titulo: UBS may increase home working after coronavirus
resumo: A significant proportion of UBS's  staff could continue to work from home even after the coronavirus crisis has ended, the bank's Chief Operating Officer Sabine Keller-Busse said on Monday.

titulo: Delta to resume flights between U.S. and China
resumo: Delta Air Lines Inc  said on Monday it would resume flights between Seattle and Shanghai on June 25, making it the first U.S. airline to restart operations between the United States and China after flights were suspended in February due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Wall Street Week Ahead: Index reshuffle to give healthcare stocks a bigger profile
resumo: The U.S. healthcare sector looks set for a bulked up profile in growth indexes when FTSE Russell reconstitutes its stock indexes late next Friday, an annual event that historically creates one of the biggest trading volume days of the year.

titulo: Nokia must 'really significantly' reduce job cuts in France, finance ministry official says
resumo: Finnish telecoms equipment maker Nokia  must "really significantly" reduce its announced job cuts in France, an official at the French finance ministry said on Monday.

titulo: No clear path to salvation seen for stricken Wirecard
resumo: Wirecard's confirmation that $2.1 billion it had booked in its accounts likely never existed raises questions over whether the company is able to survive as a going concern or can be broken up and sold.

titulo: Pandemic propels old-school bond traders towards an electronic future
resumo: The mammoth bond market has long been the old-school bastion of the financial world, but the COVID-19 pandemic has cast a light on its future - and it looks electronic. Well, mainly.

titulo: Small, medium businesses feel brunt of lockdown pain: survey
resumo: Small and mid-sized businesses around the world are being hit hardest by the impact of COVID-19 lockdowns and about a fifth say they risk shutting down permanently within three months, a survey by the International Trade Centre (ITC) showed on Monday.

titulo: German minister: It's our duty to help Lufthansa, think we can reach consensus
resumo: German Finance Minister Olaf Scholz said on Monday he thought consensus could be achieved on Lufthansa  as a showdown between its biggest shareholder and the German government looms over the terms of a 9 billion euro ($10.09 billion) bailout.

titulo: Changing of guard at top German court signals de-escalation for ECB
resumo: Changes to the composition of Germany's top court are likely to make it less confrontational towards the European Central Bank following a ruling about bond purchases that sent shockwaves around Europe, according to two sources close to the court.

titulo: Toyota sees July vehicle output down 10% on initial plan
resumo: Toyota Motor Corp  said on Monday it would make 10% fewer vehicles next month than originally planned, as it gradually resumes output following factory closures earlier this year due to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Very clear Wirecard deposit certificate was 'spurious', BPI CEO says
resumo: Bank of the Philippine Islands Chief Executive Cezar Consing said it was clear that a certificate purporting to be for a Wirecard deposit was "spurious" and reiterated that no cash from the German payments firm had entered the bank.

titulo: Taiwan minister says TSMC has offset lost Huawei orders
resumo: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC)  has made up its order book with other customers now that it has lost China's Huawei Technologies Co Ltd [HWT.UL], which is subject to U.S. sales restrictions, a government minister said on Monday.

titulo: German finance minister says supervision institutions did their job on Wirecard
resumo: Regulators worked hard and did their job in the case of payments firm Wirecard, which on Monday said that 1.9 billion euros ($2.13 billion) missing from its accounts was likely never there, Finance Minister Olaf Scholz said on Monday.

titulo: German prosecutors probe all possible crimes in connection with Wirecard
resumo: German public prosecutors said on Monday that they were probing all possible criminal offences in connection with payments firm Wirecard after the company discovered a 1.9 billion euro ($2.13 billion) hole in its accounts.

titulo: German watchdog says Wirecard case is 'total disaster'
resumo: German financial watchdog Bafin on Monday said that developments at payment group Wirecard were a "total disaster".

titulo: Exclusive: U.S., Brazil protest Thailand's pesticide ban over impact on wheat, soy exports - documents
resumo: The United States and Brazil lodged separate protests with Thailand over its ban on two farm chemicals earlier this month, documents reviewed by Reuters show, saying the "restrictive" and "serious" move could hurt key agricultural exports.

titulo: Inflation dog may finally bark, investors bet
resumo: Gold, forests, property stocks, inflation-linked bonds - these are just some of the assets investors are pouring money into on the view that the recent explosion of government spending and central bank stimulus may finally rouse inflation from its decade-long slumber.

titulo: Exclusive: After BP takes a hit, investors widen climate change campaign
resumo: Investors managing £1.8 trillion ($2.2 trillion) in assets are widening a campaign pressing oil majors to better reflect climate risks in their accounting, and will soon target other businesses with heavy fossil fuel exposure, the group said on Monday.

titulo: Global dollar crunch appears over as central banks rely less on Fed backstop
resumo: A global crunch for U.S. dollars that was a hallmark of the early moments of the coronavirus crisis appears to have passed,  the latest milestone in a remarkable turnaround in financial conditions engineered by the Federal Reserve and other top central banks.

titulo: American Airlines seeks $3.5 billion in new financing
resumo: American Airlines Group Inc  said on Sunday it plans to secure $3.5 billion in new financing, to improve the airline's liquidity as it grapples with travel restrictions caused by the coronavirus.

titulo: China eases green rules for petrol-electric hybrids, giving makers space to manoeuvre
resumo: China re-classified petrol-electric hybrid vehicles on Monday so they get more favourable treatment than all-petrol or diesel counterparts under new clean car rules, making it easier for automakers to meet environment quotas and offer more choice.

titulo: Time is money: the armchair traders of lockdown
resumo: At 7am on Friday morning, Dean d'Arco, 31, a phone shop manager from Belfast, Northern Ireland, logged on for his last day as a round-the-clock armchair stock market trader.

titulo: Brands tout Black heritage as some shoppers question authenticity
resumo: L'Oreal SA, Unilever and other multinationals that acquired personal-care brands founded by Black people are moving to reassure their core customers, some of whom have pledged in the wake of racial-justice protests to direct their spending toward only Black-owned companies.

titulo: Lufthansa gears up for bailout showdown with investor
resumo: Lufthansa  will seek to avoid a grounding and insolvency, Chief Executive Carsten Spohr said on Sunday, before a showdown between the airline's biggest shareholder and the German government over the terms of a 9-billion-euro bailout.

titulo: Wirecard CEO exits as search for missing billions hits dead end in Asia
resumo: Wirecard's chief executive quit on Friday as the German payments firm's search for $2.1 billion of missing cash hit a dead end in the Philippines and it scrambled to secure a financial lifeline from its banks.

titulo: Wirecard's missing money didn't enter Philippine financial system, central bank says
resumo: None of the $2.1 billion missing from scandal-hit German payments firm Wirecard AG  appears to have entered the Philippine financial system, the central bank said on Sunday.

titulo: Huawei to receive planning permission to build 400-million-pound facility in UK: The Times
resumo: Chinese telecoms equipment maker Huawei Technologies Co Ltd is expected to receive planning permission this week to build a 400-million-pound ($494.24-million) research and development centre in Britain's Sawston village, the Sunday Times newspaper reported.

titulo: Wirecard seeks new financing strategy after Moody's junk downgrade
resumo: German payments firm Wirecard  said it had hired U.S. investment bank Houlihan Lokey  to devise a new financing strategy after Moody's slashed the company's rating to junk over the disappearance of 1.9 billion euros.

titulo: Elon Musk tweets Tesla postpones annual shareholder meeting
resumo: Elon Musk tweeted https://bit.ly/2AVPdDo late on Friday that Tesla Inc will have to postpone its annual shareholder meeting as no large gatherings are allowed by July 7.

titulo: S&P 500 closes lower as COVID-19 resurgence casts a shadow on sentiment
resumo: The S&P 500 ended lower on Friday after an up-and-down session as investors weighed spiking cases of COVID-19 and Apple Inc's announcement of fresh store closures against anticipated stimulus and continued economic recovery.

titulo: FedEx to record about $370 million in charges in fourth quarter
resumo: U.S. package delivery company FedEx Corp  said https://www.sec.gov/ix?doc=/Archives/edgar/data/1048911/000119312520174218/d949507d8k.htm on Friday it would record non-cash asset impairment charges of about $370 million in the fourth quarter, primarily related to its retail chain that provides printing, packing and shipping services.

titulo: Airbus extends furloughs in UK, Spain in latest effort to tackle COVID-19 fallout
resumo: European planemaker Airbus SE  said on Friday it is extending furlough programs for 5,300 of its employees in Spain and the United Kingdom in its latest effort to cope with the impact of the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Canada's Cineplex adopts poison pill
resumo: Cineplex Inc  said on Friday it adopted a shareholder rights plan, popularly known as a "poison pill", nearly a week after Britain's Cineworld Group Plc  abandoned its $1.65 billion deal to buy the Canadian theater chain.

titulo: Exclusive: Amazon signals entry into alcohol delivery in India with nod in key state - document
resumo: Amazon.com Inc  has secured clearance to deliver alcohol in India's eastern state of West Bengal, according to a document seen by Reuters, signalling the U.S. e-commerce giant's first foray into the country's multi-billion-dollar sector.

titulo: Boeing names new Space Station chief in latest change affecting program
resumo: Boeing Co's  International Space Station program manager Mark Mulqueen will leave the company next month and be replaced by the senior official overseeing Boeing's Starliner astronaut capsule, a spokesman said on Friday.

titulo: Fed officials signal rising caution on U.S. economic recovery amid virus spread
resumo: Two U.S. Federal Reserve officials sounded increasing pessimism on Friday on the swiftness of any economic recovery from the novel coronavirus epidemic and warned the unemployment rate could rise again if the disease is not brought under control.

titulo: U.S. shares retreat on renewed pandemic fears, safe-havens rise
resumo: Demand for safe havens rose and global equity markets turned south on Friday after Apple Inc said it would temporarily shut 11 U.S. stores as coronavirus cases continue to rise, rekindling fears of a deadly second wave of the pandemic.

titulo: UK tells telcos to stockpile Huawei gear in face of U.S. sanctions: letter
resumo: British security officials have told UK telecom operators to ensure they have adequate stockpiles of Huawei equipment due to fears that new U.S. sanctions will disrupt the Chinese firm's ability to maintain critical supplies, according to a letter seen by Reuters.

titulo: Dollar records small weekly gain on safe-haven demand
resumo: Fears of a second wave of coronavirus infections have increased demand for safe-haven assets, driving the U.S. dollar on Friday to its best weekly gain in a month.

titulo: Acknowledging derogatory term, Eskimo Pie owner says to change ice cream's name
resumo: The U.S. owner of Eskimo Pie ice cream will change the product's brand name and marketing, it told Reuters on Friday, becoming the latest company to rethink racially charged brand imagery amid a broad debate on racial injustice.

titulo: Running on 'hopium': Explaining the market rally in Wall Street's terms
resumo: Risk assets such as stocks and high-yield corporate bonds have climbed over the past two-and-a-half months despite a dire global economic outlook in the wake of the novel coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Volkswagen seeks open-source approach to refine car operating system
resumo: Volkswagen  wants to use an open-source approach to refine elements of a software-based car operating system being developed by the carmaker, Christian Senger, its board member responsible for digital services and software, said.

titulo: Oil boosted by OPEC+ cuts even as virus weighs on market
resumo: Oil prices rose on Friday but pulled back sharply from early highs on concerns that continued spread of the novel coronavirus could stall the United States' economic rebound.

titulo: U.S. current account deficit edges down; COVID-19 curbs trade
resumo: The U.S. current account deficit dipped to a near two-year low in the first quarter as the COVID-19 pandemic restricted the flow of goods and services, while companies continued to repatriate profits back home.

titulo: Take Five: Reshuffle the decks
resumo: After one of the most severe equity market selloffs in decades comes the June 26 "Russell Recon", the once-a-year re-jig of FTSE Russells's U.S. index range, tracked by over $9 trillion in assets.

titulo: Apple to shut some U.S. stores again due to rising COVID-19 cases
resumo: Apple Inc said on Friday it is temporarily shutting  some stores again in Florida, Arizona, South Carolina, and North Carolina in the United States, as novel coronavirus cases continue to rise in the country.

titulo: Gucci hikes handbag prices to curb coronavirus hit, says analyst
resumo: Gucci has raised the price of handbags by up to 9%, joining rivals Louis Vuitton and Chanel in a bet their brands will still lure the wealthy, a Jefferies report said on Friday, as top luxury brands try to shake off the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Incoming Renault CEO says he's ready for turnaround challenge
resumo: Renault's  incoming Chief Executive Luca de Meo said on Friday he was confident the ailing French carmaker, which has just launched major cost cuts, could deliver a positive surprise for investors after a period of faltering sales and margins.

titulo: U.S. economy will likely need more support, Fed's Rosengren says
resumo: The continued spread of the novel coronavirus could hamper the U.S. economic rebound from the pandemic, and more fiscal and monetary support will likely be needed, Boston Federal Reserve President Eric Rosengren said on Friday.

titulo: Discount grocers look to jump on home delivery bandwagon
resumo: Discount supermarket chains Aldi and Lidl look poised to accelerate their push into home delivery to satisfy burgeoning demand for online grocery shopping in a shift expected to endure beyond the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Trump renews threat to cut ties with Beijing, a day after high-level U.S.-China talks
resumo: President Donald Trump on Thursday renewed his threat to cut ties with China, a day after his top diplomats held talks with Beijing and his trade representative said he did not consider decoupling the U.S. and Chinese economies a viable option.

titulo: Top French court upholds $56 million Google privacy breach fine
resumo: France's top administrative court upheld a 50 million-euro fine ($56 million) imposed last year on Alphabet's Google for breaching European Union online privacy rules, it said on Friday.

titulo: Philly refinery sale expected to close next week for $27.5 million less
resumo: A sale of the Philadelphia Energy Solutions oil refinery site to real estate developer Hilco is expected to close next week for $27.5 million less than planned, the bankrupt refiner said in a court filing.

titulo: Deutsche Telekom to keep grip on T-Mobile in chase for U.S. top spot
resumo: Deutsche Telekom  will retain control of T-Mobile  should Japan's Softbank  liquidate its stake, CEO Tim Hoettges said on Friday as he vowed to claim top spot in the U.S. market.

titulo: Piraeus Bank hires advisers to auction off $7.8 billion of bad loans: sources

resumo: (This June 18 story corrects to say in para 5 that PAI Partners bought Roompot for 503 million euros (not 600 million euros) in 2016)

titulo: Europe's Just Eat Takeaway to buy U.S. delivery firm Grubhub for $7.3 billion
resumo: European food ordering firm Just Eat Takeaway.com NV  on Wednesday announced a $7.3 billion deal to buy U.S. food delivery firm Grubhub Inc  and create the world's biggest food delivery company outside of China.

titulo: Boeing supplier Spirit announces layoffs due to virus, 737 MAX impacts
resumo: Aircraft parts maker Spirit AeroSystems Holdings Inc  announced on Wednesday a 21-day layoff for staff doing production and support work for Boeing Co's  737 program.

titulo: Better three months late than never for Fed 'Main Street' loans, Powell says
resumo: It is not too late for small and medium-sized U.S. businesses to benefit from the Federal Reserve's "Main Street" lending program, Chair Jerome Powell said on Wednesday, even though its launch in the coming days will be nearly three months after the central bank announced the nearly $600 billion program.

titulo: Possible Fed move to cap yield rise could further weaken U.S. dollar
resumo: The U.S. dollar would probably come under further pressure if the Federal Reserve adopts targets for U.S. Treasury yields that would limit their rise and ensure that interest rates remain near zero for some time.

titulo: Tesla shares surge past $1,000 as Musk revs up the Semi
resumo: Tesla Inc's  stock jumped above $1,000 a share on Wednesday after Chief Executive Elon Musk told his staff it was time to bring the Tesla Semi commercial truck to "volume production."

titulo: Dollar, stocks slip as Fed signals slow growth
resumo: The dollar slid to a three-month low and a gauge of global stocks fell on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve projected a more sluggish recovery than the market expected, but the Nasdaq hit a fresh high on expectations of low interest rates past 2022.

titulo: ConocoPhillips CEO says surprised by swift recovery in oil prices
resumo: ConocoPhillips  Chief Executive Officer Ryan Lance on Wednesday expressed surprise at the quick rebound in U.S. oil prices that slipped into the negative territory in April and said he expects prices to remain volatile in the near term.

titulo: Nikola in talks with automakers for Badger pickup truck production: founder
resumo: Nikola Corp , a maker of hydrogen-fueled vehicles, is in the process of picking a partner to produce its Badger pickup truck, while also aiming to start deliveries of its electric commercial big rigs next year, founder Trevor Milton said on Wednesday.

titulo: American, Delta say demand uptick helping cash burn; United requiring health checklists
resumo: American Airlines Group Inc  and Delta Air Lines Inc  said on Wednesday that a modest recovery in demand was helping to slow daily cash burn rates in June after the U.S. government reported record low passenger numbers in April amid the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Instant View: Fed sees GDP falling 6.5% in 2020, keeps rates at zero
resumo: The U.S. Federal Reserve on Wednesday signaled years of extraordinary support for an economy facing a torturous slog back from the coronavirus pandemic, with policymakers projecting a 6.5% decline in gross domestic product this year and a 9.3% unemployment rate at year's end.

titulo: Oil edges higher, even as U.S. inventory rise revives glut worries
resumo: Oil rebounded from earlier losses on Wednesday, even as U.S. data showed crude inventories rose to a record high, reviving worries of a persistent glut due to weak demand.

titulo: U.S. Treasury chief says considering more direct payments in next coronavirus aid bill
resumo: U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin said on Wednesday he would seriously consider more direct payments to individuals in the next phase of coronavirus rescue legislation, adding that funds should also be targeted to help sectors struggling to reopen, including hospitality and tourism.

titulo: Dollar sinks to three-month lows as Fed keeps easing policy
resumo: The dollar extended losses, dropping to a fresh three-month low against a basket of major currencies on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve made no policy changes, as expected, and pledged to continue its asset purchases aimed at stabilizing a U.S. economy that has been ravaged by the novel coronavirus.

titulo: U.S. inflation subdued with economy in recession
resumo: U.S. consumer prices fell for a third straight month in May and underlying inflation was weak as demand for goods and services remained subdued amid a recession caused by the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Mall operator Simon Property abandons $3.6 billion acquisition of Taubman
resumo: Simon Property Group Inc , the biggest U.S. mall operator, said on Wednesday it was ending its $3.6-billion deal to buy Taubman Centers Inc , citing the beating the retail sector has taken during the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Congress seeks answers on Juniper Networks breach amid encryption fight
resumo: A group of U.S. lawmakers preparing to fight a legislative attack on encrypted communications is trying to establish what happened when encryption was subverted at a Silicon Valley maker of networking gear.

titulo: U.S. May deficit nearly doubles to $399 billion
resumo: The U.S. federal budget deficit in May nearly doubled to $399 billion from a year earlier amid continued strong spending on coronavirus relief programs and a 25% drop in receipts, the Treasury Department said on Wednesday.

titulo: J&J moves up start of coronavirus vaccine human trials to July
resumo: Johnson & Johnson moved up the start of human clinical trials for its experimental vaccine against the highly contagious coronavirus by two months to the second half of July, as the drugmaker rushes to develop a prevention for COVID-19, the company said on Wednesday.

titulo: OECD sees deepest peace-time slump in a century
resumo: The global economy will suffer the biggest peace-time downturn in a century before it emerges next year from a coronavirus-inflicted recession, the OECD said on Wednesday.

titulo: A casino or stock market? Retail buying frenzy goes wild
resumo: A raft of small cap stocks has soared by hundreds of millions of dollars in value in recent weeks as frenzied retail traders piled in to a blistering stocks rally.

titulo: Delta expects second-quarter revenue to slump, looks to amend debt deals
resumo: U.S. carrier Delta Air Lines  on Wednesday forecast a 90% plunge in second-quarter revenue and warned it would need to renegotiate its debt agreements to avoid a default next year.

titulo: Global risk rally has a powerful ally: the weaker dollar
resumo: Investors are betting a weaker U.S. dollar will further boost a rally that has seen stocks, emerging market currencies and other risky assets soar in recent weeks.

titulo: Goldman Sachs forms fund to address racial injustice
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc  said on Wednesday it had launched a $10 million fund to support the work of organizations addressing racial injustice, structural inequity and economic disparity.

titulo: UK watchdog extends deadline for final ruling on Amazon's Deliveroo deal
resumo: Britain's competition regulator has given itself two more months to make a final ruling on Amazon's  purchase of a minority stake in online delivery group Deliveroo so it can take full account of representations from all interested parties.

titulo: Walmart's Mexico business set to absorb two internal units
resumo: The Mexican unit of U.S. retailer Walmart said on Wednesday a shareholder meeting would discuss the absorption of two internal units, Holding de Restaurantes y Servicios and Tiendas Wal-Mart.

titulo: Emirates airline redundancies continue for second day, sources say
resumo: Emirates laid off more pilots and cabin crew on Wednesday in a second day of redundancies at one of the world's biggest long-haul airlines, three company sources said.

titulo: Ford, Volkswagen target 8 million vehicles in van alliance

titulo: Goldman traders gain $1 billion in commodities revenue after oil slump
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc  commodities unit generated more than $1 billion in revenue this year through May as traders positioned their bets for the collapse in oil prices, a source familiar with the group's finances said on Wednesday.

titulo: Twitter, Square to make June 19 a holiday to support racial diversity
resumo: Jack Dorsey, the chief executive of Square Inc and Twitter Inc, said June 19, popularly known as 'Juneteenth', would be a permanent company-wide holiday in the United States to show support for racial diversity.

titulo: Ford to return to pre-COVID production rates in U.S. plants by early July, COO says
resumo: Ford Motor Co  expects to have its U.S. vehicle assembly plants return by early July to building at the rates they did before the coronavirus pandemic shut down the U.S. auto industry for two months, a top executive said on Wednesday.

titulo: Airlines heading for $84 billion loss this year: IATA
resumo: (This June 9 story corrects to show combined 2020-2021 loss forecast about $100 billion, paragraph 5)

resumo: U.S. activist investor Cerberus is calling on Commerzbank  to appoint two of its nominees to the supervisory board, cut costs and adopt a new strategy, as it battles to make a success of a big bet on German banks.

titulo: Exclusive: ECB prepares 'bad bank' plan for wave of coronavirus toxic debt
resumo: European Central Bank officials are drawing up a scheme to cope with potentially hundreds of billions of euros of unpaid loans in the wake of the coronavirus outbreak, two people familiar with the matter told Reuters.

titulo: Fiat Chrysler plans to resume Panda production on June 16: union
resumo: Fiat Chrysler (FCA)  plans to resume production of its Panda small car at its plant in Pomigliano, near Naples in southern Italy, on June 16, a union representative said on Wednesday.

resumo: Social network company Reddit on Wednesday named venture capital investor Michael Seibel to its board, days after co-founder and former director Alexis Ohanian resigned and called for the company to replace him with a black candidate.

titulo: Exclusive: RWE, Thyssenkrupp plan hydrogen production venture
resumo: Germany's RWE plans to produce hydrogen from renewable energy to supply steelmaker Thyssenkrupp, the two companies told Reuters.

titulo: U.S. banks prepare branches for gradual post-coronavirus re-opening

titulo: NATO chief says on Huawei: UK review of 5G security is important

titulo: Germany should make provisions to ensure banks have sufficient capital: OECD
resumo: Germany should make provisions to ensure banks have sufficient capital in case of a wave of bankruptcies in Europe's largest economy caused by the coronavirus crisis, a senior OECD economist said on Wednesday.

titulo: Pompeo chides HSBC for 'corporate kowtow' to Beijing

titulo: Julius Baer faces investigation over ties to Argentine client: Neue Zuercher Zeitung

titulo: DoubleLine's Gundlach bearish on U.S. dollar, says stocks 'lofty'

titulo: FCC awarding up to $16 billion to address U.S. areas lacking broadband service

resumo: (This June 8 story corrects GDP decline in paragraph 6 to 5.0% from 4.8%)

titulo: Japanese traders gird for Fed to anchor yields, clip dollar's wings

resumo: Global banks are seeing renewed appetite from wealth management clients to borrow money to buy stocks as markets rebound, bankers said, which comes just months after the strategy burned some investors.

titulo: Factbox: Deals that may be affected by Australia's foreign M&A crackdown
resumo: (This June 5 story corrects paragraph 6 to show stake in common facilities being sold)

titulo: IBM exits facial recognition business, calls for police reform

titulo: Nasdaq ends at record, confirms bull market on economic recovery hopes

resumo: Macy's Inc  said on Monday it raised a total of $4.5 billion, including $3.15 billion in new borrowings against its real estate assets, as the department store chain tries to navigate through the fallout from the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: GE reopens parts of prior debt offerings to raise $3 billion
resumo: General Electric Co  said on Monday it reopened portions of its prior debt offerings for $3 billion in total proceeds to boost its cash reserves amid the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Stocks rally on quick economic revival hopes, oil slides

titulo: Nasdaq hits record high as U.S. recession becomes official
resumo: On the same day that the Nasdaq marked a new bull market, the U.S. economy was officially declared to be in a recession. The two milestones on Monday illustrate how an 11-week surge in stocks has occurred despite widespread economic devastation fueled by the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Ryanair's O'Leary says UK quarantine will be quashed or dropped

titulo: Nestle confirms sale of Buitoni brand's North American business

titulo: Nasdaq sets new record, close to confirming bull market in place

titulo: Exclusive: Volkswagen supervisory board to discuss VW brand leadership - sources

titulo: VW says Stefan Sommer, board member for procurement to leave

titulo: Exclusive: Fiat, PSA merger hits EU roadblock, may need concessions - sources
