resumo: Billionaire investor Daniel Loeb is taking back the reins as sole chief investment officer at his hedge fund Third Point LLC, less than one year after he appointed long-time colleague Munib Islam to be his co-chief investment officer, according to a letter the firm sent its investors.

titulo: GM delays tentative restart for plant in Silao, Mexico to May 21
resumo: General Motors Co signaled it has pushed back the tentative date for restarting operations at its auto assembly plant in the central Mexican city of Silao to May 21 from May 20, according to a message to workers seen by Reuters on Tuesday.

titulo: MGM, Caesars and Boyd will participate in employee COVID-19 testing plan
resumo: The Las Vegas gaming industry will offer COVID-19 tests to employees before they return to work, the Culinary Union announced Tuesday, moving the hard-hit sector a step closer to reopening.

titulo: Business group cautions U.S. on 'reshoring' too much China supply
resumo: The U.S. Chamber of Commerce on Tuesday warned the U.S. government against overdoing a major effort underway to rip U.S. supply chains out of China in the wake of the coronavirus pandemic, saying such moves could harm the economy.

titulo: Lockheed may delay delivery of up to 24 F-35 jets as COVID-19 hits suppliers
resumo: Lockheed Martin  said on Tuesday it will slow production of its stealthy F-35 fighter jets at its Texas facility, possibly delaying delivery of between 18 and 24 jets due to a parts shortage as the coronavirus hampers production across the jet's vast supply chain.

titulo: Early data on Moderna's COVID-19 vaccine insufficient: Stat News
resumo: Data from a small, early-stage safety trial testing Moderna Inc's  experimental COVID-19 vaccine does not provide the critical data needed to assess its effectiveness, health-focused Stat News reported on Tuesday, citing experts.

titulo: Ford requires visitors to wear masks but unclear if Trump will comply
resumo: Ford Motor Co  on Tuesday reiterated its policy that all visitors to its manufacturing plants must wear a mask but said the White House will ultimately decide if U.S. President Donald Trump will comply during a planned visit.

titulo: Euro gains on EU recovery-fund plan, oil wavers
resumo: The euro and European government debt rallied on Tuesday, lifted by a Franco-German proposal to fund grants for regions hit hardest by the coronavirus pandemic, while oil traded mostly higher on growing demand as countries eased business lockdowns.

titulo: Early data on Moderna's COVID-19 vaccine insufficient: Stat News
resumo: Data from a small, early-stage safety trial testing Moderna Inc's  experimental COVID-19 vaccine does not provide critical data to assess its effectiveness, Stat News reported on Tuesday, citing experts.

titulo: Trump administration signs up new company to make COVID-19 drugs in U.S.
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump's administration awarded a contract worth up to $812 million for a new U.S. company to manufacture drugs and drug ingredients to fight COVID-19 on American soil, aiming to end dependence on other countries.

titulo: Visa extends work-from-home for majority of employees through 2020
resumo: Visa Inc  will allow a majority of its workforce to work from home through 2020, according to Chief Executive Officer Alfred Kelly Jr., mirroring similar moves from other fintech companies earlier in the week.

titulo: Elderly home turns to wearables for contact tracing, sidestepping Apple-Google limits
resumo: When a senior living facility in Amarillo, Texas suspected a nurse may have caught the novel coronavirus this month, it had a list within five minutes of staff and residents the nurse could have infected.

titulo: U.S. economy won't reclaim all of its lost ground until after 2021: CBO
resumo: The U.S. economy should bounce back sharply in the third quarter after its deep coronavirus-related collapse, but it will not fully retake recover lost ground until sometime after next year, the non-partisan Congressional Budget Office said on Tuesday.

titulo: Coronavirus hammers U.S. homebuilding; permits tumble
resumo: U.S. homebuilding dropped by the most on record in April and permits for future construction tumbled, underlining fears that the coronavirus crisis would lead to the deepest economic contraction in the second quarter since the Great Depression.

titulo: Airlines say ticket cancellations slowing; business models may change
resumo: Top U.S. airlines and Air Canada  on Tuesday reported slower ticket cancellations and an improvement in bookings on some routes, though executives said overall demand remained weak and changes to travel rules may force new business models.

titulo: Nasdaq informs China's Luckin Coffee it plans to delist it
resumo: Luckin Coffee Inc  said on Tuesday that Nasdaq Inc  has given it notice that it plans to delist it from the  U.S. stock exchange a month after the Chinese coffee chain disclosed that some of its employees fabricated sales accounts.

titulo: Mnuchin says seeing large second quarter unemployment, negative economic data
resumo: U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin said on Tuesday that he is continuing to see large unemployment numbers and other negative economic indicators during the second quarter, but the situation is expected to improve as the U.S. economy starts to reopen.

titulo: Facebook to launch new shopping feature across apps
resumo: Facebook Inc  is launching Shops, a service that will allow businesses to display and sell products on the world's largest social network's platforms, Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg said on Tuesday.

titulo: As coronavirus crushes small restaurants, big chains see room to move in
resumo: Prime storefronts left empty by failed businesses. Cheaper or even flexible rents. Landlords willing to add drive-thru lanes.

titulo: White House panel: Build new tech infrastructure for future jobs
resumo: A White House advisory panel on Tuesday urged the government and private industry to work together on new technological infrastructure to support future jobs and underpin a solid economic recovery from the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: U.S. energy secretary criticized by advocacy groups for 'redlining' comment
resumo: Liberal groups criticized U.S. Energy Secretary Dan Brouillette on Tuesday for a comment they interpreted as comparing the reluctance of some banks to finance fossil fuels projects with discrimination against minorities.

titulo: American Airlines may use loyalty program as collateral for U.S. debt: President
resumo: American Airlines Group Inc  may use its Advantage loyalty program as collateral for a secured U.S. government loan, President Robert Isom said at a conference on Tuesday, noting that government talks are ongoing.

titulo: Fiat Chrysler's loan request raises doubts about 5.5 euro billion dividend
resumo: Italy could look into Fiat Chrysler's  planned 5.5 billion euro ($6 billion) payout as part of its merger with Peugeot after the Italian-American carmaker asked for a 6.3 billion euro state-backed loan, a senior government source said.

titulo: Lucky U.S. chickens fly the coop as pandemic hits egg demand
resumo: About 1,000 lucky chickens flew on two chartered cargo planes to a California sanctuary from an Iowa farm that has been euthanizing its flock as the coronavirus outbreak slashes restaurant demand for eggs, an animal rights group said.

titulo: Lufthansa expects hundreds of aircraft to be grounded until 2022
resumo: Lufthansa is bracing for hundreds of aircraft to remain grounded due to the coronavirus pandemic well into 2022 and that further job cuts at its maintenance and catering businesses were inevitable.

titulo: Walmart sales surge at stores and online on coronavirus-led demand
resumo: Walmart Inc  emerged among the winners of the coronavirus lockdowns, beating Wall Street's quarterly revenue and profit forecasts and setting an online sales record as millions of consumers stocked up on food and cleaning supplies.

titulo: GM says it is 'almost there' on million-mile electric vehicle battery
resumo: General Motors Co is "almost there" on developing an electric vehicle battery that will last one million miles, a top executive said on Tuesday.

titulo: France's Renault reaches deal on 5 billion euro state-backed loan: sources
resumo: French carmaker Renault  has sealed a deal with banks on a 5 billion-euro ($5.47 billion) state-guaranteed loan to help the company to cope with the coronavirus outbreak, two sources close to the matter told Reuters on Tuesday.

titulo: British retailer Asda's customers fear economic depression
resumo: British supermarket group Asda got a sales boost in the first quarter from consumers stocking up before the coronavirus lockdown but it also highlighted that 90% of its shoppers now fear an economic depression.

titulo: Asda CEO expects resumption of stake sale talks when Walmart ready
resumo: Talks between Walmart  and possible buyers of a majority stake in its British supermarket Asda will likely resume when the industry settles into a "new norm" with the worst of the coronavirus crisis behind it, the boss of Asda said on Tuesday.

titulo: Microsoft to adapt its cloud software for healthcare industry
resumo: Microsoft Corp said on Tuesday it plans to roll out a version of its cloud-based software that will be modified to suit the needs of healthcare organizations.

titulo: Home Depot misses profit estimates as coronavirus costs weigh
resumo: Home Depot Inc  missed estimates for quarterly profit on Tuesday, as the home improvement chain spent about $850 million on benefits for employees keeping its stores and warehouses running through the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Few U.S. oil and gas firms return small-business COVID-19 loans
resumo: U.S. securities filings show that only four of 12 listed oil and gas companies that received emergency government aid made available for small businesses said they would return it ahead of a deadline for firms that do not need the funds to do so.

titulo: S&P recovery will be far faster than 2008: Citigroup
resumo: U.S. stock markets will deliver among the steepest recoveries in their history over the next year, returning to levels from before March's coronavirus lockdowns more than twice as fast as after the 2008 financial crisis, according to analysts from U.S. bank Citigroup.

titulo: Citigroup launches new ESG investment banking group
resumo: Citigroup Inc  said on Tuesday that is launching a new business unit within its corporate and investment bank dedicated to environmental sustainability to strengthen its commitment to an area that has grown increasingly important to corporate clients and investors.

titulo: 'Nothing off-limits': Thyssenkrupp could sell steel business in historic shift
resumo: Thyssenkrupp could sell most of its steelmaking division, its CEO said on Tuesday, marking a historic reversal in strategy for the German conglomerate which has built its 200-year-old legacy as an industrial champion on the business.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp CEO: Cannot put timeline on restructuring
resumo: Thyssenkrupp's CEO on Tuesday said that she could not put a timeline on the group's fresh turnaround plans, saying that selling assets - a key part of its strategy - was difficult in the current environment.

titulo: Renault to cut 400 jobs at its Slovenia unit
resumo: French carmaker Renault  plans to cut about 400 out of some 3,200 jobs at its Slovenian unit Revoz after the coronavirus epidemic hit demand for its products, Revoz said on Tuesday.

titulo: U.S. housing starts fall sharply in April
resumo: U.S. homebuilding dropped to a five-year low in April, underlining fears that the novel coronavirus crisis would lead to the deepest economic contraction in the second quarter since the Great Depression.

titulo: United Airlines says air travel demand improving
resumo: United Airlines Holdings Inc  said on Tuesday it has seen a moderate improvement in demand for air travel so far in May in the United States and some international markets, as well as a reduction in cancellation rates.

titulo: 'All my dreams are shattered': coronavirus crushes Asia’s garment industry
resumo: Zarchi Lwin pawned her only two gold bangles for $140 when the owner of the Myanmar factory where she sewed winter coats for British retailer Next Plc shut it down after orders dried up due to the coronavirus.

titulo: Factbox: Fashion brands cut orders with Asian garment makers
resumo: As stores closed across Europe and the United States in March and April in response to the new coronavirus, many fashion brands and retailers cancelled orders for clothes, bags, and shoes worth billions of dollars from Asian garment factories, forcing them to close and lay off hundreds of thousands of workers.

titulo: Empty middle seat? Depends on which country you are flying in
resumo: In Thailand, you cannot have food or water in flight and must wear a mask. In Malaysia and Indonesia, the plane needs to be half-empty. In the United States and Europe, it's not mandatory for airlines to leave the middle seat open.

titulo: Phlow gets $354 million U.S. funding to ensure drug supply amid COVID-19 pandemic
resumo: U.S. drug manufacturer Phlow said on Tuesday it had received $354 million from the government to speed up the manufacturing of drugs being tested and used to fight the new coronavirus.

titulo: Southwest says bookings outpace cancellations in May
resumo: Southwest Airlines Co  said on Tuesday it recorded positive bookings on a net basis so far this month as passenger reservations outpaced trip cancellations, helping the company slow its cash burn rate.

titulo: Factbox: New measures being put in place for air travel differ by country
resumo: Governments, airlines and airports around the world are putting in place new measures to help protect travellers and allow for a return to the air during the global coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Past its peak? Battered oil demand faces threat from electric vehicles
resumo: Oil companies may be facing uncertainty as the coronavirus pandemic triggers a collapse in demand for their products, but auto makers are betting the crisis will help accelerate an electric future.

titulo: Mitsubishi Motors to cut costs after slump in annual profit
resumo: Mitsubishi Motors Corp  will focus on cutting fixed costs by 20% or more in the next two years after reporting an 89% drop in annual profit, its weakest performance in three years, and skipping its year-end dividend.

titulo: Exclusive: Delta will add flights to keep planes no more than 60% full as demand rises - sources
resumo: Delta Air Lines Inc  will keep planes no more than 60% full through at least July, adding more flights to its schedule than demand would usually justify, people familiar with the matter said.

titulo: Factbox: House cleaning at Thyssenkrupp could start M&A wave
resumo: Steel-to-submarines conglomerate Thyssenkrupp has changed the structure of its sprawling empire, splitting operations into three categories as it seeks to simplify the business, stop cash burn and revive its battered share price.

titulo: JPMorgan hands out $30 billion in loans to small businesses: memo
resumo: JPMorgan Chase & Co  has given out more than $30 billion in loans to over 250,000 businesses as part of the United States' coronavirus aid program for small businesses, Chief Executive Officer Jamie Dimon said in a memo on Tuesday.

titulo: Deutsche Boerse chief says plans to fulfil his term as CEO
resumo: Deutsche Boerse  Chief Executive Theodor Weimer said on Tuesday that he planned to complete his term through the end of 2024 at the helm of the stock exchange operator.

titulo: Disney's streaming chief Mayer to become TikTok CEO
resumo: Walt Disney Co's   top streaming executive, Kevin Mayer, will leave the entertainment and theme parks giant to become the chief executive officer of TikTok, the popular video app owned by China's ByteDance Technology Co, the companies said on Monday.

titulo: EU recovery fund should support aerospace sector: France's Le Maire
resumo: The aerospace industry should be among the sectors that can tap a planned European recovery fund, French Finance Minister Bruno Le Maire said on Tuesday.

titulo: Exclusive: Nasdaq to tighten listing rules, restricting Chinese IPOs - sources
resumo: Nasdaq Inc  is set to unveil new restrictions on initial public offerings (IPOs), a move that will make it harder for some Chinese companies to debut on its stock exchange, people familiar with the matter said on Monday.

titulo: Abu Dhabi's Etihad cuts hundreds of jobs: sources
resumo: Abu Dhabi's Etihad Airways has laid off hundreds of employees this month, including cabin crew, after the coronavirus pandemic forced it to ground flights, according to industry and company sources.

titulo: Tesla's China car registrations plunge 64% month-on-month in April
resumo: U.S. electric vehicle maker Tesla Inc  suffered a slump of 64% in car registrations in China in April on the month, data from auto consultancy LMC Automotive showed.

titulo: Fan-free sports and curbside retail as California loosens coronavirus rules
resumo: Businesses in the San Francisco Bay Area may open for curbside retail and manufacturing, while fan-free professional sports events might be allowed as soon as June, as California continued to loosen coronavirus health restrictions on Monday.

titulo: U.S. overtakes China as most attractive country for renewables investment: research
resumo: The United States has overtaken China as the most attractive country in the world for renewables investment and the global clean energy sector is expected to bounce back quickly despite the coronavirus pandemic, research showed on Tuesday.

titulo: Electric car sales to fall, but not as much as other vehicles: research
resumo: Worldwide electric car registrations are set to fall 18% this year, but those of combustion engine cars are set to drop even faster, analysts BloombergNEF (BNEF) said on Tuesday, as the automotive sector is hammered by the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: An easing of coronavirus prevention measures helps China's auto plants rev up
resumo: In the eastern Chinese city of Yuyao, a group of five face-masked workers at a Geely auto plant, stood almost shoulder to shoulder behind an SUV as they conducted paint and other quality checks.

titulo: Regulator asks for Credit Suisse directors' mobile data in spy inquiry: sources
resumo: Swiss regulators have requested electronic messaging data from the mobile phones of several Credit Suisse managers and supervisory board directors as part of a probe into spying at the bank, three people familiar with the matter said.

titulo: Long-lasting global recession likely due to COVID-19, says World Economic Forum report
resumo: Risk managers expect a prolonged global recession as a result of the coronavirus pandemic, a report by the World Economic Forum showed on Tuesday.

titulo: Financial gain trumps espionage as top motivator in cyber attacks: report
resumo: Money trumped spying as the top motivator for data breaches last year, according to Verizon's annual report on cyber crimes published on Tuesday.

titulo: Coronavirus vaccine from Moderna appears safe, shows promise in data from eight people
resumo: Moderna Inc's  experimental COVID-19 vaccine, the first to be tested in the United States, produced protective antibodies in a small group of healthy volunteers, according to very early data released by the biotech company on Monday.

titulo: Australia's Qantas set to ramp up domestic flights without social distancing
resumo: Qantas Airways Ltd could restart 40-50% of its domestic capacity in July if states relax border controls, and expects to offer low and flexible fares without social distancing measures to stimulate travel demand, its chief executive said on Tuesday.

titulo: SEC settles charges against three former KPMG auditors
resumo: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Monday said it settled charges against three former KPMG LLP audit partners stemming from a cheating scandal at the firm.

titulo: Starbucks says reopening many stores in Japan
resumo: Starbucks Coffee Japan said it was reopening many of its stores in Japan, including Tokyo, starting on Tuesday, although many of them will be limited to take-out and drive-through services.

titulo: SoftBank in talks to sell down T-Mobile U.S. stake to Deutsche Telekom: WSJ
resumo: SoftBank Group Corp is in talks to sell "a significant portion" of its T-Mobile US stake to controlling shareholder Deutsche Telekom AG, the Wall Street Journal reported on Monday, citing unidentified sources.

titulo: S&P 500 closes at 10-week high on vaccine hopes, stimulus pledge
resumo: U.S. stocks jumped on Monday, and the S&P 500 closed at a 10-week high, on encouraging early-stage data for a potential coronavirus vaccine and on the promise of more stimulus to lift an economy beaten down by the pandemic.

titulo: Uber to focus on core rides, delivery business as it cuts 23% of workforce
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc  will concentrate on its core businesses in ride-hailing and food delivery and cut 23% of its workforce in an attempt to become profitable despite the coronavirus pandemic, Chief Executive Officer Dara Khosrowshahi said in an email to employees on Monday.

titulo: NIBC says it will pay 2019 dividend to Blackstone to ensure acquisition
resumo: Dutch bank NIBC Holding NV  said late on Monday it has agreed to pay its 2019 dividend to its proposed buyer, U.S. equity firm Blackstone Group Inc , before the deal settles, in order to remove one hurdle to the proposed 1.36 billion-euro ($1.47 billion) takeover.

titulo: Supplier restarts flow of critical truck parts to Detroit automakers

titulo: As flying returns, jetmakers seek to quell fears over cabin air
resumo: Jet manufacturers and airlines are launching an urgent initiative to convince nervous travelers that the air they breathe on planes is safe, believing this is critical to rebuilding a travel industry floored by the novel coronavirus.

titulo: Delta to resume flying several major routes in June
resumo: Delta Air Lines  said on Monday it would resume flying several major routes in June that were suspended due to the coronavirus pandemic, including some Trans-Atlantic and Caribbean destinations.

titulo: Square joins Twitter in letting employees work from home permanently
resumo: Jack Dorsey-led Square Inc said on Monday that most of its employees can continue to work from home permanently even after offices reopen, following a similar announcement from Twitter Inc, which is also headed by the billionaire.

titulo: Global shares, oil rally on vaccine hopes, lockdown easing
resumo: A gauge of global equity markets surged almost 3% on Monday and oil rallied to highs last seen in mid-April as data from an early-stage trial for a coronavirus vaccine lifted hopes of a faster recovery from the pandemic-driven economic slump.

titulo: Trump says should be possible to allow PPP borrowers more time to use loans
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday extending the amount of time borrowers in the Paycheck Protection Program have to spend their funds to 24 weeks from the current eight weeks "should be easy."

titulo: FCA has to strengthen investments in Italy to seek state-backed loan: Economy Minister
resumo: Italy's Economy Minister Roberto Gualtieri on Monday said Fiat Chrysler [FCHA.MI] has to strengthen investments in Italy and avoid delocalizing its factories outside the national borders to apply for a state backed loan.

titulo: Oil jumps to two-month high on easing lockdowns, positive vaccine results
resumo: Oil prices on Monday jumped to their highest in over two months on positive early results on a potential coronavirus vaccine, optimism about a resumption in economic activity and signs producers were following through on planned output reductions.

titulo: More than 8% of U.S. mortgages now in forbearance: MBA weekly survey
resumo: Roughly 4.1 million U.S. mortgage borrowers have had their payments paused or reduced as the novel coronavirus outbreak hits household finances, but the increase in the number of people needing such help is slowing, the latest weekly survey from the Mortgage Bankers Association showed on Monday.

titulo: Automated delivery cashes in on pandemic-driven demand
resumo: The coronavirus crisis is accelerating a shift in the world of autonomous cars toward delivering packages instead of people, as big players open up a lead over startups in the race for funding.

titulo: J.C. Penney to permanently shut about 242 stores as part of bankruptcy plan
resumo: J.C. Penney Co Inc plans to trim its store count by 29% to about 604, as the U.S. department store chain looks to focus on those more profitable, a regulatory filing showed on Monday, two days after it filed for bankruptcy protection.

titulo: Ailing icon Thyssenkrupp seeks partners for steel, warship units
resumo: Thyssenkrupp  on Monday said it was looking for partners for its steel and warship divisions, singling out just three lines of businesses that will stay within the struggling German industrial icon.

titulo: U.S. auto industry returns to life after lockdown
resumo: The Detroit Three automakers and their suppliers began restarting assembly lines on Monday after a two-month coronavirus lockdown in a slow revival of a sector that employs nearly 1 million people in the United States.

titulo: Exclusive: U.S. probes PerkinElmer's role in massive Medicare fraud - sources
resumo: Medical diagnostic firm PerkinElmer Inc faces a federal investigation into its role in an alleged Medicare fraud involving tens of thousands of unnecessary genetic cancer tests, according to three sources with knowledge of the probe and documents reviewed by Reuters.

titulo: U.S. Senator Klobuchar blasts Uber, Grubhub deal talks
resumo: U.S. Senator Amy Klobuchar on Sunday blasted merger talks between ride-hailing firm Uber Technologies Inc and online food delivery company Grubhub Inc, calling any deal bad for competition and consumers.

titulo: JPMorgan fills senior leadership roles after February makeover
resumo: JPMorgan  has made a number of changes in senior positions in its investment bank in the Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA) region to support a February reshuffle of the top roles, according to an internal memo seen by Reuters.

titulo: Total calls off plan to buy Occidental Petroleum's Ghana assets
resumo: Total  has called off a plan to acquire Occidental Petroleum's  assets in Ghana, which was conditional on the completion of the acquisition of Occidental's other assets in Algeria, the French energy company said on Monday.

titulo: IMF chief warns full global economic recovery unlikely in 2021
resumo: The global economy will take much longer to recover fully from the shock caused by the new coronavirus than initially expected, the head of the International Monetary Fund said, and she stressed the danger of protectionism.

titulo: Huawei calls U.S. move to curb chips supply 'arbitrary', expects business impact
resumo: Huawei Technologies in its first official response to the Trump administration's move to curb its access to global chip supplies called it "arbitrary" and said its business would be impacted.

titulo: Amazon's French warehouses to reopen with 30% staff: unions
resumo: Amazon's French warehouses are set to reopen on Tuesday with about 30% of employees, three union officials said, ending a month-long dispute over whether the e-commerce giant was doing enough to protect workers from the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Walmart trailed supermarkets amid peak panic-buying: data
resumo: Americans increased their visits to supermarkets far more than to Walmart Inc  stores as panic-buying peaked in mid-March, a Reuters analysis of foot traffic to retailers in the United States shows.

titulo: SoftBank's Son hopes 'winged unicorns' can save crumbling tech empire
resumo: SoftBank Group Corp  CEO Masayoshi Son on Monday pinned his hopes on a small group of "winged unicorns" to save the performance of his $100 billion Vision Fund - although he gave few clues on which ones they would be.

titulo: U.S. homebuilder confidence rises in May
resumo: Confidence among U.S. single-family homebuilders rose in May, potentially signaling that the worst of the economic downturn was probably over as the country gradually reopens after lockdowns to slow the spread of the novel coronavirus.

titulo: Exclusive: Nissan's survival plan for Europe: SUVs and sharing with Renault
resumo: Nissan Motor Co  is working on a plan to scale back its European business, focusing on sport utility and commercial vehicles as well as more effective sharing of products and investments with partner Renault, three sources told Reuters.

titulo: Starbucks reopens in Italy but keeps Milan flagship closed
resumo: Italians will be able to grab a cup of their favorite Starbucks coffee again after the group said on Monday it would reopen its Italian cafes, with the exception of its flagship location in central Milan.

titulo: A California cafe reopens with a payroll loan, but the future unclear
resumo: Chris and Amy Hillyard opened their Oakland, California restaurant late last month for the first time since mid-March, thanks in large part to a government loan aimed at tiding over small firms during the worst of the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Coronavirus widens climate rift between European and U.S. oil majors
resumo: Europe's top oil and gas companies have diverted a larger share of their cash to green energy projects since the coronavirus outbreak in a bet the global health crisis will leave a long-term dent in fossil fuel demand, according to a Reuters review of company statements and interviews with executives.

titulo: Ethiopian Airlines sees Boeing 737 MAX compensation deal by end-June
resumo: Ethiopian Airlines expects a settlement with planemaker Boeing  by end of June over compensation related to the 737 MAX grounding in March 2019 following two fatal crashes, CEO Tewolde Gebremariam told Reuters on Friday.

titulo: U.S. car rental firm Hertz names new CEO
resumo: Hertz Global Holdings Inc  on Monday appointed Paul Stone as its chief executive officer days after the U.S. car rental company raised going-concern doubts following a bigger quarterly loss.

titulo: U.S. mulls paying companies, tax breaks to pull supply chains from China
resumo: U.S. lawmakers and officials are crafting proposals to push American companies to move operations or key suppliers out of China that include tax breaks, new rules, and carefully structured subsidies.

titulo: Trump admin slaps solar, wind operators with retroactive rent bills
resumo: The Trump administration has ended a two-year rent holiday for solar and wind projects operating on federal lands, handing them whopping retroactive bills at a time the industry is struggling with the fallout of the coronavirus outbreak, according to company officials.

titulo: Factbox: Investors pour billions into automated delivery startups
resumo: Since September, investors have pumped at least $6 billion into more than two dozen startups that are automating delivery of goods and food, from heavy trucks to sidewalk robots and drones.

titulo: SoftBank's Vision Fund tumbles to $18 billion loss in 'valley of coronavirus'
resumo: Japan's SoftBank Group Corp reported a stunning $18 billion loss at its giant Vision Fund, pushing Masayoshi Son's conglomerate to a record loss and highlighting the deepening crisis at its portfolio companies from the global downturn.

titulo: SoftBank to raise over $11 billion for buybacks using Alibaba shares
resumo: Softbank Group will raise 1.25 trillion yen ($11.7 billion) using Alibaba stock to fund buybacks of its own shares, Chief Executive Masayoshi Son said on Monday.

titulo: What did eight weeks and $3 trillion buy the U.S. in the fight against coronavirus?
resumo: Unemployment checks are flowing, $490 billion has been shipped to small businesses, and the U.S. Federal Reserve has put about $2.5 trillion and counting behind domestic and global markets.

titulo: Tech firms sweeten deals for U.S. banks cutting costs in crisis
resumo: Top technology services firms are offering payment deferrals, discounts of up to 20% and other sweeteners to some U.S. banks to keep their business as the pandemic forces Wall Street to cut tech budgets, according to executives involved in the talks.

titulo: Panasonic sees strong demand from Tesla, in talks to expand Nevada battery plant
resumo: Panasonic Corp's  finance chief said the company is seeing strong demand for battery cells from U.S. partner Tesla  and they are in talks to expand their joint plant in Nevada, which is now profitable.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp steel workers open to consolidation talks - labor boss
resumo: Thyssenkrupp's  powerful labor representatives are open to consolidation talks in the steel sector, a leading union official told Reuters on Monday.

titulo: Fed's Powell: 'Medical metrics' most important data for U.S. economy now: CBS
resumo: The most important data for the U.S. economy right now are the "medical metrics" around the coronavirus pandemic, Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell said Sunday night in broadcast remarks where he outlined the likely need for three to six more months of government financial help for firms and families.

titulo: Britain, U.S. hopeful trade talks can proceed at pace: London
resumo: Britain and the United States are hopeful that negotiations for a trade agreement can proceed at an accelerated pace, Britain's department for trade said on Monday in an update on the talks after the first round concluded last week.

titulo: Japan braces for worst postwar slump as pandemic tips economy into recession
resumo: Japan's economy slipped into recession for the first time in 4-1/2 years in the last quarter, putting the nation on course for its deepest postwar slump as the coronavirus crisis ravages businesses and consumers.

titulo: Alibaba's Jack Ma resigns from SoftBank board
resumo: SoftBank Group Corp said on Monday that Alibaba co-founder Jack Ma will resign from its board, in the latest departure by a high-profile ally of CEO Masayoshi Son.

titulo: Apple reopening 25 more U.S. stores, will soon top 100 worldwide
resumo: Apple Inc will this week reopen more than 25 of its branded stores in the United States, the company said on Sunday, continuing a gradual process that has unlocked doors at nearly a fifth of its worldwide retail outlets.

titulo: TSMC stops new Huawei orders after U.S. restrictions: Nikkei
resumo: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co Ltd has stopped new orders from Huawei Technologies [HWT.UL] in response to Washington's move aimed at further limiting chip supplies to the Chinese company, the Nikkei reported on Monday, citing multiple sources.

titulo: SoftBank in talks to sell down T-Mobile U.S. stake to Deutsche Telekom - WSJ
resumo: SoftBank Group Corp is in talks to sell "a significant portion" of its T-Mobile US stake to controlling shareholder Deutsche Telekom AG, the Wall Street Journal reported on Monday citing unidentified sources.

titulo: Japan's economy to slump significantly due to pandemic: economy minister

titulo: GM tells Mexico workers it plans May 20 restart at Silao plant

resumo: Casual clothing chain Uniqlo has reopened more stores in the past week and plans to resume business at more shops in coming days as governments ease coronavirus restrictions, its owner Fast Retailing  said on Thursday.

titulo: Uber to require drivers, riders to wear masks around the world
resumo: Uber Technologies, Inc.  on Wednesday said it will require drivers, delivery workers and riders globally to wear a mask as countries around the world begin easing restrictions aimed at curbing the spread of the coronavirus.

titulo: Uber approaches Grubhub with acquisition offer: sources
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc is in negotiations to buy online food delivery company Grubhub Inc in an all-stock deal, according to people familiar with the matter.

titulo: Wall Street ends down on Powell's sober outlook, call to Congress for help
resumo: Wall Street's three major indexes closed lower for the second day in a row after Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell warned on Wednesday of extended economic weakness due to the coronavirus pandemic and called for Congress to agree on additional fiscal support.

titulo: Mexico green lights auto industry restart, heeding U.S. calls
resumo: Mexico will begin opening some automotive factories from May 18 under a plan unveiled on Wednesday that loosens coronavirus restrictions and paves the way for U.S. car giants to ramp up output dependent on parts made south of the border.

titulo: Less than zero? Fed's Powell shows no love for negative rates
resumo: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell had a clear message to interest rate futures traders on Wednesday: Bets that the U.S. central bank will pursue a negative interest-rate policy are off-base.

titulo: U.S. March, April job losses revised higher
resumo: The U.S. economy lost a record 20.537 million jobs in April and not 20.5 million as reported last Friday, according to revised data published by the Labor Department this week.

titulo: Brazil's Gol values Boeing's 737 MAX compensation at up to $412 million
resumo: Brazil airline Gol Linhas Aereas Inteligentes  said on Wednesday it will receive up to 2.4 billion reais ($412 million) from Boeing Co  as compensation for the grounding of the 737 MAX plane, in a mix of cash and credits.

titulo: Tesla, California county reach deal to reopen U.S. plant next week
resumo: Tesla Inc and officials in California have resolved their acrimonious clash over safety procedures at the automaker’s sole U.S. assembly plant with a deal that allows production to resume as early as Monday, county officials said.

titulo: Equities slide, bonds rise on Fed warning of prolonged recession
resumo: Stock markets tumbled on Wednesday on fears about a second wave of coronavirus infections and warnings from Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell that the U.S. faces a "significantly worse" recession than any downturn since World War Two, while bonds rose on a safety bid.

titulo: Airbus examining restructuring including job cuts -sources
resumo: Airbus  is exploring restructuring plans involving the possibility of "deep" job cuts as it braces for a prolonged coronavirus crisis after furloughing thousands of workers, industry sources said, though no decision is imminent.

titulo: Climate activists up stakes at Exxon, backing calls for independent board chairman
resumo: Shareholder activists prodding Exxon Mobil Corp on climate-change proposals are backing calls for an independent board chairman as the oil major steps up efforts to keep climate proposals off its ballot.

titulo: Amazon.com asks for federal law against price gouging during national emergencies
resumo: Online retailer Amazon.com, criticized for not acting quickly enough to curb sellers who charged hundreds of dollars for high-demand hand sanitizer during the early phase of the new coronavirus pandemic, urged Congress on Wednesday to pass a law against price gouging during times of national emergency.

titulo: In nod to grim U.S. outlook, Fed's Powell calls for more fiscal support
resumo: The head of the Federal Reserve warned on Wednesday of an "extended period" of weak economic growth, vowed to use the U.S. central bank's power as needed, and called for additional fiscal spending to stem the fallout from the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Fed Chair Powell warns of prolonged U.S. recession after coronavirus
resumo: Jerome Powell, chairman of the Federal Reserve board of governors, warned Wednesday that the long-term health of the U.S. economy may depend on more fiscal stimulus to fight the economic impacts of the coronavirus.

titulo: Powell: Fed not looking at negative rates
resumo: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, in a sober review of where the U.S. economy stands on the cusp of its reopening, said on Wednesday the country could face an "extended period" of weak growth and stagnant incomes, pledged to use more Fed power as needed, and issued a call for more fiscal spending.

titulo: Deutsche Bank criticised in internal N.Y. Fed audit: German newspaper
resumo: Deutsche Bank's  regulators in the United States have criticised the German lender in an internal audit for weaknesses in fighting money laundering and in risk management, according to a German newspaper on Wednesday.

titulo: Amazon extends temporary raise in pay through May 30
resumo: Amazon.com Inc  said on Wednesday it would extend the temporary increase in its hourly pay and double the overtime pay through May 30, bringing its total salary cost for hourly employees to nearly $800 million.

titulo: Exclusive: U.S. airlines tell crews not to force passengers to wear masks
resumo: The top three U.S. airlines have told their flight attendants not to force passengers to comply with their new policy requiring face coverings, just encourage them to do so, according to employee policies reviewed by Reuters.

titulo: Return of Big Mac - McDonald's UK emerges from lockdown
resumo: After over seven weeks of coronavirus lockdown, Britons hankering for a Big Mac may be a step closer to getting their hands on one after McDonald's  reopened 15 restaurants on Wednesday for delivery only.

titulo: Japan´s Softbank sees consolidation in Latam food delivery
resumo: Japan's SoftBank Group Corp , a shareholder in some of Latin America's largest food delivery companies, expects consolidation in most markets, a senior executive said on Wednesday.

titulo: U.S. producer prices record largest drop since 2009 as coronavirus suppresses demand
resumo: U.S. producer prices fell by the most since 2009 in April, leading to the largest annual decline in nearly 4-1/2 years, bolstering some economists' predictions for a brief period of deflation as the novel coronavirus depresses demand.

titulo: Exclusive: Europe nears crisis plan for public stakes in key companies - sources
resumo: EU officials are seeking to finalise a plan for public stakes to be taken in key European companies to shield them from the economic fallout of the COVID-19 pandemic, three sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

titulo: OPEC slashes oil demand forecast again, sees biggest hit this quarter
resumo: OPEC slashed its forecast on Wednesday for global oil demand this year and predicted this quarter would see the steepest decline even as some countries ease lockdown measures designed to stem the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Wall Street bonuses set to fall by as much as 30% in 2020: report
resumo: Wall Street bonuses for 2020 could fall by as much as 25%-30% due to the deep cuts to revenues recorded by banks and hedge funds earlier this year as a result of the novel coronavirus, according to a report published Wednesday by compensation consulting firm Johnson Associates Inc.

titulo: Jersey jackpot of $2 million awaits Deutsche's lost shareholders
resumo: While Deutsche Bank  employees fret about their jobs and pay, Germany's largest lender is trying to track down several hundred former staff to claim share payouts it has been holding in Jersey.

titulo: S.Korean shoppers go the distance to beat Chanel price hike
resumo: More than a hundred shoppers waited in line outside a Chanel store in Seoul on Wednesday, shrugging off concerns about a second wave of coronavirus infections to beat imminent price hikes by the French luxury brand.

titulo: Over 300 lawmakers worldwide urge IMF, World Bank to cancel poor countries' debt
resumo: Over 300 lawmakers from around the world on Wednesday urged the International Monetary Fund and World Bank to cancel the debt of the poorest countries in response to the coronavirus pandemic, and to boost funding to avert a global economic meltdown.

titulo: U.S. producer prices post biggest drop since 2009
resumo: U.S. producer prices fell by the most since 2009 in April, leading to the largest annual decline in nearly 4-1/2 years, which could bolster some economists' predictions for a brief period of deflation as the novel coronavirus depresses demand.

titulo: Chanel says increasing prices on handbags, small leather goods
resumo: French luxury house Chanel said on Wednesday it was increasing prices on its handbags and some small leather goods worldwide to take into account a rise in the cost of raw materials amid the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Sony braces for lowest profit in four years, hit broadly by coronavirus
resumo: Sony Corp  expects operating profit to drop at least 30% this financial year to its lowest in four years as the company anticipates a hit to demand for its TVs, cameras and smartphone image sensors from the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Airline SAS to require face masks on all flights
resumo: Swedish-Danish airline SAS  said on Wednesday wearing a face mask would be compulsory on all its flights through to the end of August to reduce the risk of coronavirus contagion.

titulo: Qatar Airways in talks to defer Airbus, Boeing orders: SCMP
resumo: Qatar Airways, one of the Middle East's biggest airlines, is in talks with Airbus  and Boeing  to defer aircraft orders for several years, its chief executive was quoted as saying on Wednesday.

titulo: Mitsubishi slashes SpaceJet budget, drops variant
resumo: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries  (MHI) said it was more than halving the annual development budget for its SpaceJet regional jet as the coronavirus pandemic squeezes profit, and will suspend plans for a possible variant seen as key to winning future orders, particularly from U.S. airlines.

titulo: CMA CGM taps French loan scheme to boost cash in coronavirus crisis
resumo: Shipping group CMA CGM has obtained a 1.05 billion euro ($1.1 billion) loan to be 70% guaranteed by the French government as it seeks to bolster its cash position during the coronavirus pandemic, which has hammered international trade.

titulo: EasyJet, Germanwings lose legal fight against EU's Italian airport ruling
resumo: Easyjet  and Lufthansa's  Germanwings on Wednesday lost their challenge against an EU ruling ordering Italy to recover illegal state aid granted to airlines serving Sardinia.

titulo: Maersk sees drop in global ship container demand this year
resumo: Shipping group A.P. Moller-Maersk  warned of a sharp drop in global container volumes due to the coronavirus pandemic, with weaker retail sales and depressed car production dampening demand.

titulo: TUI to cut jobs and costs as it prepares for July holiday restart
resumo: Travel group TUI  will cut 8,000 jobs and look to shed 30% of its costs as it gears up for a July restart to European tourism, the German company said on Wednesday.

titulo: European holidays to restart in July, tourism to recover by 2022: TUI CEO
resumo: Tourism will recover to its pre-coronavirus levels by 2022 at the latest and European holidays will restart in July said Fritz Joussen, the chief executive of travel group TUI .

titulo: Tesla's California fight heats up competition for jobs
resumo: Tesla Inc Chief Executive Elon Musk's fight with local  authorities over the reopening of its California plant has gotten the attention of those who scout sites for new factories and corporate offices, as well as economic development officials hungry for more jobs.

titulo: Korean Air to issue $817 mln in new shares as virus strains industry
resumo: South Korea's largest airline, Korean Air , plans to sell around 1 trillion won ($816.55 million) in new shares in its biggest rights issue in 20 years to raise funds amid mounting strains in the industry due to the pandemic.

titulo: Forex firm Travelex to default on coupon payment
resumo: Troubled currency services provider Travelex [TFPI.UL] on Wednesday warned of defaulting on a 14.4 million euro ($15.6 million) coupon payment on some of its senior notes as it grapples with a hit to earnings amid the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Emirates plans scheduled flights from May 21 to nine cities
resumo: Emirates Airline from May 21 plans to operate scheduled flight services from Dubai to London Heathrow, Frankfurt, Paris, Milan, Madrid, Chicago, Toronto, Sydney and Melbourne, it said in a statement on Wednesday.

titulo: Resurgent Wall Street disconnected from reality on the ground
resumo: With the U.S. economy facing its potentially deepest economic decline in nearly a century, Wall Street is pulling further and further ahead from Main Street.

titulo: Deutsche Bank targets 200 billion euros of sustainable investment by 2025
resumo: German lender Deutsche Bank  plans to pump at least 200 billion euros ($216.8 billion) into so-called sustainable financing and investments by 2025, its first formal targets for doing so.

titulo: Amazon expects to extend France warehouse closures to May 18
resumo: Amazon  plans to extend until May 18 the closure of its six French warehouses, which have been closed since April 16 after court rulings ordering the U.S. e-commerce giant to restrict deliveries during the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Japan stands by its integrated resort policy despite Sands' move
resumo: Japan has no plan to change its schedule regarding the launch of integrated resorts (IR) - Las Vegas-style complexes that include casinos and shopping arcades - despite Las Vegas Sands'  decision to end its plan to open an IR casino in Japan, a top government spokesman said on Wednesday.

titulo: Aston Martin posts deep quarterly loss as pandemic slams brakes on sales
resumo: Aston Martin  posted a deep first-quarter loss after sales dropped by nearly a third due to the impact of the coronavirus crisis, though the luxury car maker said production of a crucial sport utility vehicle was on track.

titulo: TUI burning 250 million euros cash a month: CEO
resumo: Travel group TUI  , which is struggling with the impact of the coronavirus pandemic, is burning cash at a rate of 250 million euros ($271 million) a month, its CEO said on Wednesday.

titulo: For Hire: Central Europe's business services firms spy a chance in crisis
resumo: As the COVID-19 pandemic wipes out jobs and empties offices across the world, companies in Central Europe that provide remote, lower-cost services for multinationals are emerging as winners in the new business order.

titulo: Las Vegas Sands scraps plans for Japan integrated resort casino project
resumo: Las Vegas Sands  said it has ended plans to open an integrated resort (IR) casino in Japan, dealing another blow to Japan's attempts to energise its economy through tourism.

titulo: Nissan plans $2.8 billion in cuts as part of restructuring plan: Bloomberg
resumo: Nissan Motor Co  plans to cut $2.8 billion in annual fixed costs as part of its restructuring plan, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, as it braces for a drop in sales that could complicate its recovery from years of poor profitability.

titulo: Trump wants California to let automaker Tesla reopen assembly plant
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday urged that Tesla Inc  be allowed to reopen its electric vehicle assembly plant in California, joining CEO Elon Musk's bid to defy county officials who have ordered it to remain closed.

titulo: Intel Capital invests in Chinese chip companies amid tech tensions
resumo: Intel Capital, the venture arm of chipmaker Intel Corp , has invested in two Chinese startups in the semiconductor sector, the company announced on Wednesday, as part of its latest batch of deals.

titulo: Musk, Texas governor talk about potential Tesla move to Lone Star state
resumo: Texas Governor Greg Abbott said on Tuesday that he spoke with Elon Musk, the chief executive of Tesla Inc , in recent days about a potential move of the company's electric vehicle assembly plant to the Lone Star state.

titulo: Contact tracing, temperature checks and masks: airline industry outlines new norms
resumo: Airlines and airports are recommending a layered approach to temporary safety measures as air travel restarts, warning that no single measure can mitigate all of the risks during the pandemic, according to a briefing document seen by Reuters.

titulo: Exclusive: Neiman Marcus creditor calls for deal with Saks Fifth Avenue - letter
resumo: Hedge fund Mudrick Capital Management LP asked Neiman Marcus Group's independent directors on Tuesday to explore a combination with rival department store chain Saks Fifth Avenue, challenging the company's plan to reorganize under bankruptcy protection.

titulo: Occidental offering voluntary job buyouts, citing need for spending cuts: document
resumo: Occidental Petroleum Corp  is offering its employees voluntary buyouts over the next two weeks, according to a document seen by Reuters on Tuesday, citing the sharp decline in oil prices and the coronavirus pandemic for "severe dislocations" in its business.

titulo: The U.S. needs more fiscal help to fight coronavirus, Fed officials say
resumo: U.S. businesses and households are going to need more fiscal support to get through what will likely be a longer period of recovery from the coronavirus shutdown than initially expected, Federal Reserve policymakers said on Tuesday.

titulo: S&P 500 tumbles on fears of virus resurgence in economic reopening
resumo: The S&P 500 dropped 2% on Tuesday as investors took profits following a warning from the top U.S. infectious disease expert that premature moves to reopen the nation's economy could lead to novel coronavirus outbreaks and set back economic recovery.

titulo: U.S. federal court tells SEC, Justice Dept to review RBS whistleblower case
resumo: The U.S. Justice Department and Securities and Exchange Commission must review whether an ex-Royal Bank of Scotland employee is owed a whistleblower payout and provide documents relating to his case, a federal U.S. court said on Tuesday.

titulo: U.S. attorneys seek Amazon data on coronavirus-linked worker deaths, infections
resumo: A group of 13 U.S. attorneys general on Tuesday asked Amazon.com Inc  to provide data on coronavirus-related deaths and  infections among its workforce, along with evidence of the company's compliance with paid sick leave laws.

titulo: Fed's Quarles defends decision to let banks maintain dividends
resumo: A senior Federal Reserve official on Tuesday defended before Congress the central bank's decision to allow lenders to continue issuing dividends, even as the regulator seeks permission from lawmakers to further relax capital rules.

titulo: KKR leads $43 million investment round in Slice pizza platform
resumo: U.S. private equity firm KKR & Co Inc  said on Tuesday it led an investor group making a $43 million investment in Slice, an online delivery platform for local pizza shops across the country.

titulo: Stock market rally pauses, bonds edge higher on fears of second virus wave
resumo: World equity markets slid and safe-haven bonds climbed on Tuesday as rising concerns about a second wave of coronavirus infections outweighed stronger economic data from China and upbeat corporate earnings in Europe.

titulo: Twitter to let some employees to work from home permanently
resumo: Twitter Inc  on Tuesday became the first major tech company to allow employees who can work remotely to do so indefinitely, as the coronavirus outbreak forces unprecedented changes in work culture across the world.

titulo: U.S. reports record $738 billion budget deficit in April
resumo: The United States on Tuesday reported a record $738 billion budget deficit in April as an explosion in government spending and a shrinking of revenues amid the novel coronavirus pandemic pushed it deeply into the red.

titulo: Morgan Stanley pays $5 million fine to settle SEC charges it misled investing clients
resumo: A unit of Morgan Stanley agreed to pay a $5 million fine to settle U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission charges it misled retail investing clients about the costs of a "wrap fee" program, the regulator said on Tuesday.

titulo: Montana judge upholds ruling that canceled Keystone XL pipeline permit
resumo: A federal judge in Montana on Monday upheld his ruling last month that canceled an environmental permit for the long-delayed Keystone XL oil pipeline and threatened other oil and natural gas pipeline projects with delays.

titulo: Vodafone keeps dividend as pandemic hits roaming, but boosts data
resumo: Vodafone  maintained its dividend on Tuesday, bucking a corporate trend to cut or scrap payouts due to the coronavirus crisis, as the world's second-biggest mobile operator met expectations with a 2.6% rise in full-year core earnings.

titulo: Coronavirus crisis leads to largest U.S. consumer price decline since 2008
resumo: U.S. consumer prices dropped by the most since the Great Recession in April, weighed down by a plunge in demand for gasoline and services including airline travel as Americans stayed home during the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: China working to fulfil pledge on U.S. purchases despite import slowdown: sources
resumo: China is committed to its Phase 1 trade deal with the United States and is working towards meeting its promise to boost U.S. purchases, even though the pace of buying has been restrained by the coronavirus outbreak, three Chinese sources said.

titulo: Luckin Coffee sacks CEO, COO for alleged fraud
resumo: Chinese coffee chain Luckin Coffee Inc  fired its chief executive and chief operating officers following an internal probe on fabrication of annual sales numbers, the company said on Tuesday.

titulo: Truck maker Volvo ditches dividend payment amid COVID-19 crisis
resumo: Swedish truck maker Volvo  will withdraw its proposed dividend for 2019, it said on Tuesday, joining a slew of firms opting not to make payments to shareholders because of the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: BOC Aviation scoops up deals with airlines in sector's biggest crisis
resumo: Armed with strong funding, Asian lessor BOC Aviation has struck an estimated $5.5 billion of deals with carriers including Southwest  and United Airlines  to buy and lease back aircraft as the sector faces its deepest ever crisis.

titulo: Hyatt to lay off 1,300 employees as pandemic cripples travel
resumo: Hyatt Hotels Corp  said late Monday it would lay off 1,300 people globally as it tries to cope with the coronavirus crisis, which has virtually halted global travel by keeping people indoors.

titulo: Pfizer to outsource some drug production, focus on coronavirus vaccine
resumo: (This May 8 story corrects to delete word "intravenously" in para 8 in reference to Pfizer's 1.5 billion doses of injected vaccines)

titulo: Saudi Aramco profit falls 25% but dividend in line with planned payout for year
resumo: Saudi Aramco, the world's top oil exporting company, on Tuesday reported a 25% fall in first-quarter net profit, missing analyst estimates, but its quarterly dividend was in line with a plan for a $75 billion payout for the year.

titulo: Boeing sees zero orders again in April, MAX cancellations mount
resumo: Boeing Co  recorded zero orders for the second time this year in April and customers canceled another 108 orders for its grounded 737 MAX plane compounding its worst start to a year since 1962.

titulo: Logitech sales surge as locked-down families, workers stay connected
resumo: Logitech International  reported a big jump in sales of computer products during its fourth quarter on Tuesday as more people worked from home and families turned to technology to keep in touch during the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Tesla's Musk says ready for arrest as he reopens California plant against local order
resumo: Tesla Inc  Chief Executive Elon Musk on Monday said production was resuming at the automaker's sole U.S. vehicle factory, in California, defying an order to stay closed and saying if anyone had to be arrested it should be him.

titulo: Ryanair expects Europe travel surge despite masks, quarantine
resumo: Temperature checks, masks and quarantine will not deter people from a holiday in the sun after three months "locked up" at home, Ryanair's chief executive Michael O'Leary said on Tuesday as he announced plans for 1,000 flights a day from July.

titulo: Trump again urges Fed to adopt negative U.S. interest rates
resumo: President Donald Trump on Tuesday again pushed the Federal Reserve to adopt negative interest rates, even as several members of the U.S. central bank have said they do not see a need for rates - now near zero - to move into negative territory.

titulo: German watchdog investigates payments company Wirecard
resumo: Germany's financial watchdog BaFin said on Tuesday it was conducting multiple investigations into Wirecard , sending its shares lower and adding to pressure over allegations it has denied.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp in informal talks about warship unit consolidation
resumo: Thyssenkrupp  is in informal talks about consolidation in the shipbuilding sector, its finance chief said on Tuesday, responding to speculation that the group might sell parts of its Marine Systems (TKMS) unit or merge it with rivals German Naval Yards and Luerssen.

titulo: Embraer blames failed Boeing deal for sharp drop in commercial jet deliveries
resumo: Planemaker Embraer  said Tuesday that its commercial jet deliveries slumped by more than half during the first three months of 2020 compared to a year ago, blaming the drop on preparations for its failed deal with Boeing Co .

titulo: EasyJet founder offers $6 million reward for help in cancelling Airbus order
resumo: EasyJet  founder Stelios Haji-Ioannou is offering 5 million pounds ($6.2 million) for any information that leads to the cancellation of its order for 107 Airbus  jets as he steps up his campaign to oust the budget airline's management.

titulo: Brussels Airlines slashes jobs, fleet as COVID-19 bites
resumo: Brussels Airlines, part of Germany's Lufthansa , said on Tuesday it would reduce its fleet by 30% and its workforce by a quarter to guarantee its survival during and after the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: China's Tesla wannabes risk running out of road in virus-stricken times
resumo: Challenged by the arrival of Tesla in China last year, domestic electric vehicle (EV) start-ups were struggling even before the economic shock wrought by the coronavirus, but now for some it has become a battle for survival.

titulo: AIG approaches shareholder pay vote with cautious approval from proxy firms
resumo: American International Group Inc  faces a crucial test on Wednesday, when shareholders are to vote on whether the board of directors has adequately addressed their concerns about executive pay.

titulo: Ryanair cools on Airbus, moves back towards all-Boeing fleet
resumo: Ryanair plans to move back towards an all-Boeing fleet by cancelling leases for Airbus A320s for its Lauda subsidiary and likely replacing 30 Airbus jets at the Austrian airline with Boeing 737s, Chief Executive Michael O'Leary told Reuters.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp faces deep quarterly loss due to coronavirus, shares tank
resumo: German conglomerate Thyssenkrupp  warned on Tuesday that its operating loss could swell to 1 billion euros ($1.1 billion) in the April to June quarter due to the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Toyota sees 80% profit drop as virus wipes $14 billion off car sales
resumo: Toyota Motor Corp  said on Tuesday it expects profit to drop by 80% to its lowest in nine years, as Japan's biggest automaker grapples with the impact of the novel coronavirus which has sapped global demand for vehicles.

titulo: ArcelorMittal offers $2 billion of shares, convertibles at discount
resumo: ArcelorMittal , the world's largest steelmaker, is issuing $2 billion of shares and convertible notes at a deep discount to accelerate debt reduction plans that have been slowed by the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Honda profits shrink to four-year low, coronavirus clouds outlook
resumo: Honda Motor Co  on Tuesday posted its lowest operating profit in four years and refrained from releasing an earnings outlook for the current year because of uncertainty about the longer-term impact of the coronavirus on global car demand.

titulo: China announces new tariff waivers for some U.S. imports
resumo: China announced on Tuesday a new list of 79 U.S. products eligible for waivers from retaliatory tariffs imposed at the height of the bilateral trade war, amid continued pressure on Beijing to boost imports from the United States.

titulo: Popeyes sticks to China expansion plan in spite of virus
resumo: Fried chicken chain Popeyes is forging ahead with the opening of its first outlet in China despite the coronavirus outbreak and plans to set up "a few more" by the end of the year in several  Chinese cities, its executives said on Tuesday.

titulo: Honda says has enough cash but may issue bonds if new coronavirus waves hit
resumo: Honda Motor Co  has enough cash on hand, equivalent to almost two months of sales, to weather the downturn caused by the coronavirus pandemic, but may issue bonds to raise funds if there is second or third wave of infections, a senior managing director at the company, Kohei Takeuchi said on Tuesday.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp CFO: we don't have a liquidity problem
resumo: Thyssenkrupp  does not have a liquidity problem due to a 1 billion euro loan from state-owned lender KfW [KFW.UL] and the expected transfer of funds from the sale of its elevator division, its chief financial officer said.

titulo: Trump 'not interested' in reopening U.S.-China trade deal after report of Beijing discontent
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday he opposed renegotiating the U.S.-China "Phase 1" trade deal after a Chinese state-run newspaper reported some government advisers in Beijing were urging fresh talks and possibly invalidating the agreement.

titulo: Ryanair CEO says UK quarantine plan is idiotic, unenforceable
resumo: Ryanair  CEO Michael O'Leary on Tuesday said Britain's plan to have arriving passengers self-isolate in their accommodation for 14 days upon arrival was "idiotic" and effectively unenforceable.

titulo: Once taboo, investors begin to imagine negative U.S. rates
resumo: Negative interest rates in the United States were once unimaginable. The coronavirus has changed that.

titulo: Tencent Music misses quarterly revenue estimates, signals better second quarter
resumo: China's Tencent Music Entertainment  narrowly missed market estimates for quarterly revenue on Monday as the COVID-19 crisis hurt the company's social entertainment services business, sending its shares down 4.5% in extended trade overnight.

titulo: Tim Hortons China says Tencent has invested, did not disclose amount
resumo: Tim Hortons China said Tuesday it received an investment from Chinese tech giant Tencent, as the Canadian coffee brewer seeks to expand in the Chinese market.

titulo: Potbelly settlement with activists includes twist: stock, not cash, for outlays
resumo: Sandwich maker Potbelly Corp on Monday promised to reimburse activist shareholders' expenses with stock not cash, creating a situation that lawyers call an outlier for now but which they said could find traction in tough economic times.

titulo: WeWork directors seek to preserve right to sue owner SoftBank
resumo: A special committee of WeWork board members filed a motion in Delaware on Monday to prevent it from being disbanded, as it pursues legal action against majority owner SoftBank Group Corp  over an abandoned $3 billion tender offer for the office space-sharing start-up.

titulo: Boeing CEO sees passenger levels under 25% in September, pain for airlines: NBC interview
resumo: Boeing Co  Chief Executive Dave Calhoun does not expect passenger traffic to reach even a quarter of its levels in September, creating the need for airlines to make "adjustments" as they weather the impact of the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Bitcoin goes through third 'halving', falls vs U.S. dollar
resumo: Bitcoin slid on Monday in volatile trading, after it went through a technical adjustment that reduced the rate at which new coins are created, but the outlook remained upbeat as the increase in supply slows down.

titulo: United to notify flyers about full flights after social media backlash
resumo: United Airlines Holdings Inc  said on Monday that starting next week it will notify passengers ahead of time if their flight is expected to be "closer to full capacity," allowing them to re-book on a different flight or receive a travel credit.

titulo: Shut casinos hit Caesars as COVID-19 puts gambling industry in survival mode
resumo: Caesars Entertainment Corp  missed quarterly revenue estimates on Monday as it shuttered its casinos amid nationwide lockdowns to contain the spread of the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: S&P 500 closes barely higher as investors balance pandemic with recovery
resumo: The S&P 500 closed barely higher, eking out a nominal gain on Monday as investors weighed new spikes in coronavirus infections with expectations that an economy crippled by mandated shutdowns will soon be re-opened for business.

titulo: Oklahoma regulators take no action after oil output hearing
resumo: Oklahoma's energy regulators on Monday took no action on applications from oil and gas producers seeking to win state-support for measures they said would help stabilize oil prices.

titulo: Global equities markets waver, bonds dip on fears of second wave of infections
resumo: Global equities wavered on Monday as investors weighed a jump in coronavirus cases in South Korea and Germany against signs that more parts of the U.S. economy could soon emerge from economic lockdown. Safe havens such as U.S. Treasuries edged higher in anticipation of further stimulus measures to limit the damage of the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Tesla sues California county in virus factory closure fight, threatens to leave
resumo: Tesla Inc sued local authorities in California on Saturday as the electric carmaker pushed to re-open its factory there and Chief Executive Elon Musk threatened to move Tesla's headquarters and future programs from the state to Texas or Nevada.

titulo: California governor to look into Tesla reopening in defiance of health order
resumo: California Governor Gavin Newsom said both he and local health officials would speak to officials at Tesla Inc, after the company ordered workers back on the job despite ongoing coronavirus restrictions in Alameda County, where its auto plant is located.

titulo: Richard Branson to sell 25 million shares in Virgin Galactic to battle pandemic impact
resumo: Billionaire Richard Branson's Virgin Group said on Monday that it may sell up to 25 million shares of space tourism company Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc to raise funds amid the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Marriott profit misses as bookings, revenue per room plunge
resumo: U.S. hotel operator Marriott  missed already lowered estimates for first-quarter profit by a huge margin on Monday, as bookings plunged due to the coronavirus-led lockdowns that kept a quarter of its hotels closed.

titulo: KKR builds 5.2% stake in ProSieben, says company undervalued
resumo: U.S. private equity house KKR  said on Monday it had acquired a stake of 5.2% in ProSiebenSat.1 Media , becoming the third investor to amass a sizable shareholding in the struggling German broadcaster.
