titulo: H&M's sales tumble, stockpiles grow in March to May
resumo: H&M , the world's second-biggest fashion retailer, said  local currency sales had tumbled 57% since the start of March compared with a year ago as the COVID-19 pandemic and lockdowns kept most of its stores closed.

titulo: Wall Street bullish on ride-hailing firms on lower driver costs, recovery signs
resumo: Wall Street gave a resounding vote of confidence to ride-hailing stocks ahead of Uber's  results on Thursday, after Lyft indicated a slow recovery in demand and lower costs as rising unemployment creates greater supply of drivers.

titulo: Lufthansa seeks 9 billion euro bailout amid political discussions
resumo: Lufthansa  is negotiating a 9 billion euro ($9.71 billion) bailout with Germany's economic stabilisation fund to ensure its future, the airline said on Thursday, confirming an earlier Reuters report.

titulo: Millions more Americans file for jobless benefits; productivity tanks
resumo: Millions more Americans sought unemployment benefits last week, suggesting layoffs broadened from consumer-facing industries to other segments of the economy and could remain elevated even as many parts of the country start to reopen.

titulo: U.S. employers must try to accommodate workers vulnerable to coronavirus: agency
resumo: The U.S. agency that enforces laws prohibiting disability discrimination said on Thursday that employers must try to accommodate workers with medical conditions that make them vulnerable to the novel coronavirus before sending them home.

titulo: AB InBev sees worse ahead, but some light in China
resumo: Anheuser-Busch InBev , the world's largest beer maker, forecast a "materially worse" second quarter as coronavirus restrictions curb drinking across the globe, although China was showing early signs of recovery.

titulo: Liberty and Telefonica agree $38 billion UK tie-up to take on BT
resumo: Liberty Global  and Telefonica  have agreed to merge their British businesses in a $38 billion deal that will create a powerhouse in mobile and broadband to take on market leader BT .

titulo: Air France job cuts to test CEO Smith's consensual style
resumo: Air France-KLM  is opening talks with its French unions on workforce cuts, Chief Executive Ben Smith told Reuters, as the airline group warned of mounting losses with no clear end in sight to the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Air France-KLM boss right to tackle subject of jobs: minister
resumo: Air France-KLM  Chief Executive Ben Smith is right to tackle the subject of jobs at the struggling airline, said French government minister Jean-Baptiste Djebbari on Thursday, as trade unions brace for possible cuts.

titulo: JetBlue posts quarterly loss, scales back Airbus orders
resumo: JetBlue Airways Corp  on Thursday posted a $268 million quarterly loss as the coronavirus pandemic hurt air travel demand, and cut its order book with Airbus SE  by about 34% through 2022 to save $1.1 billion in aircraft spending.

titulo: Facebook names first members of oversight board that can overrule Zuckerberg
resumo: Facebook Inc's  new content oversight board will include a former prime minister, a Nobel Peace Prize laureate and several constitutional law experts and rights advocates among its first 20 members, the company announced on Wednesday.

titulo: Harley appoints turnaround specialist Jochen Zeitz to revive sales
resumo: Harley-Davidson Inc  on Thursday appointed board member and turnaround specialist Jochen Zeitz to the role of chief executive officer, as the struggling motorcycle maker looks to tap into his expertise to woo customers and revive sales.

titulo: Next enters British beauty contest in former Debenhams stores
resumo: British clothes retailer Next  will open five standalone beauty halls in former Debenhams stores across the country as a first step in its ambition to become a new force in beauty retailing, the company said on Thursday.

titulo: Twilio shares surge 32% after results crush Wall Street estimates
resumo: Twilio Inc  shares surged 32% to a record high after the cloud communication provider's quarterly results and forecast smashed Wall Street estimates on increased demand from telehealth and education companies amid widespread lockdowns.

titulo: Kohl's to reopen 25% of stores as states lift lockdown restrictions
resumo: Kohl's Corp  said on Thursday it plans to reopen a quarter of its stores by next week, as retailers start to get business going again in states where coronavirus-induced lockdown restrictions have eased.

titulo: U.S. demand for cars expected to rebound while travel, dining lag: ad agency
resumo: Consumer demand for cars could begin to recover this month in the United States after steep declines due to the coronavirus pandemic, but the hard-hit travel and dining industries will likely continue to face difficult times, according to a major U.S. advertising agency.

titulo: Fashion shift online picks up even as stores reopen
resumo: Retailers selling fashion online are emerging as the winners of the coronavirus crisis as consumers are staying home even as stores start to reopen, results from major companies showed on Thursday.

titulo: PayPal, Square eye boost as crisis spurs online shopping shift
resumo: Wall Street analysts looked past a surprisingly shaky first quarter for payment processors PayPal Holdings Inc  and Square Inc  hoping that the shift towards more online buying will see their businesses surge this year.

titulo: Rolls-Royce signals job cuts as lowers engine output forecast
resumo: British aero-engine maker Rolls-Royce signalled on Thursday it expects to cut some of its workforce, after reports that it was considering cutting up to 8,000 jobs to weather a global aerospace sector slump.

titulo: BT scraps dividend, invests in fibre as rivals agree merger
resumo: Britain's biggest telecoms group BT  suspended its dividend and said it would spend billions more on faster fibre broadband connections, as it prepares to meet the challenge posed by the merger of two of its biggest rivals.

titulo: GSK sells $3.4 billion Hindustan Unilever stake in largest India block trade
resumo: GlaxoSmithKline  has sold its stake in Unilever's  Indian business  for $3.4 billion, marking India's largest block trade, which will help the British company in its goal of reinvigorating its drug development pipeline.

titulo: Apple awards $10 million to nasal swab maker COPAN Diagnostics to help boost factory output
resumo: Apple Inc said on Thursday that it was awarding $10 million to a maker of nasal swabs and other materials for collecting samples for Covid-19 tests in a move aimed at boosting the swab maker's production to 1 million collection kits per week by early July.

titulo: U.S. and Europe spar over next stage of aircraft subsidy dispute
resumo: The United States said there was "no valid basis" for the European Union to retaliate against U.S. goods in a long-running subsidy row after Boeing lost state tax breaks, but the EU said it was pressing ahead with its demand for tariffs.

titulo: British Airways owner IAG must restructure across the board: Walsh
resumo: The owner of British Airways, Iberia, Vueling and Aer Lingus must cut costs across the board to weather the coronavirus crisis as air travel won't return to normal until at least 2023, its chief executive Willie Walsh said on Thursday.

titulo: PayPal sees strong second quarter as online spending surges (May 6)
resumo: PayPal Holdings Inc  said it expects a strong recovery in payments volumes in the second quarter as social distancing drives more people to shop online, even as lockdowns start to ease, sending its shares up 8% in extended trading.

titulo: Global luxury gloom to deepen despite easing lockdowns: Bain
resumo: Global sales of luxury goods are expected to slump by 50% to 60% in the second quarter even as some countries begin to ease coronavirus lockdowns and despite signs of recovery in the Chinese market, consultancy Bain said on Thursday.

titulo: IAG CEO says would like group to continue to have presence at Gatwick
resumo: The chief executive of British Airways-owner IAG  Willie Walsh said on Thursday that his personal was that the airline group should continue to have a presence at London's Gatwick Airport.

titulo: Qatar Airways in talks with banks for billions of dollars in loans: sources
resumo: Qatar Airways is in talks with banks for loans worth billions of dollars, three sources familiar with the matter said, as the state-owned airline readies to begin rebuilding its network that has been crushed by the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Italy to inject 3 billion euros in new Alitalia: industry minister
resumo: Italy's government will inject fresh capital worth at least 3 billion euros ($3.24 billion) in the ailing carrier Alitalia, which is being nationalised following financial problems, Industry Minister Stefano Patuanelli said on Thursday.

titulo: German economy minister: We'll prevent Lufthansa from being sold out
resumo: The German government will prevent Lufthansa , the country's flagship carrier, from being sold out, Economy Minister Peter Altmaier told Bild newspaper on Thursday.

titulo: World food prices fall sharply in April because of coronavirus: U.N.
resumo: World food prices fell for a third consecutive month in April, hit by the economic and logistical impact of the coronavirus pandemic, the United Nations food agency said on Thursday.

titulo: Media giant Vivendi raises stake in Lagardere to 13.36%
resumo: French media conglomerate Vivendi  has increased its stake in Lagardere  to 13.36% from 10.6%, an AMF regulatory filing published on Thursday showed.

titulo: Norwegian Air secures shareholder funds in fight to stay aloft
resumo: Norwegian Air  has secured vital funding from shareholders as it battles to survive the novel coronavirus pandemic that drove down April passenger volumes by 98.7%, the budget carrier said on Thursday.

titulo: Climate activists spray 'fake oil' onto Barclays HQ in London's Canary Wharf
resumo: Climate activists from the Extinction Rebellion group sprayed "fake oil" onto the Barclays headquarters in London's Canary Wharf on Thursday, calling on the bank to divest from fossil fuels.

titulo: Britain's Superdry sales plunge 37% as stores shuttered
resumo: British fashion retailer Superdry  said its revenue plunged 37% in its latest quarter, reflecting the closure of its stores across the world because of the novel coronavirus.

titulo: UK parliament tells Barclays CEO: explain emergency business loans delays
resumo: The British parliament's treasury committee chairman has told the chief executive of Barclays  to explain delays to granting emergency loans to small businesses.

titulo: Britain's GSK to sell entire stake in Hindustan Unilever
resumo: (Reporting by Pushkala Aripaka in Bengaluru; Editing by Anil D'Silva)

titulo: Bottler Coca Cola HBC's April sales lose fizz as lockdowns weigh
resumo: Soft drinks bottler Coca Cola HBC AG  on Thursday said sales in April fell by more than a third as fast-food chains, theatres and other public arenas stayed shut to limit the spread of the new coronavirus during stay-at-home orders.

titulo: Liberty Global expects no major regulatory challenge to O2-Virgin deal: CEO
resumo: Liberty Global  expects no major regulatory challenge to a deal to merge its Virgin Media unit with British mobile operator O2, owned by Spain's Telefonica , its chief executive said on Thursday.

titulo: Air France-KLM loss gives first taste of coronavirus impact
resumo: Two weeks of coronavirus shutdown were enough to hit Air France-KLM  with an 815 million-euro ($880 million) first-quarter operating loss, the airline group said on Thursday - predicting demand could take "several years" to recover.

titulo: Liberty Global, Telefónica agree 24 billion pound deal to merge UK groups: FT
resumo: The British units of Liberty Global Plc  and Telefónica SA  will merge in a deal worth 24 billion pounds ($29.57 billion), the Financial Times reported https://www.ft.com/content/2b73a385-7218-47dc-83bf-18e6e3d5f8f4 on Thursday.

titulo: Smithfield Foods to restart South Dakota pork plant from May 7
resumo: Smithfield Foods Inc will restart its plant in Sioux Falls, South Dakota from May 7 after being idled for more than three weeks due to the coronavirus outbreak, the world's biggest pork processor said late on Wednesday.

titulo: Frontier Airlines rescinds empty middle seat charge after lawmaker pressure
resumo: U.S. budget carrier Frontier Airlines withdrew on Wednesday a "More Room" policy that would have given passengers the option of paying extra to keep the middle seat empty on flights after a backlash from politicians, according to a letter to lawmakers seen by Reuters.

titulo: Sinclair agrees to pay record-setting $48 million FCC civil penalty
resumo: Sinclair Broadcast Group has agreed to pay a $48 million fine to the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) resolving the probe into the company's abandoned deal to buy Tribune Media in what the agency said was its largest-ever civil penalty.

titulo: Ford's China ventures saw sales growth in April as market rebounds
resumo: U.S. automaker Ford Motor Co's  two ventures in China have reported year-on-year sales growth for April, indicating the world's biggest auto market has started its recovery from coronavirus-induced lows.

titulo: T-Mobile beats phone subscriber estimates as lockdown creates demand surge
resumo: T-Mobile US Inc  said on Wednesday it added more monthly bill paying phone subscribers than expected in the first quarter, thanks to a surge in demand for cheaper phone services as people work from home amid coronavirus lockdowns.

titulo: Lyft sees rider demand improve in late April, 'on path to profitability'
resumo: Lyft Inc  on Wednesday posted higher-than-expected revenue and the ride-hailing company vowed further cost cuts to become profitable, saying ridership hit by the coronavirus pandemic had improved in late April.

titulo: Peloton gets lockdown boost as home workouts drive exercise bike sales
resumo: Peloton Interactive Inc  raised its forecast for full-year revenue on Wednesday as "stay at home" orders led to a surge in sales of the company's exercise bikes and fitness subscriptions, sending its shares up 5%.

titulo: S&P 500, Dow drop as financial sector declines counter tech gains
resumo: The S&P 500 and the Dow fell on Wednesday as declines in financials and defensive groups countered gains in tech shares and as data showed U.S. private employers laid off 20 million workers in April, underscoring the economic fallout of the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Fox Corp quarterly revenue tops estimates as Super Bowl boosts viewership
resumo: Fox Corp  beat Wall Street estimates for third-quarter revenue and profit on Wednesday as Super Bowl broadcast boosted television viewership, sending the company's shares up more than 2% in extended trading.

titulo: World shares mixed amid hopes for business pickup; oil slips
resumo: Oil prices slid and world equity markets seesawed on Wednesday as investor hopes for a pickup in business activity were dashed by downbeat economic data and a rise in U.S. crude stockpiles to three-year highs that highlighted low fuel demand.

titulo: New York Times adds record digital subscribers, posts upbeat first-quarter results
resumo: The New York Times Co  crossed five million digital subscribers, adding a record number and withstanding a heavy drop in ad revenue in the first quarter that was dominated by heavy news coverage around the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Surging small cap stocks offer hope of U.S. growth rebound
resumo: Big gains in small caps stocks are fueling hopes that a bounce in broader equity markets may stick, as investors ponder how quickly the U.S. economy can emerge from its coronavirus-fueled slowdown.

titulo: The magic word for Detroit automakers is trucks
resumo: The coronavirus pandemic brought the U.S. auto industry to its knees, but Americans' love affair with beefy pickup trucks is helping the Detroit automakers get on the road to recovery.

titulo: Uber to cut 3,700 jobs, CEO Khosrowshahi to waive base salary
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc will cut about 3,700 full-time jobs and Chief Executive Officer Dara Khosrowshahi will forgo his base salary for the remainder of the year, the company said on Wednesday, as the COVID-19 pandemic decimates ride-hailing businesses.

titulo: Breakout time behind plexiglass: Renault factory restarts in France
resumo: With temperature scanners on the door and tables partitioned by plexiglass shields in the breakout area, workers at one of Renault's  French plants are adjusting to new routines as carmakers try to cope with the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Fed lending program not for insolvent oil drillers, Kaplan says
resumo: Insolvent or overly leveraged firms, including oil drillers and oil services firms, won't be able to tap the U.S. central bank's "Main Street" emergency lending program, Dallas Federal Reserve President Robert Kaplan said on Wednesday.

titulo: Breakout time behind plexiglass: Renault factory restarts in France
resumo: With temperature scanners on the door and tables partitioned by plexiglass shields in the breakout area, workers at one of Renault's  French plants are adjusting to new routines as carmakers try to cope with the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Fed lending program not for insolvent oil drillers, Kaplan says
resumo: Insolvent or overly leveraged firms, including oil drillers and oil services firms, won't be able to tap the U.S. central bank's "Main Street" emergency lending program, Dallas Federal Reserve President Robert Kaplan said on Wednesday.

titulo: Gold price boosts Barrick profit as miner eyes copper deals
resumo: Barrick Gold Corp   reported a nearly 55% rise in quarterly profit on Wednesday as gold prices surged, bolstering its ability to snap up mines including in copper, its chief executive said.

titulo: Factbox: What to know about Facebook's content oversight board
resumo: Facebook Inc  on Wednesday announced the first 20 members of its independent oversight board that can overrule the company’s own content moderation decisions. Here are some key facts about how the board will work:

titulo: WTO must decide if U.S. complied with Boeing aid ruling, Airbus says
resumo: The World Trade Organization must decide whether the United States has fully complied with WTO rulings on illegal government aid to Boeing Co , and that process could take some time, a spokesman for Airbus said on Wednesday.

titulo: Abercrombie, Gap prepare to reopen stores as lockdowns ease
resumo: Abercrombie & Fitch Co  and cash-strapped Gap Inc  on Wednesday laid out plans to reopen stores in areas where lockdown curbs have eased, while also introducing social-distancing measures.

titulo: GM sets May 18 North American restart as profit tops estimates
resumo: General Motors Co  on Wednesday outlined plans for a May 18 restart of most of its North American plants shut down by the coronavirus pandemic as it reported a stronger than expected quarterly profit, sending its shares up 5.3%.

titulo: KKR's first-quarter profit rises 11% on stronger asset sales
resumo: Private equity firm KKR & Co Inc  reported an 11% jump in after-tax distributable earnings in the first quarter on Wednesday, driven by growth in asset sales and management fees ahead of the coronavirus-linked market turmoil.

titulo: VW sees multi-speed coronavirus rebound led by China
resumo: Volkswagen  sees demand rebounding in China, helped by new buyers switching from public transport and sales of premium vehicles, but warned business would not recover from the coronavirus crisis as quickly in other parts of the world.

titulo: BMW cuts outlook, sees coronavirus pain lasting all year
resumo: BMW AG  expects the coronavirus pandemic to hit demand and earnings throughout this year, prompting the German automaker to cut its profitability forecast for passenger cars following a drop in first-quarter deliveries.

titulo: StanChart highlights climate goals amid criticism over carbon financing
resumo: Standard Chartered  defended its environmental credentials after renewed criticism from climate campaigners for its funding of fossil fuel companies as the bank held its annual shareholder meeting on Wednesday.

titulo: CVS Health maintains 2020 profit forecast despite coronavirus hit
resumo: CVS Health Corp  on Wednesday said it was sticking to its 2020 forecast and expected a strong second quarter, as reduced medical service use for its Aetna insurance business offset  coronavirus-related expenses and the pandemic's impact on pharmacy sales and prescriptions.

titulo: As central banks break the junk debt barrier, investors will follow
resumo: May 6 (Reuters)- Recent central bank bond-buying to calm market turmoil has breached the wall dividing top-grade debt from so-called junk-rated issues, raising the likelihood of the investment industry and even regulators eventually dismantling the barrier.

titulo: United Airlines to raise $2.25 billion in bonds, rolls back plan to cut work hours
resumo: United Airlines Holdings Inc  said on Wednesday it plans to raise $2.25 billion through a bond offering amid a coronavirus-linked slump in air travel demand, and backtracked on a controversial plan to cut working hours for thousands of employees.

titulo: Fed's Bullard: Drop in U.S. payrolls 'not a surprise' given shutdowns - CNBC
resumo: The massive drop in U.S. private payrolls for April, reported on Wednesday, is "not a surprise" but employment could recover dramatically in the second half of the year if the coronavirus pandemic is controlled, St. Louis Federal Reserve president James Bullard said.

titulo: UK watchdog clears Shell's 'drive carbon neutral' ad on petrol pumps
resumo: Britain's advertising watchdog has ruled Royal Dutch Shell's  ad campaign on petrol pumps promising customers they can "drive carbon neutral" is acceptable, following complaints.

titulo: China's Alibaba launches 'outlet' platform to shift luxury overstock
resumo: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba Group Holding Ltd  has launched a new luxury platform targeting younger consumers that also aims to help high-end brands shed excess inventory built up during the global coronavirus lockdown.

titulo: Qatar Airways expects to re-open routes this month
resumo: Qatar Airways said on Wednesday it would this month start to rebuild its network, in anticipation countries will ease air travel restrictions and based on monitoring of passenger trends.

titulo: Tyson Foods to resume limited production at largest U.S. pork plant
resumo: Tyson Foods Inc  will resume limited production at its largest U.S. pork plant this week, the company said late on Tuesday, a week after President Donald Trump ordered companies to keep meat-processing plants open to protect the supply chain.

titulo: Yum China keeps bets on dine-in, sticks to expansion plans after virus
resumo: Yum China  is betting that Chinese consumers will still choose to dine-in in the aftermath of the coronavirus crisis and plans to open more Pizza Hut and KFC outlets in China's smaller cities in the coming months, its chief executive said.

titulo: E-cigarette maker Juul Labs to exit South Korea after year of health controversies
resumo: U.S. e-cigarette maker Juul Labs said on Wednesday it would end operations in South Korea, a year after it entered the market there but failed to gain traction amid government health warnings.

titulo: Britain's mid-sized banks coping with coronavirus, but warn of pain ahead
resumo: A slew of Britain's mid-sized banks on Wednesday reported steady deposits and demand in the face of the COVID-19 pandemic, but warned it was too early to assess the long-term damage of the outbreak to their businesses.

titulo: Turkish Airlines plans to resume limited flights in June: draft
resumo: Turkish Airlines plans a gradual resumption of flights from June and will take four months to return to near full operation, a draft plan seen by Reuters showed, as the economy reopens following a slowdown in coronavirus cases.

titulo: Hapag-Lloyd seeks savings to offset coronavirus losses: CEO
resumo: German container line Hapag-Lloyd  is to implement cost cuts to take account of the coronavirus crisis, its chief executive Rolf Habben Jansen said on Wednesday.

titulo: Disney takes $1.4 billion coronavirus hit, sets date to reopen Shanghai park
resumo: Walt Disney Co  estimated on Tuesday that global measures to contain the coronavirus pandemic cut profits by $1.4 billion, mostly from its shuttered theme parks, but said it would reopen Shanghai Disneyland to a reduced number of visitors next week.

titulo: Disney tests reopening strategy at Shanghai Disneyland
resumo: Walt Disney Co.  will kick off its strategy next week to begin restoring its lucrative parks business that has suffered $1 billion in lost profits from the coronavirus-led shutdown.

titulo: Allianz aims to maintain dividend amid coronavirus outbreak, CEO says
resumo: The chief executive of German insurance giant Allianz  said on Wednesday that 2020 will not be another record earnings year but that the company aims to maintain its dividend as it deals with the wrath of the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Samsung heir Lee apologises over succession, won't hand control to children
resumo: Samsung Group heir Jay Y. Lee, embroiled in a bribery scandal, on Wednesday made a rare apology over controversial succession plans and said he will not hand over management rights to his children at the family-controlled conglomerate.

titulo: BMW to delay Hungary plant in coronavirus savings drive
resumo: BMW  will put investments on hold as it seeks to conserve cash to counter a fall in demand caused by the spread of the coronavirus pandemic, Chief Financial Officer Nicolas Peter said on Wednesday.

titulo: Hyundai Motor sales down 57% amid coronavirus pandemic
resumo: South Korea's Hyundai Motor  said on Wednesday its provisional April sales fell 57% on year to 159,079 vehicles globally, as the coronavirus pandemic and efforts to curb its spread sharply contracted auto demand and dealership traffic.

titulo: Amber Capital: no plans to sell Lagardere stake and it may even raise it
resumo: The founder of activist investment company Amber Capital, which lost out this week in a bid to shake up the governance at French group Lagardere , said on Wednesday he had no plans to exit his stake in Lagardere and added that he could even raise it.

titulo: Metro Bank posts rising deposits, lower first quarter lending
resumo: Britain's Metro Bank  reported a modest dip in lending in the first quarter and a 77 million pounds rise in total deposits to 14.6 billion pounds ($18.15 billion) as customers shrugged off lower fixed term deposit rates.

titulo: Exclusive: U.S. airlines burn through $10 billion a month as traffic plummets
resumo: U.S. airlines are collectively burning more than $10 billion in cash a month and averaging fewer than two dozen passengers per domestic flight because of the coronavirus pandemic, industry trade group Airlines for America said in prepared testimony seen by Reuters ahead of a U.S. Senate hearing on Wednesday.

titulo: Impossible Foods to sell plant-based burgers in Kroger's 1,700 stores
resumo: Plant-based meat maker Impossible Foods will start selling its flagship burgers in supermarket chain Kroger Co's  1,700 stores in the United States, as it taps growing demand for vegan alternatives among home chefs.

titulo: Exclusive: Lord & Taylor to liquidate its stores as soon as they reopen - sources
resumo: Venerable U.S. retailer Lord & Taylor plans to liquidate inventory in its 38 department stores once restrictions to curb the spread of coronavirus are lifted as it braces for a bankruptcy process from which it does not expect to emerge, people familiar with the matter said on Tuesday.

titulo: California sues Uber, Lyft over misclassifying drivers as contractors
resumo: California and three of its largest cities on Tuesday sued Uber Technologies Inc and Lyft Inc, accusing them of classifying their drivers improperly as independent contractors instead of employees, evading workplace protections and withholding worker benefits.

titulo: 3M wins injunction against mask seller accused of price gouging
resumo: 3M Co won an injunction from a federal judge against a New Jersey company it accused of illegally using its trademarks to sell 3M-branded N95 respirator masks at inflated prices to exploit the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: United Airlines to cut 30% of management in October, preparing pilot changes too: company memos
resumo: United Airlines Holdings Inc plans to cut at least 3,400 management and administrative positions in October as the coronavirus pandemic crushes air travel demand, and has told pilots to brace for changes as well, according to two memos seen by Reuters.

titulo: Wall Street rises as lockdowns ease, healthcare shares jump
resumo: Wall Street's main indexes gained on Tuesday as healthcare stocks rallied, oil prices surged and a number of countries and U.S. states eased coronavirus-induced restrictions in an attempt to revive their economies.

titulo: Beyond Meat plans U.S. discounts, hopes to replace beef on summer grills
resumo: Plant-based burger maker Beyond Meat plans to offer large value packs and discounts to some U.S. retailers this summer, hoping to grab a greater share of the market as prices for beef rise amid production disruptions caused by the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: EA sees full-year revenue above estimates as home-sheltering boosts gaming
resumo: Video game publisher Electronic Arts Inc forecast full-year adjusted revenue above Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, riding on the broader surge in videogame sales as people shelter at home due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Mattel looks to Christmas for recovery as sales warning hits shares
resumo: Mattel Inc  will have a new line of "Baby Yoda" toys ready to hit shelves in time for Christmas, the company said on Tuesday, even as it predicted a steep drop in second-quarter sales due to the coronavirus lockdowns.

titulo: Spirit AeroSystems says Boeing wants 125 737 MAX shipsets for 2020, down from 216
resumo: Spirit AeroSystems Holdings  said on Tuesday it has reached an agreement with Boeing Co  to deliver 125 737 MAX shipsets to the planemaker in 2020, down from the 216 it had previously announced.

titulo: Occidental posts $2.2 billion loss on charges, deepens spending cuts
resumo: Occidental Petroleum Corp on Tuesday swung to a first quarter loss on writedowns and charges, and the troubled U.S. oil producer cut its budget for the third time since March in response to a historic oil-price crash.

titulo: Airbnb cuts 1,900 jobs as coronavirus hits home rentals
resumo: Airbnb Inc is laying off 25% of its workforce, or nearly 1,900 employees, the home rental startup said on Tuesday, as the COVID-19 pandemic brings global travel to a near standstill.

titulo: Fed policymakers see slow, uneven U.S. recovery after coronavirus downturn
resumo: The U.S. economy could start to recover in the second half of the year after what is shaping up to be the worst recession in decades, but growth is likely to be slow and uneven, several top Federal Reserve policymakers indicated on Tuesday.

titulo: Beyond Meat results beat, suspends 2020 forecast on COVID-19 concerns
resumo: Beyond Meat Inc reported better-than-expected quarterly results on Tuesday as it sold more of its plant-based meat products and cut costs on packaging and labor, sending its shares up 3% after the bell.

titulo: Match quarterly revenue hit by slowing Tinder growth
resumo: Match Group Inc  reported quarterly revenue that fell short of Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, hit by slowing growth in its popular dating app "Tinder" as fewer people signed up and paid for its premium features amid the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Oil surge lifts global stocks out of three-day losing streak
resumo: A gauge of global stock markets rose on Tuesday, snapping a three-day losing streak, while oil prices soared as investors shrugged off grim economic data on hopes the easing of coronavirus lockdowns will jump start economies and revive fuel demand.

titulo: BMW cuts margin outlook for cars division, citing coronavirus woes
resumo: BMW  on Tuesday lowered the outlook for the profitability of its automotive and motorcycles divisions, citing worse-than-expected demand which has been hit by  measures to contain the coronavirus.

titulo: Lyft offers new cheaper rides with more waiting time
resumo: Lyft Inc  said on Tuesday it would expand its program that provides cheaper rides to customers willing to wait longer for their pick-ups, at a time when curbs against the coronavirus outbreak have brought its affordable shared services to a halt.

titulo: Delta caps aircraft seating capacity to ensure safety amid COVID-19
resumo: Delta Air Lines  said on Tuesday it was putting a limit to seating capacity in its airplanes, as the U.S. carrier looks to provide a safe flying experience to customers amid the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Book a table for fast food? Burger King trials post-lockdown app in Italy
resumo: Burger King is trialling an app for ordering food and booking a table at its outlets in Italy, as it seeks to persuade locals it is safe to eat out again after more than two months of coronavirus lockdown.

titulo: Walgreens to restore standard operating hours at most U.S. stores
resumo: Drugstore chain Walgreens Boots Alliance Inc  said it would resume standard operating hours at most of its stores across the United States, beginning Tuesday.

titulo: France's Lagardere sees off activist Amber in shareholder showdown
resumo: French publishing and media group Lagardere  on Tuesday fended off Amber Capital's most daring attempt yet to shake up governance at the company, after shareholders rejected the activist investor's call to replace the supervisory board.

titulo: Fed's Evans says 'reasonable' to expect U.S. growth in second half of 2020
resumo: Chicago Federal Reserve President Charles Evans said on Tuesday it's "reasonable" to expect the U.S. economy to return to growth in the second half of the year, but that such an outcome is only slightly more likely than other, more pessimistic views.

titulo: U.S. trade deficit widens, services sector contracts amid coronavirus
resumo: The U.S. trade deficit increased by the most in more than a year in March as a record drop in exports offset a shrinking import bill, suggesting the novel coronavirus outbreak was upending the global flow of goods and services.

titulo: Lufthansa to pay no dividend for 2019 as airline seeks bailout
resumo: Lufthansa  shareholders on Tuesday agreed to not distribute 298 million euros ($323 million) in retained profits as a dividend for 2019, as the airline enters the final stretch of negotiations for a 10 billion euro bailout.

titulo: Wendy's menu runs short as virus hits U.S. beef supplies

titulo: U.S. states test safety of reopening as pandemic pushes jobless claims higher

titulo: U.S. House passes $500 billion coronavirus bill in latest relief package

resumo: U.S. coronavirus deaths topped 49,000 on Thursday as the number of lives lost in April rises by an average of 2,000 a day, according to a Reuters tally.

resumo: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) on Thursday reported 828,441 cases of the novel coronavirus, an increase of 25,858 from its previous count, and said the number of deaths had risen by 1,804 to 46,379.

resumo: L Brands Inc  filed a legal complaint against Sycamore Partners on Thursday, after the buyout firm walked away from a $525 million deal to acquire a majority stake in the company's Victoria's Secret lingerie brand.

titulo: Boeing plans to cut 787 Dreamliner output, jobs: Bloomberg News

titulo: UAW says virus makes early May restart of U.S. auto plants 'too risky'

titulo: Air France-KLM says CEO Ben Smith to give up 2020 bonus

titulo: American quandary: How to secure weapons-grade minerals without China

titulo: Factbox: Rare earths projects under development in U.S.

titulo: U.S. firms may face probes over payroll loans, Treasury, DOJ officials warn

titulo: Nissan, partners to draft deeper alliance in May: Yomiuri

titulo: China on slow road to recovery, but recession risk is high: Reuters poll

titulo: U.S. House poised to pass more coronavirus aid, avoids remote voting wrangle

titulo: Venture firm Benchmark raises new fund without early Uber investor: source

titulo: Exclusive: Two SpiceJet lessors in talks to reclaim planes over missed payments - sources

titulo: U.S. companies paying down maxed out credit lines with bond issues: BoA note

titulo: Boeing 737 program chief to oversee commercial programs

titulo: Chevron barred from drilling, transporting oil in Venezuela: U.S official
