titulo: Facebook bets on India with $5.7 billion Reliance deal
resumo: Facebook  is set to spend $5.7 billion to buy a 9.99% stake in Reliance Industries'  digital arm, as it looks to roll out services for India's grocers and small businesses by capitalizing on WhatsApp's extensive reach in the country.

titulo: Exclusive: Quibi downloads reach 2.7 million, TV feature to start in May
resumo: New streaming service Quibi, which offers entertainment and news in episodes of 10 minutes or less on mobile phones, has been downloaded more than 2.7 million times since its April 6 debut, founder Jeffrey Katzenberg told Reuters on Wednesday.

titulo: U.S. auto sales show signs of life after gloomy coronavirus March: J.D. Power
resumo: Auto retail sales in the United States are beginning to recover from a massive slump in March due to the outbreak of the coronavirus and nationwide stay-at-home orders, according to analysts at research firm J.D. Power on Wednesday.

titulo: Google Stadia gains users after giving free access for two months
resumo: Google's cloud-based game streaming service, Stadia, has witnessed a surge in users after the tech giant gifted two months of free access to its premium version for gamers sheltering at home to curb the spread of the coronavirus.

titulo: U.S. securities regulator warns investors over Chinese company disclosures
resumo: The head of the U.S. securities regulator on Wednesday warned investors against putting money into Chinese companies as they rebalance their portfolios following market turmoil due to ongoing problems with those companies' disclosures.

titulo: Rogers pulls 2020 outlook, misses estimates as coronavirus crisis deepens
resumo: Rogers Communications Inc pulled its 2020 forecast and reported a lower-than-expected profit on Wednesday, as widespread cancellation of sports events hammered ad sales and demand for new routers took a hit due to the lockdowns.

titulo: Securities group asks SEC to intervene for brokers in audit-trail database fight
resumo: U.S. brokers should not be forced to sign an agreement that could make them liable for breaches of a massive new industry trading database that they have no control over, a leading financial industry trade group told regulators on Wednesday.

titulo: 'A disaster': Roche CEO's verdict on some COVID-19 antibody tests
resumo: Some blood tests being marketed to tell people if they have had the new coronavirus are a "disaster", Roche  Chief Executive Severin Schwan said on Wednesday as he prepares to launch the drugmaker's own antibody test next month.

titulo: Brent crude rebounds from more-than 20-year low; U.S. oil up 20% in wild trade
resumo: Brent crude oil rebounded from two days of losses and U.S. futures surged on Wednesday, bolstered by tentative talk of additional supply cuts from OPEC producers and U.S. inventory builds that were less dire than some expected.

titulo: U.S. Treasury market faces structural issues even as liquidity improves
resumo: The recent liquidity crisis in the $17 trillion U.S. Treasury market has exposed structural problems that are unlikely to be fixed any time soon, which could lead to new bouts of volatility and make it more costly for the U.S. government to sell bonds.

titulo: Next round of coronavirus business aid to be as fair as possible: Kudlow
resumo: White House economic adviser Larry Kudlow on Wednesday said the Trump administration would try to fairly distribute the next round of aid for small businesses hurt by the coronavirus outbreak, saying demand for the first round of funding was "overwhelming."

titulo: Cash is king as U.S. quarterly reporting season gains steam
resumo: As the U.S. quarterly earnings season picks up steam this week, investors are getting a glimpse of how companies are weathering the coronavirus outbreak, including the strength of their balance sheets.

titulo: Pricing the benefits of cheap oil in a world of economic lockdowns
resumo: The oil price collapse that took U.S. crude prices sub-zero for the first time in history may turn out to be a silver lining for the world economy, possibly offering a springboard for recovery when coronavirus lockdowns finally end.

titulo: Netflix to raise $1 billion to fund original content
resumo: Netflix Inc  said on Wednesday it plans to raise about $1 billion in debt to beef up original content, a day after the streaming pioneer doubled its own projections for new customers as stuck-at-home users binged on movies and shows.

titulo: Kraft-Heinz extends factory worker bonuses in pandemic
resumo: Kraft Heinz  will extend for two weeks a $100 per week stipend for factory workers producing its packaged foods during the COVID-19 pandemic, the company said on Wednesday.

titulo: Air France-KLM nears bailout with improved guarantees: sources
resumo: Air France-KLM  is moving towards a 10 billion euro  ($10.9 billion) government-backed rescue deal, sources said, after France agreed to higher guarantees on loans designed to tide the airline group through the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Oil exec and Trump ally Hamm seeks U.S. probe of oil price crash
resumo: The founder of Continental Resources Inc , an ally of U.S. President Donald Trump, is pressing the U.S. commodity markets regulator and the exchange to probe whether market manipulation or system failure was behind this week's unprecedented plunge in U.S. crude futures.

titulo: Google says state-backed hackers increasing coronavirus phishing attacks
resumo: Security experts at Alphabet Inc's  Google have identified more than a dozen government-backed hacking groups using the COVID-19 pandemic as cover for phishing and malware attempts.

titulo: Fox's Rupert, Lachlan Murdoch to forgo salary due to hit from coronavirus
resumo: Fox Corp  Chairman Rupert Murdoch and Chief Executive Officer Lachlan Murdoch will forgo their salaries in response to the coronavirus crisis, according to an internal memo sent to employees on Wednesday.

titulo: Whether OPEC+ formally agrees, deeper oil cuts now look inevitable
resumo: Whether or not OPEC+ oil producers formally agree to extra oil output curbs, rapidly filling storage capacity and plummeting demand due to the coronavirus crisis may force them to cut more.

titulo: Ships, trains, caves: Oil traders chase storage space in world awash with fuel
resumo: Oil traders are struggling to find enough ships, railcars, caverns and pipelines to store fuel as more conventional storage facilities fill up amid abundant supply and plummeting demand due to the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Iran says it will fare better than others after oil crash amid battered economy
resumo: Iran will not suffer as much as other countries from the oil price plunge as it is less reliant on crude exports, its president said on Wednesday, with the state budget depending far less than before on oil revenue due to U.S. sanctions.

titulo: Iran's oil minister says more output cuts may be needed
resumo: Oil producer countries may have to cut output more than the record amount already agreed to tackle an "unprecedented" slump in prices, Iranian Oil Minister Bijan Zanganeh was quoted as saying on Wednesday by the ministry's website SHANA.

titulo: Nasdaq cautious as volume spike drives first-quarter profit beat
resumo: Nasdaq Inc  reported higher-than-expected first-quarter profits on Wednesday, benefiting from market volatility fueled by the coronavirus crisis, but the transatlantic exchange operator also warned of uncertainty ahead.

titulo: Euro zone economy set for deepest recession on record: Reuters poll
resumo: The euro zone economy is already in its deepest recession on record, according to a Reuters poll of economists, who have stuck to a bleak outlook and worry the downturn might be even worse as coronavirus lockdowns across the bloc take their toll.

titulo: AT&T pulls 2020 financial forecast as coronavirus clouds business
resumo: AT&T Inc  pulled its financial forecast for the year on Wednesday as the impact of the coronavirus pandemic clouded its view for the foreseeable future.

titulo: Delta warns of choppy coronavirus recovery, working to halve cash burn
resumo: Delta Air Lines Inc  said on Wednesday it does not expect air travel to recover for two or three years and is working to halve its cash burn after posting its first quarterly loss in eight years due to the coronavirus pandemic."We should be prepared for a choppy, sluggish recovery even after the virus is contained," Chief Executive Ed Bastian said.

titulo: Expedia shares fly on report of talks to sell stake for $1 billion
resumo: Shares of Expedia Group Inc  rose 9% on Wednesday after a report that the online travel services company was in advanced talks to sell a stake to private-equity firms Silver Lake Partners and Apollo Global Management Inc  for about $1 billion.

titulo: EU 'misunderstandings' push back deal on coronavirus economic recovery
resumo: It may take European Union countries until the summer or even longer to agree on how exactly to finance aid to help economies recover from the coronavirus pandemic as major disagreements persist, a bloc official said on Wednesday.

titulo: Housing regulator offers further support for mortgage-finance companies
resumo: The U.S. housing regulator on Wednesday announced its second step in as many days to help ease the liquidity strain in the mortgage market.

titulo: Daimler resumes engine production at Mercedes in Germany
resumo: Mercedes-Benz is ramping up engine production at its plant in Bad Cannstatt, Stuttgart, this week after Germany eased lockdown measures in a sign Europe's largest economy is getting back to work after the coronavirus brought the country to a standstill.

titulo: American Eagle pulls annual forecast on coronavirus worries; shares plunge
resumo: Shares of American Eagle Outfitters Inc  dropped 14% on Wednesday after the apparel retailer withdrew its full-year forecast and said current-quarter results would be significantly hit by coronavirus-related store closures.

titulo: IKEA opens half of stores in Israel after lockdown eased
resumo: IKEA, the world’s biggest furniture brand, opened three of its six stores in Israel on Wednesday after the government started to gradually ease its coronavirus lockdown.

titulo: Explainer: Why Norwegian Air matters to Norway
resumo: Norwegian Air , the budget carrier that changed the way people travel across the Atlantic, is fighting for survival as the coronavirus pandemic deepens the company's financial straits, forcing it to furlough staff and cancel flights.

titulo: Baker Hughes posts $10 billion loss as oil sector demand tanks
resumo: Oilfield firm Baker Hughes Co on Wednesday reported a $10 billion first-quarter loss and revenue fell more than expected as an 80% plunge in oil prices crushed demand for services and equipment.

titulo: Georgia tests boundaries of life post-pandemic with 'risky' reopening
resumo: A handful of mostly southern U.S. states will begin loosening economic restrictions this week in the midst of a still virulent pandemic, providing a live-fire test of whether America's communities can start to reopen without triggering a surge that may force them to close again.

titulo: Virus outbreak raises spectre of cash injection for indebted EDF
resumo: Plummeting electricity demand and falling power prices during the coronavirus outbreak could leave EDF  in need of a capital injection by the end of 2020 to avoid a spiralling debt crisis, a source close to the French firm and analysts said.

titulo: Tyre industry pushes back against evidence of plastic pollution
resumo: A growing body of scientific research linking tyre wear to microplastic pollution, as well as increasing scrutiny from lawmakers in the European Union (EU), has led the $180 billion-a-year tyre industry to fight back.

titulo: Nissan to resume production at Spanish plant, pilot safety steps in UK
resumo: Nissan will restart production at its Barcelona factory from May 4 and it will begin piloting safety measures at its British plant with the return of around 50 staff this week, the Japanese carmaker said on Wednesday.

titulo: Migrant wages sent home set to drop $142 billion in 2020: World Bank
resumo: Global remittances are set to tumble by $142 billion in 2020, the sharpest fall in recent history, the World Bank estimates, as the coronavirus crisis chokes off a cash lifeline for hard-pressed households in poorer countries.

titulo: Explainer: How the EU can finance economic recovery after the COVID-19 pandemic
resumo: The European Union is looking at ways to revive its economy after what is expected to be the 27-nation bloc's deepest recession, caused by the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Tissue sales help Kimberly-Clark beat quarterly results
resumo: Kimberly-Clark Corp  said on Wednesday sales of tissue and toilet paper rose 13% in the first quarter as consumers stocked up in preparation for the coronavirus pandemic, helping the company beat Wall Street estimates for profit and sales.

titulo: Elevator maker Kone beats forecasts, sees China recovery
resumo: Kone  reported a smaller than expected 10% fall in first-quarter profit on Wednesday, with demand for its elevators and services strong in many countries despite the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Glow at home: Beauty industry remakes product pitches in the age of coronavirus
resumo: Out: smoky eyes, red lipstick and fake eyelashes. In: dewy skin, under-eye concealer, moisturized hands.

titulo: Safe as houses? Trading from home tests bank defences during crisis
resumo: Trading worth billions of dollars has shifted to kitchen tables or bedrooms as traders work through the coronavirus crisis from home, testing patchy fixes to make sure they stick to the rules.

titulo: No takers: Hyundai cars sit in U.S. ports as virus keeps buyers away
resumo: As Detroit's automakers shut production in March due to the coronavirus pandemic, South Korea's Hyundai Motor cranked up its factories back home to ship cars to the United States, a move that is proving costly for the world's fifth-largest auto group.

titulo: Brazil's Rio at mercy of Pimco, Dodge & Cox as oil cash dries up
resumo: Already grappling with one of Brazil's most severe outbreaks of the novel coronavirus and a budget deep in the red, Rio de Janeiro state faces a potential threat to its solvency at the hands of investment giants PIMCO and Dodge & Cox.

titulo: BlackRock says sustainability reports might slide during pandemic
resumo: Top asset manager BlackRock Inc, which has vowed to put more focus on climate issues, said that companies might give a lower priority to sustainability reports as they struggle with the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Brexit's back: double-whammy for sterling after virus shock
resumo: Shoved aside by the coronavirus pandemic, Brexit is set to work its way into the headlines again as a June deadline for extending Britain's 11-month transition period turns currency traders' focus - and selling pressure - back to the pound.

titulo: U.S. orders Chevron to 'wind down' Venezuela operations by December 1
resumo: The Trump administration on Tuesday gave Chevron Corp , the last major U.S. oil company still operating in Venezuela, until Dec. 1 to "wind down" its business in the OPEC member-nation and will allow it to conduct only severely limited operations there until then.

titulo: Toyota's South African unit sees 15-20% hit to production due to virus
resumo: The South African unit of Toyota  could see its production drop by around 15-20% in 2020, its president and CEO Andrew Kirby said on Wednesday.

titulo: Airports of Thailand expects passengers to decline by more than 50%
resumo: State-owned Airports of Thailand Pcl  said on Wednesday it expects the number of passengers passing through its airports to decline by 53% this year due to the outbreak of the new coronavirus.

titulo: Fast Retailing CEO criticises Japan's lack of virus tests: report
resumo: The head of Fast Retailing Co , owner of fashion chain Uniqlo, criticised Japan's lack of coronavirus testing in a media interview, joining a chorus of executives questioning Prime Minister Shinzo Abe's handling of the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Exxon Mobil starts building $10 billion China petrochemical complex: Xinhua
resumo: Exxon Mobil Corp  kicked off construction on Wednesday of its $10 billion petrochemical complex in south Chinese city Huizhou, state news agency Xinhua reported.

titulo: EU antitrust regulators resume probe into Boeing, Embraer deal
resumo: EU antitrust regulators have resumed their investigation into Boeing's  bid to buy the jetmaking arm of Embraer , with a decision due by Aug. 7, a European Commission filing showed on Wednesday.

titulo: Equinor head of exploration Dodson to step down
resumo: Equinor's  head of exploration, Tim Dodson, will step down at the end of May in a planned leadership change, the Norwegian oil and gas company said on Wednesday.

titulo: Ted Baker names easyJet executive John Barton as chairman
resumo: Troubled fashion retailer Ted Baker  on Wednesday named easyJet  Chairman John Barton as its non-executive chairman, less than a month after appointing Rachel Osborne to the permanent role of chief executive officer.

titulo: Exclusive: Facebook agreed to censor posts after Vietnam slowed traffic - sources
resumo: Facebook's local servers in Vietnam were taken offline early this year, slowing local traffic to a crawl until it agreed to significantly increase the censorship of "anti-state" posts for local users, two sources at the company told Reuters on Tuesday.

titulo: Apollo, Oaktree, BGH among those circling Virgin Australia: sources
resumo: Private equity and distressed situation specialists Apollo Global Management, Oaktree Capital Management and BGH Capital are among those that have expressed interest in restructuring Virgin Australia Holdings Ltd , five sources said.

titulo: Global CEOs see U-shaped recession due to coronavirus: survey
resumo: Global business leaders are preparing for a drawn-out U-shaped recession due to the impact of coronavirus and many fear their companies won't survive the pandemic, a survey of thousands of chief executives showed on Wednesday.

titulo: Netflix doubles expected signups but warns coronavirus boost may fade
resumo: Netflix Inc  more than doubled its own projections for new customers as quarantined audiences binged on series such as "Tiger King," but the company predicted a weaker second half of the year if stay-at-home orders to fight the coronavirus are lifted.

titulo: $500 billion coronavirus aid package passes U.S. Senate, headed to House
resumo: The U.S. Senate on Tuesday unanimously approved $484 billion in fresh relief for the U.S. economy and hospitals hammered by the coronavirus pandemic, sending the measure to the House of Representatives for final passage later this week.

titulo: United Airlines sells $1 billion of stock in fresh move to weather pandemic
resumo: United Airlines Holdings Inc  on Tuesday announced a public offering to raise more than $1 billion, the first major airline to sell equity to help it survive a sharp travel downturn in the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Lululemon apologizes after staffer offends with 'bat fried rice' T-shirt
resumo: Canadian exercise apparel brand Lululemon  issued statements on Tuesday apologizing for, and distancing itself from, a T-shirt design promoted by one of its art directors that triggered outrage and accusations of racism online.

titulo: Virgin Galactic dealmaker defies IPO lull with $720 million blank-check deal
resumo: Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc  Chairman Chamath Palihapitiya on Tuesday raised $720 million for a new blank-check company, 20% more than its original target, after the coronavirus crisis upended plans last month.

titulo: Trump says he will discuss more coronavirus money for states after current bill passes
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday said discussions on additional coronavirus-related aid for state and local governments would start after Congress passes a bill funding more small business loans, hospitals and testing.

titulo: U.S. Senate deal does not extend assistance to local news outlets
resumo: A nearly $500 billion spending deal reached by U.S. Senate leaders on Tuesday does not expand payroll assistance to struggling local newspapers and broadcast stations, whose advertising revenues have plummeted during the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: U.S.' Pelosi, Schumer  welcome deal with funds for small business, hospitals and testing
resumo: U.S. House Speaker Nancy Pelosi and Senate Democratic leader Chuck Schumer welcomed a bipartisan deal reached on small business loan support, along with money for hospitals and testing, and said the U.S. Congress would aim to pass it this week.

titulo: Trump urges U.S. House to approve latest coronavirus relief bill
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump on Tuesday welcomed the agreement reached between Republican and Democratic lawmakers to provide more coronavirus relief funding for small businesses and others.

titulo: White House mulls liability protection for reopening businesses: Trump adviser
resumo: The Trump administration is considering the possibility of providing liability protection to small businesses that reopen and put their employees back to work as the nation tries to repair the economic damage wrought by the coronavirus, a top White House adviser said on Tuesday.

titulo: Lawsuit claims 10 big banks rigged market for 'odd-lot' U.S. corporate bonds
resumo: Ten of the world's largest banks, including JPMorgan Chase and Bank of America, have been sued for allegedly conspiring over nearly 14 years to rig prices in the $9.6 trillion U.S. corporate bond market, costing ordinary investors billions of dollars.

titulo: California regulator wants PG&E reorganization plan changes, proposes $2 billion fine
resumo: A California regulator has asked PG&E Corp for governance and oversight changes to its reorganization plan, while proposing about $2 billion in penalties for the San-Francisco based utility's role in causing the devastating 2017 and 2018 wildfires in California.

titulo: Strained U.S. mortgage firms get support but push for liquidity facility
resumo: The U.S. housing regulator on Tuesday provided some long-awaited relief for mortgage firms facing billions of dollars of missed home loan repayments, although industry officials said more liquidity assistance was needed.

titulo: JPMorgan Chase plans to return employees to work in phases: memo
resumo: JPMorgan Chase & Co, the largest U.S. lender, said on Tuesday it was working on a plan to bring thousands of employees who have been working from home for more than five weeks back onsite in stages, according to an internal memo seen by Reuters.

titulo: U.S. energy companies' quarterly reports to show depths of slump
resumo: Investors are in for more bad news on the energy front in the coming weeks as a host of the sector's biggest companies report quarterly results following the historic collapse in oil prices.

titulo: Expedia nearing deal to sell stake to Silver Lake and Apollo: WSJ
resumo: Online travel services company Expedia Group Inc  is in advanced talks to sell a stake to private-equity firms Silver Lake Partners and Apollo Global Management Inc  for about $1 billion, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday.

titulo: Exclusive: ValueAct eyes Nintendo with stake of over $1.1 billion - letter
resumo: Activist investor ValueAct Capital Partners LP has built a stake of over $1.1 billion in Nintendo , according to a letter seen by Reuters, a bet that digital software distribution and the development of new entertainment products will fuel growth at the Japanese gaming company.

titulo: Ripple sues YouTube over cryptocurrency scams
resumo: Blockchain firm Ripple sued Alphabet Inc's YouTube on Tuesday, alleging the video-sharing platform failed to protect consumers from cryptocurrency "giveaway" scams that use fake social media profiles to dupe victims into sending money.

titulo: Explainer: Antitrust law will not get in the way of U.S. acting to raise oil prices
resumo: It is illegal for oil producers to meet to discuss pushing up oil prices under U.S. antitrust law, but perfectly legal if state regulators or the federal government set lower production levels for them, legal experts said.

titulo: Brent oil futures plunge as growing glut feeds market panic
resumo: Brent oil futures prices plunged again on Tuesday, extending oil market panic into a second day with no end in sight to a swelling global crude glut as the coronavirus pandemic has obliterated demand for fuel.

titulo: Wall Street tumbles as oil crash stirs pandemic fears
resumo: Wall Street tumbled for a second straight day on Tuesday as a collapse in U.S. oil prices and glum forecasts by companies worsened fears of a deep economic downturn.

titulo: U.S. Senate passes nearly $500 billion coronavirus bill aiding small business
resumo: The U.S. Senate on Tuesday unanimously passed legislation providing nearly $500 billion in additional federal aid to help small businesses hurt by the coronavirus pandemic and to aid hospitals dealing with large numbers of seriously ill patients.

titulo: Oil giveaway spells doom, brings out market bears
resumo: The crash in U.S. crude prices has turned a reliable commodity less than worthless and given fresh urgency to bearish voices, who say it sounds alarm bells for global growth and are bracing for a catastrophic collapse in asset prices.

titulo: Texas Instruments prepares for coronavirus crisis after strong first quarter
resumo: Texas Instruments Inc  on Tuesday reported better-than-expected results for the first quarter but forecast current-quarter revenue and profit largely below Wall Street estimates, as the chip industry braces for a big hit from the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Snap shares rise 20% as coronavirus spurs use, revenue growth beats Street
resumo: Snap Inc  on Tuesday beat Wall Street estimates for quarterly revenue and user growth for its Snapchat app, as more people seek entertainment while they stay at home during the global coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Fiat Chrysler draws down on 6.25 billion euro credit facility
resumo: Fiat Chrysler Automobiles N.V.   said on Tuesday it had drawn down on a 6.25 billion euro ($6.79 billion) credit line to buffer its cash reserves during the uncertainty around the coronavirus health crisis.

titulo: Chipotle deliveries, online orders soar due to coronavirus, sending shares higher
resumo: Chipotle Mexican Grill Inc  on Tuesday reported that soaring digital and delivery sales driven by the coronavirus crisis helped offset the impact of shuttered dining rooms, and the fast-casual chain said it had enough cash and liquidity to get through the next year, sending its shares up more than 5%.

titulo: Analyst View: Oil price crash, what next?
resumo: A historic rout in oil markets sent U.S. crude prices plummeting to as much as minus $40 a barrel on Monday as traders rushed to get rid of unwanted stocks with storage capacity already overflowing amid a coronavirus-induced demand collapse.

titulo: GM folds Maven car-sharing unit, citing coronavirus
resumo: General Motors Co  said on Tuesday it is shutting down its Maven car-sharing unit, after suspending operations because of the novel coronavirus.

titulo: Uber launches website to help drivers navigate U.S. unemployment system
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc  on Tuesday launched a website to help its ride-hail and delivery drivers navigate the intricate U.S. unemployment system, with many gig workers still waiting to receive jobless pay provided under a U.S. coronavirus response bill.

titulo: Boeing names CFO to manage new group overseeing key operations
resumo: Boeing  said on Tuesday it was naming chief financial officer Greg Smith to oversee a newly formed group that will consolidate several key operational areas.

titulo: Oil crash pummels stocks and bond yields; dollar rises
resumo: Stock markets around the world fell on Tuesday, as a collapse in oil prices brought on by oversupply and a shortage of storage further exposed the depth of economic damage from the coronavirus outbreak and sent investors looking for shelter.

titulo: Explainer: How does Texas have the power to curb the state's oil output?
resumo: Texas oil and gas regulators in coming days are poised to decide whether to order larger producers in the state to shut in 20% of their output, wading into global oil politics as the coronavirus crisis slashes demand for oil .

titulo: LendingClub to lay off about a third of employees due to coronavirus
resumo: Online lender LendingClub Corp  said on Tuesday it would lay off 460 employees, including President Steven Allocca, as part of a restructuring plan to slash costs amid the economic fallout of the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Amazon staff take off work to raise safety concerns in pandemic: activists
resumo: A U.S. labor and activist coalition said employees of Amazon.com Inc would take off work starting on Tuesday to draw attention to what it says are unsafe conditions at warehouses during the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Small business loan program discriminated against women, minorities: lawsuit
resumo: The $349 billion small business loan program passed last month as part of the federal coronavirus relief package discriminated against woman- and minority-owned businesses by prioritizing business with employees over those that do not, according to a lawsuit filed by two Maryland business owners.

titulo: U.S. videogame sales surge in March as lockdown keeps people indoor
resumo: Videogame sales in March hit their highest in over a decade, as Americans turned to games like "Animal Crossing: New Horizons" and "Call of Duty: Modern Warfare" because of lockdowns to stem the spread of the coronavrius.

titulo: U.S. House could vote on Thursday on coronavirus loan bill: Hoyer
resumo: More than half the U.S. House of Representatives could return to the capitol on Thursday to pass a coronavirus relief bill that would fund small business loans and hospital aid, chamber's second-most powerful Democrat said on Tuesday, adding he thinks both parties would approve the legislation.

titulo: U.S. Senate's McConnell: reached bipartisan agreement on coronavirus bill
resumo: U.S. Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell welcomed a bipartisan agreement on additional coronavirus relief on Tuesday and said he hoped the Senate would quickly pass it.

titulo: Coronavirus-related job losses hit U.S. black, Hispanic families hardest, studies find
resumo: Black and Hispanic families in the United States are taking the biggest income hit due to the coronavirus pandemic, and they are less prepared to withstand the blow, according to two studies released on Tuesday.

titulo: Exclusive: Trump pressed to halt federal pension investments in China's ZTE, Hikvision
resumo: Lawmakers and former officials are making a last-ditch push to persuade the Trump administration to halt plans to invest billions of federal employee retirement dollars in Chinese companies that Washington suspects of human rights abuses or threatening U.S. security, according to sources and documents seen by Reuters.

titulo: Chipotle to pay $25 million related to food borne illness outbreaks: Justice Department
resumo: Chipotle Mexican Grill  has agreed to pay $25 million and enter a deferred prosecution agreement to resolve charges related to food borne illness outbreaks that sickened hundreds of people between 2015 and 2018, the Justice Department said on Tuesday.

titulo: New York charges Mallinckrodt with insurance fraud over opioid claims
resumo: New York state brought civil charges on Tuesday accusing Mallinckrodt Plc of insurance fraud for misrepresenting the safety and efficacy of its opioid drugs, leading to medically unnecessary prescriptions.

titulo: Corporate America seeks legal protection for when coronavirus lockdowns lift
resumo: Major U.S. business lobbying groups are asking Congress to pass measures that would protect companies large and small from coronavirus-related lawsuits when states start to lift pandemic restrictions and businesses begin to reopen.

titulo: Kering cautious on China rebound after coronavirus hits Gucci sales
resumo: Gucci sales were hit hard early in the coronavirus crisis due to the fashion group's reliance on Chinese customers, owner Kering  said on Tuesday, adding that it was premature to say how quickly China sales would rebound.

titulo: Trump calls for U.S. oil industry bailout as prices plunge
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Tuesday he has asked his cabinet to devise a plan to inject cash into the ailing U.S. oil-drilling industry to help it survive a historic collapse in crude prices.

titulo: Indian court quashes government's call for ban on Deloitte, KPMG affiliate
resumo: An Indian court on Tuesday quashed efforts by the federal government to impose a ban on auditors Deloitte Haskins & Sells LLP and a KPMG affiliate for their alleged abetment of a financial fraud at a domestic firm.

titulo: Telecom Italia and Open Fiber crank up war of words over single network
resumo: A war of words between Telecom Italia  (TIM) and Open Fiber over the creation of a single broadband network in Italy escalated on Tuesday as Telecom Italia called its smaller rival's business model a "failure".

titulo: In Versailles, king of online retail Amazon fights unions backlash
resumo: Amazon  made its case on Tuesday in a French court of appeal to keep deliveries on track in the country, rejecting unions' allegations that it was not doing its best to protect employees from COVID-19.

titulo: CPC oil producers eye boost to flows via Russia from May
resumo: Kazakh oil producers, which export crude via the Caspian  pipeline consortium (CPC), are seeking to divert flows to Russia's Transneft from May in an attempt to minimize losses as CPC Blend margins and demand fall, five market sources said.

titulo: Explainer: What is a negative crude future and does it mean anything for consumers?
resumo: The price of a barrel of benchmark U.S. oil plunged below $0 a barrel on Monday for the first time in history, a troubling sign of an unprecedented global energy glut as the coronavirus pandemic halts travel and curbs economic activity.

titulo: Lockheed Martin trims sales outlook as coronavirus hits supply chain
resumo: Lockheed Martin  said on Tuesday the spread of coronavirus has delayed shipments of vital supplies to its numerous businesses and will likely hurt its sales this year.

titulo: Amazon extends closure of French warehouses until April 25: statement
resumo: Amazon  is extending the closure of its French warehouses until April 25 included, the online retail giant said in a written statement on Tuesday.

titulo: We can make more ventilators if government ups order, says UK business consortium
resumo: A group of firms, including Airbus and Ford, could build more than the current government order of 20,000 ventilators and continue once normal operations begin to resume, the head of the project told Reuters on Tuesday.

titulo: Hailstorm devastates hundreds of hectares of Bordeaux vines in SW France
resumo: A violent hailstorm that hit the Bordeaux wine region last Friday has caused severe damage, with several hundred hectares of vines nearly destroyed, notably in the famous Saint-Emilion region, the local chamber of agriculture said on Tuesday.

titulo: U.S. home sales tumble as coronavirus keeps buyers indoors
resumo: U.S. home sales dropped by the most in nearly 4-1/2 years in March as extraordinary measures to control the spread of the novel coronavirus brought buyer traffic to a virtual standstill, with realtors and economists expecting a further deterioration in housing market activity through the second quarter.

titulo: Air Canada to suspend flights to U.S. on coronavirus concerns
resumo: Air Canada  said on Tuesday it would suspend flights  between Canada and the United States after April 26 due to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Battered consumer confidence to slow airlines' recovery: IATA
resumo: Faltering consumer confidence will slow the recovery of air travel once coronavirus restrictions end, the sector's main global body warned on Tuesday, citing bleak new survey data.

titulo: Germany's BaFin fines BlackRock for late filing of voting rights
resumo: The German financial markets watchdog BaFin said on Tuesday it had fined BlackRock Investment Management (UK) Ltd  744,000 euros ($809,000) for failure to file voting rights notifications in a timely way.

titulo: After ventilators, Xerox now plans to make hand sanitizers
resumo: Xerox Holdings Corp  said on Tuesday it will make hand sanitizer in its factories in the United States and Canada to fight the spread of coronavirus, and start deliveries to healthcare organizations later this month.

titulo: U.S. energy secretary to urge House lawmakers to buy oil for strategic reserve
resumo: The U.S. energy secretary said on Tuesday he would talk with leaders in the U.S. House of Representatives later in the day and urge them to fund the purchase of crude to fill the emergency oil reserve.

titulo: Qatar Airways defers half of some employees' wages for three months
resumo: Qatar Airways on Tuesday said mid-level employees and above will have their salaries halved for at least three months but that staff would be back paid when the airline could afford it.

titulo: Top House Republican McCarthy confirms deal reached on small business loan bill
resumo: The top Republican in the U.S. House of Representatives said on Tuesday that a deal had been reached to replenish funding for an emergency program that extends loans to small businesses hit by a drop in demand due to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: IHS slashes 2020 global light vehicle sales outlook on coronavirus hit
resumo: Data firm IHS Markit sharply lowered its 2020 forecast for global light vehicle sales on Tuesday and warned that the United States will take the biggest hit from the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Analyst View: After U.S. oil futures crash, what next?
resumo: A historic rout in oil markets sent U.S. crude prices plummeting to as much as minus $40 a barrel as traders rushed to get rid of unwanted stocks with storage capacity already overflowing amid a coronavirus-induced demand collapse.

titulo: Remember the 1980s glut: Ex-BP boss Browne warns oil will stay low
resumo: The oil price will stay low for some time as supply exceeds demand and the current situation on global oil markets is reminiscent of the 1980s oil glut, former BP boss John Browne said on Tuesday.

titulo: Oil dip unsurprising, second-quarter GDP drop could be biggest ever: White House's Hassett
resumo: White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett said on Tuesday the current dip in oil prices is due to a "very short-run thing" and markets will find a new normal.

titulo: Why Brent oil won't follow U.S. WTI futures below zero
resumo: Brent oil futures are set for extreme volatility when the front-month June contract expires next week, but they will avoid the historic plunge below zero that U.S. peer WTI has suffered, traders and analysts said.

titulo: Dash for emergency cash provokes fund industry backlash
resumo: As companies pull the emergency cash cord to help them through the coronavirus crisis, shareholders have been split by an easing of the rules to make it easier for them to turn to investors with the deepest pockets.

titulo: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD's profit slumps after subsidy cuts
resumo: Chinese electric vehicle maker BYD Co Ltd ,  reported on Tuesday a 42% drop in 2019 profit, saying cuts in government subsidies and emissions rules changes had hit demand in the sector.

titulo: UK made a firm decision on Huawei in 5G: foreign ministry's top official
resumo: Britain's government made a firm decision to allow China's Huawei to have a role in building the country's 5G phone network and as far as the foreign ministry's top official understands it is not being reopened, he said on Tuesday.

titulo: Coronavirus lockdowns to hurt Coca-Cola's second quarter sales
resumo: Coca-Cola Co  said on Tuesday its expected current-quarter results to take a hit as restaurants, theaters and other venues that represent about half of the company's revenue remain closed because of the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: SAP reverts to sole CEO to handle coronavirus pandemic
resumo: The coronavirus pandemic has brought the latest experiment in dual leadership at SAP  to an abrupt end as the German business software group named Christian Klein as its sole CEO to provide clarity to its 400,000 customers.

titulo: SoftBank shares attractive near-term as buyback eyed: UK's AVI
resumo: SoftBank Group's  planned $22.5 billion share buyback presents an attractive buying opportunity but it is not a clear longterm investment, said a London-based investor that has built up a small stake.

titulo: Starbucks brings Beyond Meat and Oatly to China with new plant-based lunch menu
resumo: Starbucks Corp  said on Monday it would roll out a plant-based food and beverage menu in China this week, launching Beyond Meat Inc  and Oatly products in a country which is trying to recover from coronavirus shutdowns.

titulo: The half a trillion dollar British money transfusion that may not be enough
resumo: It took Lucio and Meera Santoro 35 years to build up their London design company and just a week to watch it fall apart.

titulo: U.S. economy likely set for U-shaped recovery after deep rut: Reuters poll
resumo: The U.S. economic recession underway, caused by the coronavirus pandemic, will be worse than previously thought, with more economists polled by Reuters over the past week expecting a "U-shaped" recovery rather than any other option.

titulo: Google drops charges on shopping service to counter Amazon's surging ad sales
resumo: Google said on Tuesday it would stop charging merchants to place products on its Google Shopping search page as it looks to win e-commerce advertising business from Amazon.com Inc and other online retailers, just as they are struggling to supply customers with some items due to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Coronavirus casualty Virgin Australia could prosper under new owners
resumo: Virgin Australia's  entry into administration could give any successful bidder for the country's second-biggest airline the chance to free it from a complex ownership structure that has slowed decision making and been blamed for years of losses.

titulo: Primark owner scraps dividend, takes charge for surplus stock as coronavirus crisis hits
resumo: Primark owner Associated British Foods  will not pay an interim dividend to save cash during the coronavirus crisis and has booked a 284 million pound ($352 million) charge to reflect an expected lower value of stock when its stores reopen.

titulo: Primark owner AB Foods right not to pay dividend, says boss
resumo: Primark owner Associated British Foods'  decision not to pay an interim dividend to shareholders was "absolutely the right thing to do" given the coronavirus crisis, its boss said on Tuesday.

titulo: Senators from both U.S. parties press Fed for climate details
resumo: Senators from both major U.S. parties are pressing the Federal Reserve to say more about the role that climate considerations could play in its asset purchase programs, showing the new focus of investors on environmental considerations.

titulo: Tech titans dominate U.S. stock market after surge
resumo: Investors are once again crowding into a narrow range of technology and internet stocks, heightening concerns that the market's dramatic bounce from last month's lows is becoming increasingly vulnerable to sharp reversals as the coronavirus outbreak continues to batter the economy.

titulo: Demand for masks crashes website of Japan's Sharp
resumo: The website of Japanese electronics firm Sharp  crashed on Tuesday after the company started selling face masks online, as Japan's tally of coronavirus infections passed 11,000.

titulo: Daimler teams up with Volvo Trucks in fuel cell venture
resumo: Daimler and Volvo Trucks have agreed to join forces to develop, produce and sell fuel cell systems for heavy-duty vehicles, in a sign that the coronavirus crisis is accelerating consolidation.

titulo: London Stock Exchange committed to Refinitiv deal in pandemic-hit markets
resumo: The London Stock Exchange said on Tuesday it was committed to completing its $27 billion takeover of data company Refinitiv in the second half of the year, with no plans to revise its savings targets for the deal as a deep recession beckons.

titulo: LSE says no plans to revisit savings target from Refinitiv deal
resumo: The London Stock Exchange said it has no plans to change its targets for synergies from its planned $27 billion takeover of data and analytics company Refinitiv.

titulo: South Korea's Asiana to get $1.4 billion liquidity from state banks

titulo: Virgin Australia falls to virus crisis, appoints administrator to find investor
resumo: Virgin Australia Holdings Ltd  on Tuesday succumbed to third-party led restructuring that could lead to a sale, making Australia's second-biggest airline the Asia-Pacific region's biggest victim of the coronavirus crisis gripping the industry.

titulo: Huawei first-quarter revenue growth slows sharply amid U.S. ban, virus headwinds
resumo: Huawei Technologies' revenue growth slowed sharply in the first quarter but the world's top telecoms equipment supplier labeled the performance as "resilient" in light of pressure from the United States and the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

titulo: Nissan to close global headquarters, other Japan sites through May 10 due to virus
resumo: Nissan Motor Co  said on Tuesday it will shut its global headquarters in Japan for 16 days through early May to contain the spread of the coronavirus, even though the government has permitted keeping workplaces open to get the economy running.

titulo: Volvo and Daimler tie up to develop fuel cell for trucks
resumo: Swedish truck maker Volvo  said on Tuesday it had signed a deal with German rival Daimler  to set up a joint venture to develop and produce fuel cell systems for heavy-duty vehicles.

titulo: Ferrari rolls out coronavirus testing to get staff ready for work
resumo: Workers at Ferrari  are signing up en masse for voluntary coronavirus screening so they can return to work as soon as possible in an ambitious scheme that could serve as a blueprint for manufacturers desperate to resume production.

titulo: Fast Retailing plans to reopen two Uniqlo stores in Berlin this week
resumo: Casual clothing chain Uniqlo plans to reopen two stores in Berlin this week, the first in Europe to resume business after nearly all of its stores there were closed due to the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Senate moves toward possible Tuesday coronavirus vote, Pelosi sees deal 'on principles'
resumo: The U.S. Congress on Monday inched toward a $450 billion deal to help small businesses and hospitals hurt by the coronavirus pandemic, with House Speaker Nancy Pelosi saying negotiators had come to terms on the 'principles' of the package as the Senate set a Tuesday session for a potential vote.

titulo: WeWork directors defend right to represent shareholders against SoftBank
resumo: A special committee of WeWork board members on Monday rejected an assertion by SoftBank Group Corp that it does not have authority to represent the interests of minority shareholders of the office-sharing startup.

titulo: Exclusive: Lord & Taylor explores bankruptcy as stores remain shut in coronavirus pandemic
resumo: Lord & Taylor is exploring filing for bankruptcy protection after it was forced to temporarily shut all of its 38 U.S. department stores in the wake of the coronavirus outbreak, people familiar with the matter said on Monday.

titulo: Burger chain Shake Shack to return $10 million government loan
resumo: Shake Shack Inc said it will return the small business loan it received from the U.S. government, making it the first major firm to hand back money provided to help businesses ride out the impact of the coronavirus lockdown.

titulo: UAW, Detroit automakers in talks over safety rules to reopen U.S. plants
resumo: Detroit's automakers and the United Auto Workers (UAW) union are in talks about safely restarting U.S. production halted by the coronavirus pandemic, but local union leaders said on Monday any workers who feel sick must be allowed to self quarantine without losing pay.

titulo: U.S. Treasury releases $2.9 billion in airline support, finalizes payroll agreements
resumo: The U.S. Treasury Department said on Monday it had disbursed $2.9 billion in initial payroll assistance to 54 smaller passenger carrier and two major passenger airlines, while it finalized grant agreements with six major airlines.

titulo: U.S.' Pelosi: negotiators agreed 'principles' of next coronavirus aid package
resumo: U.S. House Speaker Nancy Pelosi said on Monday Republican and Democratic negotiators have come to terms on the "principles" of the next coronavirus relief package, which is expected to focus on more aid for small businesses and be taken up in the U.S. Congress this week.

titulo: Exclusive: Brazil considers further flight cuts as coronavirus crisis bites
resumo: Brazil's government is discussing with the country's main airlines further reducing a minimal flight schedule implemented due to the coronavirus crisis as travel demand remains close to zero, three sources told Reuters.

titulo: U.S. energy industry steps up lobbying for Fed's emergency aid: letters
resumo: The U.S. energy industry has asked the Federal Reserve to change the terms of a $600 billion lending facility so that oil and gas companies can use the funds to repay their ballooning debts, according to a letter seen by Reuters.

titulo: Wall Street drops as oil traders cannot give away U.S. crude
resumo: Wall Street tumbled on Monday after U.S. crude futures turned negative for the first time ever, with traders forced to pay to unload crude as the May contract expired during a global economic slump unleashed by the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Trump hopes for deal in U.S. Congress on small business program, vote possible in Senate Tuesday
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday he hoped negotiators in Congress would reach a deal to provide more aid for small businesses hurt by the coronavirus pandemic, with a vote possible in the Senate on Tuesday.

titulo: IBM sees shift in client spend priorities, withdraws annual forecast

titulo: GE enters into $15 billion loan agreement
resumo: General Electric Co  entered into a revolving credit agreement for $15 billion, according to a regulatory filing on Monday.

titulo: Negative $40 oil reflects panic - and U.S. crude market economic reality
resumo: Traders desperate to avoid owning oil fled the markets on Monday, sending crude futures into negative territory for the first time ever, in recognition that the coronavirus pandemic has sapped demand for fuel and there is not enough storage for the massive glut of oil present on U.S. soil.

titulo: Oil price crashes into negative territory for the first time in history amid pandemic

titulo: IMF may need 'exceptional measures' to facilitate pandemic response: Georgieva

titulo: Plunge in U.S. oil prices sinks equities; bonds yields edge lower
resumo: A historic plunge in U.S. crude oil prices pulled global equity markets lower on Monday and helped drive bond prices higher as investors sought out safe havens amid the economic carnage of the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Instant View: Spot U.S. oil futures crash below zero with nowhere to store crude
resumo: Energy traders fled from the expiring May U.S. oil futures contract in a frenzy on Monday, sending the contract deep into negative territory for the first time in history, as barely any buyers are willing to take delivery of oil barrels because there is no place to put the crude.

titulo: U.S. restaurants seek more aid, on track to lose $240 billion by end of 2020

titulo: United Airlines sees $2.1 billion loss as coronavirus hits Latin America growth hopes, seeks more federal aid

titulo: SunPower idles factories as coronavirus hits solar demand

titulo: Coronavirus pushes Latin America's budding startups into survival mode

titulo: Volkswagen in Brazil to keep factories closed through April
resumo: Volkswagen AG  said on Monday it will keep its factories in Brazil, South America's top auto producer, shut through the end of the month, but that a decision has not been made on whether it will reopen them in May or later.

titulo: Vivendi shareholders approve dividend of 0.60 euro per share
resumo: Vivendi's  shareholders approved on Monday the payment of a dividend of 0.60 euro per share for the fiscal year 2019, with 99.52% of shareholder votes supporting the plan.

titulo: FCC approves Ligado plan to deploy mobile broadband network

titulo: Atlantia unit eyes govt guarantee scheme to fight virus impact - sources

titulo: IMF's Georgieva fears coronavirus impact on developing countries

titulo: IMF's Georgieva says coronavirus crisis is worst since Great Depression
resumo: The crisis sparked by the spread of the novel coronavirus is the worst since the Great Depression, IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said on Monday.

titulo: Investor groups call for UK firms to provide a virtual AGM

titulo: Branson says Virgin Atlantic will need UK government help to survive

titulo: 'Fasten your seatbelt': Investors brace for Europe Inc. results amid coronavirus

titulo: Halliburton books $1.1 billion charge, slashes spending by 50%

titulo: Saudi Arabia gets physical with Russia in underground oil bout
resumo: Behind a Saudi-Russian truce to stabilise oil markets with a record output cut, market players are seeing the two production heavyweights still trading blows in the physical market.

titulo: Some life insurers hit pause on older Americans during coronavirus crisis
resumo: (This April 17 story corrects to fix name of company in second paragraph to make it Protective Life Insurance Co instead of Protective Insurance Co.)

titulo: Investors much less confident about market outlook, Trump re-election chance: survey
resumo: The markets are much less confident about Europe and the United States getting back to business ahead of the summer, a Deutsche Bank investor survey showed on Monday, even as some countries begin gradually re-opening their economies.

titulo: Standard Chartered, Astra International agree to reduced sale price for stake in Indonesian bank
resumo: Standard Chartered  said on Monday it had agreed with Indonesian conglomerate PT Astra International  to sell their stake in Indonesia's PT Bank Permata  to lender Bangkok Bank PCL  at a reduced price.

titulo: Exclusive: Russia orders companies to cut oil output by 20% from February levels - sources

resumo: Norwegian Air  on Monday reported that four Swedish and Danish subsidiaries had filed for bankruptcy and that it had ended staffing contracts in Europe and the United States, putting some 4,700 jobs at risk.

titulo: Exclusive: Airbus puts six jets made for AirAsia up for sale as crisis deepens
resumo: Airbus  has put six jets made for one of its largest customers up for sale after giving up on Malaysia's AirAsia  taking delivery of them, sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

titulo: Chicago Fed economic index takes recessionary plunge in March

titulo: Virgin Australia poised to enter voluntary administration: sources
resumo: Virgin Australia  is poised to enter voluntary administration, two sources close to the matter said on Monday, with the cash-strapped airline unable to weather the coronavirus crisis because of its A$5 billion ($3.2 billion) of debt.

titulo: Coronavirus relief deal for U.S. small businesses may come Monday, Trump says
resumo: U.S. Democrats and Republicans are near agreement on extra money to help small businesses hurt by the coronavirus pandemic and could seal a deal as early as Monday, President Donald Trump said on Sunday.

titulo: Exclusive: India foreign investment rules aimed at China to include Hong Kong - sources
resumo: New Indian rules to ensure scrutiny of investments from companies based in neighbouring countries, especially during the coronavirus outbreak, will also apply to Hong Kong, two senior government sources told Reuters on Monday.

titulo: KFC to sell plant-based fried chicken made by Cargill in China

titulo: Brazil set to start QE cautiously but may need to bring out 'bazooka'

titulo: Bridgestone to suspend 11 Japanese plants during late April-early May

titulo: China's top brokerage hires Vanguard ex-Asia boss for global drive
resumo: China's Citic Securities Co. ,  has hired Charles Lin, the former Asia head of Vanguard Group, to be vice chairman of Hong Kong unit CLSA, as the biggest Chinese securities company seeks to build up an international presence.

titulo: Germany to grant rescue liquidity for carrier Condor: sources

titulo: Danske Bank board proposes no dividends be paid for 2019
resumo: Danske Bank's  board of director will propose that no dividends be paid for 2019, it said in a statement on Monday.

titulo: Primark commits to paying for 370 million pounds of orders during coronavirus crisis
resumo: Fashion retailer Primark has committed to pay suppliers for 370 million pounds ($461 million) of orders, though all its stores are closed due to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: New Aston Martin boss says focus on SUV launch, restarting factories

titulo: Ray-Ban maker EssilorLuxottica to scrap dividend, eyes cost cuts
resumo: Oakley and Ray-Ban maker EssilorLuxottica  said on Monday it would scrap its dividend and might also consider cost cuts as the coronavirus pandemic chokes its business.

titulo: Wynn Resorts CEO calls for Las Vegas Strip to conditionally reopen in mid- to late May
resumo: Wynn Resorts Chief Executive Officer Matt Maddox on Sunday called on the Nevada governor to begin to reopen the Las Vegas Strip in mid- to late May with extensive safety measures in place, assuming the state is in line with certain benchmarks around the spread of the coronavirus.

titulo: China's CDB Financial scraps purchase of 29 Boeing 737 MAX jets

titulo: With economy in crisis, U.S. lets importers delay some tariff payments
resumo: The Trump administration on Sunday said it would allow importers hit by the national health crisis to defer for three months any tariff payments they owe the government.

titulo: Facebook to introduce gaming app on Monday: New York Times
resumo: Facebook Inc  is planning to launch a gaming mobile app on Monday focused mainly on live game streaming in a bid to take on Amazon.com Inc's  Twitch, Google's  YouTube and Microsoft Corp's  Mixer services, the New York Times reported on Sunday.

titulo: Stock market gains alongside economic pain; some worry about over-optimism
resumo: The U.S. stock market has rebounded swiftly despite a raft of terrible economic news, driven by a massive boost from the Federal Reserve, hopes of a successful reopening of the economy and possible coronavirus treatments, as well as investors' fear-of-missing-out. But not everyone is buying the bounce.

titulo: Amazon to extend French warehouse closures after union dispute
resumo: Amazon  will keep its six warehouses in France closed until at least April 22 after a stand-off with unions over sanitary conditions amid the coronavirus outbreak, it said on Sunday.

titulo: U.S. banks seek term changes as Fed finalizes Main Street Lending Program
resumo: U.S. banks are pushing the Federal Reserve to change the terms of a $600 billion lending program for small and medium-sized businesses, including reducing minimum loan sizes and allowing more flexibility on underlying reference rates, industry groups said.

titulo: Exclusive: Amazon deploys thermal cameras at warehouses to scan for fevers faster
resumo: Amazon.com Inc has started to use thermal cameras at its warehouses to speed up screening for feverish workers who could be infected with the coronavirus, employees told Reuters.

titulo: Coronavirus set to usher in big changes at U.S. offices
resumo: The novel coronavirus may do to U.S. office rentals what e-commerce did to malls, or so goes a line of thinking running through commercial real estate circles.

titulo: Beer may lose its fizz as CO2 supplies go flat during pandemic
resumo: Dwindling supplies of carbon dioxide from ethanol plants are sparking concern about shortages of beer, soda and seltzer water - essentials for many quarantined Americans.

titulo: Main Street bailout rewards U.S. restaurant chains, firms in rural states
resumo: Over two frantic weeks, the U.S. government pledged $350 billion to Main Street businesses across America desperate for cash after coronavirus lockdowns.

titulo: Wall Street Week Ahead: In companies' earnings, words may matter more than numbers
resumo: Investors gearing up for another week of corporate earnings may give more weight to the words of company executives than headline-grabbing numbers, as Wall Street seeks evidence that corporations can weather the uncertainty caused by the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: U.S. judge puts Amazon challenge to Pentagon JEDI contract on hold
resumo: A U.S. judge on Friday put on hold a suit by Amazon.com Inc  challenging the Pentagon's decision to award a $10 billion contract to Microsoft Corp .

titulo: Walmart to hire 50,000 more workers in coronavirus-driven hiring spree
resumo: Walmart Inc  said on Friday it would hire 50,000 more workers at its stores, clubs and distribution centers to meet a surge in demand for groceries and household essentials from consumers stockpiling during the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Ford issues $8 billion debt securities after virus causes $2 billion loss
resumo: Ford Motor Co  on Friday raised $8 billion from corporate debt investors to shore up its cash reserves as the coronavirus outbreak pummeled vehicle sales and production, resulting in an estimated loss of about $2 billion for the first quarter.

titulo: Netflix adds $50 million to relief fund for production workers
resumo: Netflix Inc  has increased the amount of money in its coronavirus emergency relief fund by $50 million, bringing the total for displaced production workers to $150 million, a company spokeswoman said on Friday.

titulo: AMC says it has enough cash through July as movie theaters aim to re-open
resumo: AMC Entertainment Holdings Inc , the world's largest movie theater operator, said on Friday it has enough cash to withstand a global suspension of operations until a possible partial reopening of its locations in July.

titulo: GM dispatches first batch of ventilators for COVID-19 patients

titulo: Gilead increases enrollment target for remdesivir trial in COVID-19 patients

titulo: NY Fed markets head says 'time is of the essence' in establishing new lending facilities
