titulo: Global economy in 2020 on track for sharpest downturn since 1930s: IMF
resumo: The global economy is expected to shrink by 3.0% during 2020 in a stunning coronavirus-driven collapse of activity that will mark the steepest downturn since the Great Depression of the 1930s, the International Monetary Fund said on Tuesday.

titulo: J&J sees medical devices business recovery at end of year after taking coronavirus hit
resumo: Johnson & Johnson  on Tuesday said it expects its  medical device business to begin recovering in the fourth quarter as elective medical procedures delayed by the coronavirus pandemic start to resume.

titulo: Two big U.S. banks offer glum outlook for coronavirus recession
resumo: The main things investors wanted to learn from first-quarter results by JPMorgan Chase & Co  and Wells Fargo & Co  on Tuesday was just how bad the coronavirus recession is going to get and how long it will take to get back to normal.

titulo: JPMorgan profit dives as banks brace for coronavirus-led loan defaults
resumo: JPMorgan Chase & Co's profit plunged by more than two-thirds in the first quarter as the largest U.S. bank put aside nearly $7 billion in reserves to protect it from a wave of potential loan defaults in the months ahead.

titulo: Explainer: Trump has little power to restart U.S. economy
resumo: President Donald Trump said on Monday it was his decision when to reopen the U.S. economy, which has been largely shut down to slow the spread of the novel coronavirus.

titulo: Fiat Chrysler bolsters cash with $3.8 billion syndicated credit
resumo: Fiat Chrysler  said on Tuesday it had successfully completed the syndication of a 3.5 billion euro ($3.8 billion)credit facility which it had agreed last month with two banks.

titulo: Amazon ordered to limit French trade to essential goods

titulo: Tesla surges 28% in two days as traders look beyond coronavirus disruption
resumo: Shares of Tesla Inc  surged 12% on Tuesday after Credit Suisse upgraded its rating on the electric carmaker and as Wall Street rallied on optimism the coronavirus outbreak was reaching a plateau.

titulo: Adidas gets 3 billion euro government backed loan, suspends dividend
resumo: German sports retailer Adidas  on Tuesday said it received approval for a syndicated 3 billion-euro ($3.3 billion) government-backed loan to mitigate the financial impact on its business from the spread of the coronavirus.

titulo: Spirit expects quarterly loss as coronavirus hits plane output
resumo: Spirit AeroSystems Holdings Inc  said on Tuesday it would swing to a first-quarter loss and warned of a bigger blow in the current quarter, as customers Boeing Co  and Airbus SE  whittle down production due to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: NMC Health board replaced by administrators
resumo: The administrators of troubled hospital operator NMC Health  said on Tuesday they had replaced the company's board and asked its main lender, Abu Dhabi Commercial Bank (ADCB), to lead a steering group to discuss cutting its debt pile.

titulo: Spain denies requisitioning coronavirus tests bound for workers
resumo: Spain's health minister denied on Tuesday that authorities had requisitioned 2,000 antibody tests which a workers union said Siemens Gamesa  had expected to receive to check whether its staff have been infected with the coronavirus.

titulo: Fauci: May 1 target for reopening U.S. economy 'overly optimistic' - AP
resumo: The top U.S. infectious disease expert, Anthony Fauci, said on Tuesday that a May 1 target date for reopening the economy was "a bit overly optimistic," citing a lack of critical testing and tracing procedures.

titulo: U.S. funds that beat market plunge seek recession-proof stocks
resumo: Beating the first quarter's market crash took some extreme strategies, such as moving nearly everything to cash, or going very short. Now, fund managers who prevailed during the worst start to a year for U.S. stocks are betting on recession-resilient companies to take them to the next leg.

titulo: Honda extends Mexico plant suspensions to April 30 due to coronavirus
resumo: The Mexican unit of Japanese automaker Honda Motor Co said on Tuesday it would extend a suspenion of operations at its plants in the states of Guanajuato and Jalisco until April 30 due to the impact of the novel coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Renault quits its main China venture after weak sales
resumo: French automaker Renault SA  is ditching its main passenger car business in China following poor sales at the loss-making venture with Dongfeng Motor Group .

titulo: Barclays delays incentive payments to CEO, CFO due to coronavirus
resumo: Barclays  is delaying the release of portions of Chief Executive Jes Staley and Chief Financial Officer Tushar Morzaria's bonuses in light of the new coronavirus pandemic, it said on Tuesday.

titulo: Norwegian Air shares plummet as survival hinges on creditors

titulo: U.S. awards airports $10 billion in grants amid travel falloff
resumo: The U.S. Transportation Department on Tuesday awarded nearly $10 billion to U.S. airports struggling with a massive falloff in travel demand because of the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Exclusive: Saudi Aramco in talks with banks to borrow about $10 billion - sources

titulo: J.C. Penney to hire advisors as it mulls debt management options: Bloomberg News
resumo: J.C. Penney Co Inc  has approached consulting firm AlixPartners LLP as the U.S. retailer looks at options for managing its debt, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

titulo: Qatar Airways signs $850 million financing deal with Standard Chartered
resumo: Qatar Airways and Standard Chartered have signed an $850 million financing deal for seven Boeing 787-9 aircraft, a statement from the Qatari airline said on Tuesday.

titulo: U.S. import prices post biggest decline in over five years; more to come

titulo: G7 backs debt moratorium for poor countries if G20 creditors, Paris Club join
resumo: Group of Seven (G7) finance officials on Tuesday vowed to continue to act as needed to combat the coronavirus pandemic and stabilize the global economy, and threw their support behind a push to provide temporary debt relief to the poorest countries.

titulo: BOJ's Kuroda told G7 ready to ease policy to combat coronavirus fallout
resumo: Bank of Japan Governor Haruhiko Kuroda said on Tuesday he explained to his G7 counterparts that the central bank would not hesitate easing monetary policy further depending on how the coronavirus pandemic affects the country's economy.

titulo: G7 agreed on need to aid emerging economies battling with virus: Japan's Aso
resumo: G7 finance leaders agreed on Tuesday on the need to provide financial and technical assistance to emerging economies battling with the fallout from the coronavirus pandemic, Japanese Finance Minister Taro Aso said.

titulo: Hyundai first to restart Czech production, others still shut

titulo: Bombardier to resume some operations in UK
resumo: Bombardier Inc  said on Tuesday it plans to resume production at some of its facilities in the United Kingdom, which had been halted in March due to government-mandated social distancing to battle the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Spain says IMF outlook confirms view of strong, short-lived crisis

titulo: Debt levels expected to sharply increase in 2020, stabilize as economies recover: IMF

titulo: Alphabet soup: How will post-virus economic recovery shape up?
resumo: (This story had been refiled to add missing words in paragraph 16 of story first published April 9.)

titulo: Dash for cash as most investors expect drawn-out recovery: BofA survey
resumo: Investors have been hoarding cash at a rate not seen since the immediate aftermath of the 9/11 attacks of 2001, BofA's April fund manager survey showed, as the coronavirus roiled global financial markets.

titulo: Britain's Next re-opens online before maxing out daily limit

titulo: Barclays halts new job cuts during coronavirus crisis: memo

titulo: Pandemic survival plans: U.S. businesses scramble to conserve cash, boost liquidity
resumo: Charlie Straface, president of Norwegian aluminum maker Norsk Hydro's North American extrusion operations, convenes a coronavirus task force every other day to draw up cost-cutting measures to offset declining revenues and protect the unit's cash balance during the economic slump related to the outbreak.

titulo: Toyota plans limited operations in France, Poland from April 22
resumo: Toyota Motor Corp  on Monday said it plans to restart limited production at vehicle plants in France and Poland from April 22 after closing them due to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Next's reopened website closed to new orders by 0730 GMT

titulo: Car parts group Valeo drops 2020 targets, gets 1 billion euros of new credit lines

resumo: Apple Inc  shipped roughly 2.5 million iPhones in China in March, a slight rebound after one of its worst months in the country ever, according to government data published on Friday.

titulo: Trump says plan to re-open shuttered U.S. economy to be completed soon
resumo: President Donald Trump said on Monday that his administration was close to completing a plan to re-open the U.S. economy, which has been largely shut down to slow the spread of the novel coronavirus.

titulo: Exxon raises $9.5 billion to load up on cash while debt market still open to new deals
resumo: Exxon Mobil Corp on Monday raised $9.5 billion in new debt, with the largest U.S. oil producer seeking to bolster its finances while debt markets remain open to new deals.

titulo: Disney enters $5 billion credit agreement

titulo: U.S. airlines nearing acceptance of payroll aid plan: sources

titulo: Factbox: Non OPEC+ crude oil supply cuts

resumo: Smithfield Foods, the world's biggest pork processor, said on Sunday it will shut a U.S. plant indefinitely due to a rash of coronavirus cases among employees and warned the country was  moving "perilously close to the edge" in supplies for grocers.

titulo: Factbox: Spread of coronavirus closes North American meat plants

titulo: Trump says plan to re-open shuttered U.S. economy to be completed soon
resumo: President Donald Trump said on Monday that his administration was close to completing a plan to re-open the U.S. economy, which has been largely shut down to slow the spread of the novel coronavirus.

titulo: Dow, S&P 500 slide as focus shifts to earnings; Nasdaq gains
resumo: The Dow and S&P 500 fell on Monday as U.S. companies prepared to kick off a quarterly earnings season expected to be rough due to the coronavirus pandemic, while Amazon.com  gains helped the Nasdaq end higher.

titulo: Telco shares are surprise losers as lockdown drives internet boom
resumo: As lockdowns worldwide drive a surge in internet use, boosting online sales for businesses as varied as gaming and food delivery, the stocks of internet providers are an unlikely laggard on global markets.

titulo: The list of U.S. Treasury questions for airlines seeking bailout cash
resumo: U.S. Treasury officials have asked airlines seeking money from a $25 billion fund for payroll support to provide detailed information on their capital structure, liquidity and collateral, people briefed on the matter said.

titulo: News Corp sees hit to ad revenue from coronavirus pandemic
resumo: Rupert Murdoch's News Corp  said on Monday its newspaper advertising revenue would take a hit from widespread business closures caused by the coronavirus outbreak, although paid digital subscriptions for the Wall Street Journal are rising.

titulo: 'Elbow to elbow:' North America meat plant workers fall ill, walk off jobs
resumo: At a Wayne Farms chicken processing plant in Alabama, workers recently had to pay the company 10 cents a day to buy masks to protect themselves from the new coronavirus, according to a meat inspector.

titulo: Crude mixed on output deal, recession fears; shares slip
resumo: Stocks on Wall Street mostly slid on Monday over concerns of what the coronavirus pandemic will do to corporate earnings, while crude prices were mixed as a global deal on record output cuts failed to quell doubts the pact would head off an oil glut.

titulo: Amazon stops accepting new online grocery customers amid surging demand
resumo: Amazon will begin to put new grocery delivery customers on a wait list and curtail shopping hours at some Whole Foods stores to prioritize orders from existing customers buying food online during the coronavirus outbreak, the company said on Sunday.

titulo: Trump: It's my decision when to reopen U.S. economy
resumo: President Donald Trump said on Monday it was his decision when to reopen the U.S. economy, not that of state governors, but legal experts disagree and governors are going their own way.

titulo: Oil mixed as demand worries offset gains from output cut deal
resumo: Oil prices were mixed on Monday, as the historic production-cut deal inked by major global oil producers was not enough to assuage existing worries about the demand destruction brought on by the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Exclusive: U.S. Treasury wants details on airline debt, liquidity, loyalty programs - sources
resumo: U.S. Treasury officials have asked airlines seeking government payroll support to provide additional detailed information on capital structure, liquidity and loyalty programs, people briefed on the matter told Reuters.

titulo: Exclusive: Treasury wants warrants, repayment from major U.S. airlines on 30% of grant money - sources
resumo: (This April 10 story has been refiled to fix typo in 'aid' in paragraph nineteen.)

titulo: U.S. airlines ease loyalty programs in coronavirus travel slump
resumo: U.S. airlines are extending loyalty program benefits and status into 2021 for members homebound because of the new coronavirus.

titulo: Single passenger flights: The daily woes of airlines, and the crew still working
resumo: When Reuters photographer Carlos Barria boarded American Airlines flight 4511 from Washington Reagan National Airport to New Orleans on Friday for an assignment, he was the only passenger on the 76-seat jet.

titulo: U.S. GDP will contract 30% in second quarter, 5% in 2020: PIMCO
resumo: (This April 8 story corrects to clarify that 30% contraction is the annualized rate)

titulo: After pandemic, Fed policymakers see slow U.S. recovery
resumo: The U.S. economy is set for a deep slide in coming months with the coronavirus forcing businesses to shut and putting millions out of work, but Federal Reserve policymakers are also warning of a slugging recovery once the pandemic subsides.

titulo: Fashion giant Inditex working at reduced capacity despite loosened Spanish lockdown
resumo: Employees at global fashion giant Inditex's 10 logistics centres in Spain - from where it sends garments to its stores worldwide - returned to work on Monday but only to less than half their normal levels of activity, workers said.

titulo: Athene pays $45 million fine in first settlement of New York pension transfer probe
resumo: Athene Holding Ltd agreed on Monday to pay a $45 million civil fine to settle charges it conducted insurance business in New York without a license, the first settlement in a probe into an industry where life insurers take over corporate pension plans.

titulo: McDonald's faces class action over 'pervasive sexual harassment'
resumo: McDonald's Corp has been hit with a class action lawsuit accusing it of subjecting female employees in its corporate-owned fast-food restaurants in Florida to widespread sexual harassment.

titulo: Ford making masks to protect workers against COVID-19
resumo: Ford Motor Co said on Monday it is producing face masks at a Michigan plant to protect its workers from the coronavirus pandemic and is seeking to have those masks certified for medical use.

titulo: Russia hails oil deal it says will save millions of U.S.jobs
resumo: The global oil production deal clinched at the weekend will help  establish a price floor and save millions of jobs in the United States, senior Russian officials said on Monday.

titulo: GE offers to buy back at least $9 billion in debt, plans new longer-maturities issue
resumo: General Electric Co  offered on Monday to buy back at least $9 billion in debt as part of a plan to cut its financial risk and manage liquidity in response to the downturn in business caused by the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Explainer: Big cuts in oil production from OPEC and others

titulo: Dish Network cuts jobs, re-evaluates business in coronavirus fallout
resumo: Dish Network Corp  has cut staff and is re-evaluating  its business to cope better with the fallout from the coronavirus pandemic, the U.S. satellite TV provider told Reuters, without disclosing the number of employees laid off.

titulo: Volkswagen offers cars for relief efforts in U.S. virus-hit regions
resumo: Germany's Volkswagen AG  said on Monday its U.S. dealers would provide cars for free delivery of essential supplies to people in need during the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Aurora Cannabis falls on capital raise, reverse stock split
resumo: Aurora Cannabis Inc's   U.S.-listed shares fell nearly 14% in morning trade on Monday after the Canadian pot producer renewed an at-the-market offering under which it can raise as much as $350 million in equity capital.

titulo: Ford expects coronavirus shutdown to cause $600 million quarterly loss

titulo: Performance Food furloughs 3,000 employees as coronavirus takes toll

resumo: (This April 9 story corrects start date of production shutdown to March 23, with furloughs starting on March 30.)

resumo: A figure of 6 billion euros ($6.5 billion) in loans to help Air France KLM  amid the coronavirus crisis is "not unrealistic", French junior transport minister Jean-Baptiste Djebbari said on Wednesday.

titulo: French carmaker PSA sets up fund for staff and new health check measures
resumo: French carmaker and Peugeot owner PSA  announced on Wednesday the creation of a solidarity fund to help staff affected by the closures of plants due to the COVID-19 outbreak, and new steps to protect the health of its workers.

titulo: UK fashion retailer ASOS raises 247 million pounds in placing
resumo: British online fashion retailer ASOS Plc  said it had raised 247 million pounds ($304 million) via a placing, to help it shore up its finances in case of a prolonged business downturn from the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Jack Dorsey pledges $1 billion of his Square stake for COVID-19 relief efforts
resumo: Twitter Inc  Chief Executive Officer Jack Dorsey on Tuesday pledged $1 billion of his stake in Square Inc , the payments processor that he co-founded and heads, to help fund relief efforts related to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Amazon to suspend delivery service competing with UPS, FedEx
resumo: Amazon.com Inc  will suspend a delivery service that aims to compete with UPS  and FedEx  in the United States.

titulo: U.S. oil state senators to talk crude markets with Saudi officials Saturday: source
resumo: Republican U.S. senators who have introduced a bill that would remove U.S. defense systems and troops in Saudi Arabia unless it cuts oil output will hold a call with the kingdom's officials on Saturday, a source familiar with the planning said on Tuesday.

titulo: Australia's Treasury Wine, to cope with virus, weighs Penfolds demerger, U.S. cutback
resumo: Australia's Treasury Wine Estates Ltd  said it may spin off the prestigious Penfolds label and shrink its low-end "commercial" division in the United States as it seeks ways to grow profit amid upheaval brought by the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Nissan's China sales drop 44.9% in March due to virus
resumo: Japanese automaker Nissan  said on Wednesday its sales in China fell 44.9% from a year earlier to 73,297 units in March, as the coronavirus epidemic continues to hit the world's biggest car market.

titulo: U.S. pushes back on call by OPEC+ to join big oil output cuts
resumo: Saudi Arabia, Russia and allied oil producers will agree to deep cuts to their crude output at talks this week only if the United States and several others join in with curbs to help prop up prices that have been hammered by the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Volkswagen Mexico says employee died in potential case of COVID-19
resumo: Volkswagen Mexico issued a statement saying one of its employees in the country had died in a potential case of coronavirus.

titulo: Airbnb's new $1 billion investment comes at lower valuation: sources
resumo: Airbnb Inc's new $1 billion investment from private equity firms Silver Lake and Sixth Street Partners has terms that imply a reduced valuation of the home rental company, people familiar with the matter said on Tuesday.

titulo: Wynn, NCR deals a sign of yet more risk returning to bond market
resumo: U.S. casino operator Wynn Resorts Ltd  and automated teller machines maker NCR Corp  jointly raised $1 billion on Tuesday, in the first unsecured junk-rated bond offerings since the market was roiled by the coronavirus outbreak at the start of March.

titulo: Boeing making new 737 MAX software updates to address computer issue
resumo: Boeing Co  said late on Tuesday it will make two new software updates to the 737 MAX's flight control computer as it works to win regulatory approval to resume flights after the jet was grounded following two fatal crashes in five months.

titulo: Levi's coming back to life in China as stores reopen, online sales rise
resumo: Levi Strauss & Co  said on Tuesday a majority of its stores in China, where the coronavirus outbreak first emerged in December, were open with sales recovering on a weekly basis and digital sales rising last month.

titulo: U.S. Treasury talks continue on $25 billion in aviation grants
resumo: Talks between U.S. airlines and the Treasury Department over  how to award $25 billion in cash grants earmarked for payroll assistance due to the coronavirus outbreak were set to continue into the evening Tuesday, people briefed on the matter said.

titulo: KKR sets up $50 million international coronavirus relief fund
resumo: KKR & Co Inc  has established a $50 million global relief fund to help people affected by the new coronavirus, according to a letter the U.S. private equity firm sent to its investors on Tuesday.

titulo: WeWork sues SoftBank for dropping $3 billion tender offer
resumo: A WeWork board committee that negotiated a $3 billion tender offer with SoftBank Group sued the Japanese conglomerate on Tuesday for abandoning the deal, accusing it of succumbing to "buyer's remorse" amid the novel coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Amazon testing disinfectant fog at New York warehouse after coronavirus protests
resumo: Amazon.com Inc is testing the use of disinfectant fog at a warehouse in Staten Island, New York starting on Tuesday, the company told Reuters, following worker protests over the risk of coronavirus infection at the site.

titulo: Mnuchin seeks additional $250 billion in small business relief from Congress by Friday
resumo: Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin said on Tuesday he wants Congress to approve an additional $250 billion for a small business relief program by Friday.

titulo: Trump says $70 billion in coronavirus rescue loans authorized. But where's the cash?
resumo: Roughly $70 billion of a $350 billion pot of loans to cover the payrolls of ailing small businesses have been originated by U.S. lenders in recent days, President Donald Trump said on Tuesday, adding that that money was "essentially loaned."

titulo: Refunds row escalates as airlines warn millions of jobs at risk
resumo: Global airlines warned that 25 million jobs across the world could be at risk from the coronavirus air travel downturn, and held out against offering refunds to passengers as cash runs out.

titulo: Airlines cannot afford to refund cancelled tickets: IATA
resumo: Global airlines cannot afford to refund cancelled flights because of the coronavirus crisis, said the head of the industry's representative body IATA, and carriers are issuing vouchers instead as they conserve cash to survive.

titulo: U.S. SEC issues compliance focus areas for brokers before new rules take effect
resumo: The head of the examination office at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Tuesday published details about the expected scope and content for compliance with a new rule requiring broker-dealers and investment advisers to disclose potential conflicts of interest.

titulo: Oil drop saps gains as Wall Street fades late
resumo: Wall Street fell on Tuesday, as a drop in oil prices steepened in the latter stages of the session and erased early gains built on tentative signs that coronavirus outbreaks in some of the biggest U.S. hot spots may be leveling.

titulo: U.S. economy will eventually reopen but with big changes: Kudlow
resumo: The Trump administration is aiming to reopen the U.S. economy when the nation's top health experts give the go-ahead, but Americans' lives will be drastically different, White House economic adviser Larry Kudlow said on Tuesday.

titulo: Former Fed chief Bernanke sees bad year, no quick recovery
resumo: The U.S. economy could shrink 30% or more this quarter as stay-at-home orders aimed at slowing the coronavirus outbreak choke off business, and it could be a couple of years before the economy regains its footing, former Federal Reserve Chair Ben Bernanke said on Tuesday.

titulo: Wall Street volatility raises fears of another selloff
resumo: The evaporation of a rally on Wall Street in the closing minutes of Tuesday's session shows that many investors fear the U.S. stock market is in danger of a renewed tumble due to uncertainty surrounding  the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Jobless Americans to see extra payments as soon as this week
resumo: Americans who have lost their jobs due to the coronavirus outbreak will start getting enhanced jobless benefits as soon as this week as states deploy hundreds of billions of dollars in federal aid, state officials said on Tuesday.

titulo: Oil drops on growing crude glut, doubts over output cuts
resumo: Oil slumped on Tuesday in the face of swelling crude supplies and weak fuel demand due to the coronavirus pandemic, while investors also grew cautious over expectations that the world's biggest producers would quickly agree on output cuts.

titulo: U.S. Treasury liquidity on the mend, but without Fed remains fragile
resumo: Some measures of liquidity in the $17 trillion U.S. Treasury market are almost back to normal, thanks to the Federal Reserve's giant purchases, after drying up two weeks ago at the height of the coronavirus panic, but trading conditions remain challenging.

titulo: Exxon lops 30% off 2020 spending, deeper and later than rivals
resumo: Exxon Mobil Corp  on Tuesday throttled back investment in shale, natural gas and deep water production, cutting planned capital spending by 30% this year as the coronavirus pandemic saps energy demand and oil prices tumble.

titulo: Exxon could delay third Guyana project as government review drags on
resumo: Exxon Mobil Corp  said on Tuesday that production at the Payara project, its third development in Guyana, could be postponed due to delays in government approvals as the company scales back spending elsewhere due to the crude price crash.

titulo: BlackRock's Aladdin investment management platform to be hosted on Microsoft's cloud
resumo: BlackRock Inc, the world's largest asset manager, has partnered with Microsoft Corp to have its Aladdin investment and risk management system hosted on the Microsoft Azure cloud platform, the companies said on Tuesday.

titulo: Investors await data on coronavirus drugs as market rally builds
resumo: Clinical data on potential treatments for the new coronavirus could help sustain a market bounce that has buoyed stocks after last month's plunge, as investors look for signs that authorities may be able to stabilize the pandemic.

titulo: Small U.S. businesses were already struggling. Then coronavirus hit
resumo: Many small businesses were struggling with funding shortfalls and financial challenges even before the coronavirus pandemic hit, leaving them with little cash on hand to weather the slowdown caused by the virus, according to data released by the Federal Reserve on Tuesday.

titulo: U.S. banking regulators encourage 'prudent efforts' by banks to help borrowers
resumo: U.S. banks should feel welcome to pursue "prudent efforts" to help borrowers impacted by the coronavirus pandemic, according to a new joint statement issued by banking regulators Tuesday.

titulo: Mexico asks U.S., Canada to grant automakers transition for USMCA rules
resumo: Mexico's government on Tuesday asked the United States and Canada to grant its automotive industry extra time to adapt its supply chains as the deadline for implementing a new North American trade deal approaches.

titulo: Hopes of progress against virus lift stocks; dollar falls
resumo: World stock markets posted sharp gains on Tuesday as signs of progress in curbing the spread of the novel coronavirus in both Europe and the United States fueled investors' appetite for risk.

titulo: Netflix rolls out more features for parental control globally
resumo: Netflix Inc on Tuesday rolled out a number of features for parental control on its streaming platform, including options to filter out select titles and review what children are watching.

titulo: United Airlines drastically cuts California flights to match demand
resumo: United Airlines Holdings Inc said on Tuesday it was temporarily, but significantly, reducing daily flying in and out of Los Angeles and San Francisco airports to better match demand that has dropped due to the new coronavirus.

titulo: Goldman Sachs must face class action over conflicts of interest, risky mortgages
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc must face a shareholder class action accusing the bank of hiding conflicts of interest, including behind-the-scenes dealings with a prominent hedge fund manager, when creating risky subprime securities before the 2008 financial crisis.

titulo: Facebook launches new chat app for couples
resumo: Facebook Inc on Tuesday launched a new messaging app for couples called "Tuned" that allows them to chat, share photos and music as well as have a timeline of shared memories.

titulo: U.S. SEC's Clayton says companies seeking bailouts must disclose plans, communicate with investors
resumo: The head of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) said on Tuesday that companies in discussions about bailouts resulting from the economic impact of the coronavirus outbreak should disclose to investors where they stand.

titulo: U.S. treasury secretary to seek additional $250 billion for small business
resumo: U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin said on Tuesday that he would seek an additional $250 billion to support small businesses hurt by the widespread economic slowdown.

titulo: TJX to furlough most of about 286,000 staff as stores stay shut amid pandemic
resumo: Off-price retailer TJX Companies Inc  said on Tuesday it would furlough most of the employees at its stores and distribution centers, as outlets remain closed to curb the spread of the coronavirus.

titulo: Layoffs, closures to wipe out 6.7% of working hours worldwide in second quarter: ILO
resumo: Workplace disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic are expected to wipe out labor equivalent to the effort of 195 million full-time workers, or 6.7% of hours clocked worldwide, in the second quarter of this year, the International Labour Organization (ILO) said on Tuesday.

titulo: Colombian businesses call for more aid after quarantine extension
resumo: Colombian businesses on Tuesday called on the government to expand measures to help companies weather coronavirus, after President Ivan Duque extended a nationwide lockdown by two weeks.

titulo: OPEC+ needs to agree output numbers before meeting: Iran oil minister
resumo: There needs to be an agreement on oil production numbers before any future meeting between OPEC and non-OPEC members, Iran's Oil Minister Bijan Zanganeh tweeted on Tuesday.

titulo: Lufthansa to discontinue Germanwings in sweeping restructuring
resumo: Lufthansa  will close its Germanwings low-cost airline as part of a broader overhaul including capacity cuts across the group, it said on Tuesday as it warned it could take years for the industry to recover from the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Online meal delivery firms knocked off course by coronavirus crisis
resumo: The lockdown of millions of people at home across the globe due to the coronavirus should have been the perfect recipe for success for the burgeoning online meal delivery market.

titulo: Exclusive: U.S. grants GE license to sell engines for China's new airplane
resumo: The Trump administration on Tuesday granted a license to General Electric Co  to supply engines for China's new COMAC C919 passenger jet, a spokeswoman for the company said.

titulo: AT&T takes on $5.5 billion loan to boost 'financial flexibility'
resumo: AT&T Inc.  on Tuesday announced a $5.5 billion term-loan agreement, which it said would give the company "financial flexibility" during the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Side effects: Fuel demand crash shuts U.S. ethanol plants, meatpackers lack refrigerant
resumo: A slew of U.S. ethanol plants have shut down as fuel demand has collapsed during the coronavirus outbreak, and meatpackers have been hit by a worrying side-effect: less carbon dioxide is now available to chill beef, poultry and pork.

titulo: Huawei laments U.S. plans to crimp its global chip supply
resumo: A Huawei Technologies Co Ltd [HWT.UL] executive on Tuesday described as "unfortunate" plans by the U.S. government to curb the Chinese telecom equipment maker's access to global chip supplies.

titulo: Southwest building June flight schedule to reflect 'different phases of recovery'
resumo: Southwest Airlines Co  said on Tuesday it would publish this week a new flight schedule for June 6 to June 27 with overall flight activity dropping by about 50% while taking into account "different phases of the recovery anticipated ahead."

titulo: Honda, Nissan furlough U.S. workers as auto demand plummets
resumo: Honda Motor Co  and Nissan Motor Co  on Tuesday said they had furloughed thousands of workers at their U.S. operations as the coronavirus pandemic slashes demand for cars in the country.

titulo: Cineworld suspends dividend, defers pay to cushion virus hit; shares soar
resumo: Cineworld's  shares soared 36% on Tuesday after it suspended its dividend and said it was in talks with landlords, film studios and major suppliers to offset the impact of the closure of all its 787 cinemas due to the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Japan's SoftBank to invest $48 million in Brazil pet products site Petlove
resumo: Japan's SoftBank Group Corp  reached an agreement to invest 250 million reais ($48 million) in pet products online retailer Petlove, the companies said in a statement on Tuesday.

titulo: In a first, China knocks U.S. from top spot in global patent race
resumo: China was the biggest source of applications for international patents in the world last year, pushing the United States out of the top spot it has held since the global system was set up more than 40 years ago, the U.N. patent agency said on Tuesday.

titulo: Graphic: Oil majors cut 2020 spending by 22% after prices slump
resumo: The world's biggest oil and gas companies are cutting spending this year following a collapse in oil prices driven by a slump in demand because of the coronavirus crisis and a price war between top exporters Saudi Arabia and Russia.

titulo: Macy's finance chief Paula Price to step down in May
resumo: Department store chain operator Macy's Inc  said on Tuesday Chief Financial Officer Paula Price would step down from the role next month after nearly two years on the job.

titulo: Global health fund calls for $8 billion to begin COVID-19 exit strategy
resumo: A leading global health fund has asked international businesses and governments to provide $8 billion to support development and production of COVID-19 tests, drugs and vaccines.

titulo: Samsung first-quarter beats estimates; chips likely to prop up virus-hit second-quarter
resumo: Samsung Electronics Co Ltd  said on Tuesday its first-quarter operating profit likely managed to rise slightly from a slump a year earlier, as solid chip sales helped cushion the blow from the coronavirus pandemic on smartphones and TVs.

titulo: VW plans to partially reopen plant in Spain's Navarra on April 20
resumo: German carmaker Volkswagen  hopes to partially reopen its plant in Spain's Navarra region on April 20 after its closure in mid-March due to the coronavirus outbreak, a spokesman for the plant said on Tuesday.

titulo: Size of OPEC+ oil cuts will depend on how much others cut: source
resumo: Any final agreement for how much OPEC and its allies, a group know as OPEC+, will cut during their talks on Thursday would depend on the volumes that other producers such as the United States, Canada and Brazil are willing to reduce, an OPEC source said on Tuesday.

titulo: Demand for Luckin app surges as Chinese rush to drink up after admission of fraud
resumo: Luckin Coffee's app has rocketed to become the second most popular in China as consumers rushed to claim a free drink that it has long offered for downloading, worried the chain might collapse after it said much of its sales last year were fabricated.

titulo: As coronavirus hits tourism, Wizz Air finds new role
resumo: Hungarian low-cost airline Wizz Air  is repatriating citizens and transporting medical equipment for east European governments, in a move coupling one-off charter traffic with savvy government relations amid the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: France's ADP loses 3 million euros a day in sales due to coronavirus - CEO
resumo: French airports operator ADP is losing more than 3 million euros  ($3.25 million) in sales a day due to the coronavirus crisis, Chief Executive Augustin de Romanet said on Tuesday.

titulo: Asian companies pose lower dividend risk due to stronger finances
resumo: Asian firms appear better equipped to pay stable dividends compared with their western counterparts that are constrained by highly levered balance-sheets and a need to preserve cash during the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Employees worried about Valero response to virus cases at Texas refinery: sources
resumo: Employees at Valero Energy Corp's Port Arthur, Texas, refinery expressed worries about the company's slow response to keep the coronavirus from spreading there after two workers tested positive, four people familiar with the matter said.

titulo: Outbreak shows signs of leveling off in New York, New Jersey, but vigilance urged
resumo: The governors of New York, New Jersey and Louisiana pointed to tentative signs on Monday that the coronavirus outbreak may be starting to plateau in their states but warned against complacency as the death toll nationwide approached 11,000.

titulo: France's Thales withdraws final dividend, suspends outlook
resumo: France's Thales  on Tuesday became the latest major company to slash its dividend, suspend profit guidance and top up liquidity in response to the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: China's Ucar, in which Luckin chairman is top shareholder, halts shares
resumo: China's Ucar Inc, in which Luckin Coffee  Chairman Charles Zhengyao Lu is the biggest shareholder, suspended trading of its shares on Tuesday after regulators questioned the car service provider about how the alleged fraud at Luckin would impact it.

titulo: Toshiba should sell stake in ex-chip unit at IPO, says HK activist fund in letter
resumo: A Hong Kong-based activist investor in Toshiba Corp  has asked the Japanese conglomerate to sell its entire stake in Kioxia Holdings when the flash memory chip firm is listed in an IPO that could raise up to $32 billion, according to a letter reviewed by Reuters.

titulo: Cemex resumes Mexico operations after brief pause due to coronavirus
resumo: Mexican cement company Cemex said on Monday it would resume operations in the country, hours after announcing a pause, as fresh guidelines from the government permitted such a move.

titulo: UPS employee dies of COVID-19 infection in Kentucky
resumo: A United Parcel Service Inc worker employed in Kentucky died over the weekend from a COVID-19 infection, Gov. Andy Beshear said at a public health briefing on Monday.

titulo: Hit by coronavirus, Fiat Chrysler, Peugeot seek to boost cash before merger
resumo: With their sales hammered by the coronavirus outbreak, car makers Fiat Chrysler and Peugeot's owner PSA have postponed their shareholder meetings and are looking at ways to boost cash reserves ahead of their planned merger.

titulo: NYSE in talks with SEC to ease listing rules during coronavirus volatility
resumo: The New York Stock Exchange said on Monday it was in talks with regulators to temporarily ease its listing requirements to take the strain off companies that may have fallen out of compliance due to the market rout spurred by the coronavirus.

titulo: Fed says it will provide financing against new U.S. 'payroll protection' loans
resumo: The Federal Reserve on Monday moved to bolster a new small-business lending program by allowing banks to turn those loans over to the U.S. central bank for cash, easing concerns among banks about getting stuck holding the low interest loans.

titulo: Wall Street soars on hopes of slowing coronavirus deaths
resumo: U.S. stocks rocketed higher on Monday, with each of the major indexes rallying at least 7%, after a fall in the daily death toll in New York, the country's biggest coronavirus hot spot, fueled optimism a leveling off of the pandemic was on the horizon.

titulo: Trump says OPEC has not asked him for a U.S. oil production cut
resumo: President Donald Trump said on Monday that OPEC had not pressed him to ask U.S. oil producers to reduce their output to support global prices, which have been hard-hit by the economic fallout of the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Alaska warned U.S. government its largest regional airline was on verge of collapse: letter
resumo: Alaska's congressional delegation warned U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin last week that many of its airlines, including its largest regional carrier, RavnAir Group, were on the verge of collapse, according to a letter seen by Reuters.

titulo: Airbnb secures $1 billion investment from Silver Lake, Sixth Street
resumo: Airbnb said on Monday private equity firms Silver Lake and Sixth Street Partners will invest $1 billion in the U.S. home rental company, bolstering its finances at a time when the coronavirus outbreak has roiled the travel and leisure industry.

titulo: Allstate to return $600 million in auto premiums as coronavirus cuts driving
resumo: Allstate Corp , one of the largest U.S. auto insurers, said on Monday it would return more than $600 million in premiums to customers as many Americans drive less due to stay-at-home orders aimed at curbing the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Boeing suspending 787 production at South Carolina plant
resumo: Boeing Co  said on Monday it would suspend production of its 787 airplane at its facilities in South Carolina amid the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: White House considering U.S. Treasury coronavirus bond: Kudlow
resumo: White House advisers have been discussing the possibility of a coronavirus-related U.S. Treasury bond, President Donald Trump's economic adviser Larry Kudlow said on Monday.

titulo: OPEC+ likely to agree to cut production if U.S. joins effort: sources
resumo: Major oil producers including Saudi Arabia and Russia are likely to agree to cut production at a Thursday meeting but only if the United States joins the effort, aimed at coping with the disastrous effect of the coronavirus on fuel demand, three OPEC+ sources told Reuters on Monday.

titulo: World stocks jump on less dire virus data, oil falls after OPEC+ delay
resumo: World stock markets jumped on Monday, encouraged by slowdowns in coronavirus-related deaths and new cases in some global hot spots, while oil prices tumbled again due to a delay in talks between Saudi Arabia and Russia to cut supply.

titulo: Honda, Fiat Chrysler aim to restart U.S., Canada auto production in May
resumo: Honda Motor Co  and Fiat Chrysler Automobiles NV said on Monday they hope to restart U.S. and Canadian auto production in May amid the ongoing coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Lufthansa to discuss permanently grounding Germanwings: sources
resumo: Lufthansa  will discuss permanently grounding its Germanwings low-cost airline unit at a management board meeting on Tuesday, two sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

titulo: Coronavirus muddies U.S. economic data as business closures push down response rates
resumo: A near total closure of U.S. businesses as authorities try to control the spread of the novel coronavirus could make U.S. economic data unreliable in the coming months and harder to get a clearer picture of the severity of the recession caused by the virus.

titulo: Oil falls after Saudi Arabia, Russia delay meeting, Cushing stockpiles soar
resumo: Oil prices slumped on Monday, pulling back from last week's gains after Saudi Arabia and Russia delayed a meeting of oil producers aimed at resolving growing worldwide oversupply as the coronavirus pandemic pummels demand.

titulo: CVS ramps up drive-through coronavirus testing sites with faster kits
resumo: CVS Health Corp , called on by the Trump administration last month to help test Americans for infections from the new coronavirus, said it was launching two offsite testing locations with Abbott Laboratories'  faster diagnostic kit and would be able to handle 1,000 tests per day.

titulo: Tiger at New York's Bronx Zoo tests positive for coronavirus
resumo: A tiger at the Bronx Zoo in New York City has tested positive for the respiratory disease caused by the novel coronavirus, in the first known case of a human infecting an animal and making it sick, the zoo's chief veterinarian said on Sunday.

titulo: Airbus announces additional production pauses
resumo: Airbus  said on Monday it had decided additional production pauses, in Germany and in the United States, in response to an industry-wide slowdown triggered by the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Capri furloughs all North America retail staff, sees stores opening around June 1
resumo: Capri Holdings Ltd  will furlough all its 7,000 retail staff in North America due to the coronavirus outbreak and expects to reopen stores around June 1, the owner of Michael Kors and Versace brands said on Monday.

titulo: Zoom shares slip over security concerns, rising competition
resumo: Shares of Zoom Video Communications Inc  fell 8% on Monday, adding to their sharp declines in the past few days, as the video conferencing app battles privacy concerns and increased competition from deep-pocketed rivals.

titulo: Alaska Air expects to lower April and May capacity by 80%
resumo: The U.S. carrier had earlier planned to reduce its April capacity by at least 10% and May capacity by at least 15%.

titulo: Canada, U.S. farms face crop losses due to foreign worker delays
resumo: Mandatory coronavirus quarantines of seasonal foreign workers in Canada could hurt that country's fruit and vegetable output this year, and travel problems related to the pandemic could also leave U.S. farmers with fewer workers than usual.

titulo: Exclusive: Qatar Petroleum - coronavirus turmoil will not stop LNG expansion abroad
resumo: Qatar Petroleum (QP) is pressing ahead with foreign as well as domestic expansion despite the global market turmoil caused by the coronavirus pandemic, its chief executive told Reuters on Monday.

titulo: Luckin Coffee chairman defaults on loan, surrenders company shares
resumo: Luckin Coffee  Chairman Charles Zhengyao Lu and Chief Executive Jenny Zhiya Qian have handed over shares in the embattled Chinese coffee chain to lenders after a company controlled by Lu's family defaulted on a $518 million margin loan, one of the banks said on Monday.

titulo: GSK to partner with Vir for potential COVID-19 treatments, invest $250 million
resumo: British drugmaker GlaxoSmithKline will invest $250 million in Vir Biotechnology Inc and collaborate to develop potential antibody treatments for COVID-19, the disease caused by the new coronavirus, the companies said on Monday.

titulo: Exclusive: Qatar Petroleum not scaling down LNG expansion despite delay in bids - CEO
resumo: Qatar Petroleum will postpone the start of production from its new gas facilities to 2025 due to a delay in the bidding process, but is not downsizing the world's largest liquefied natural gas project despite concerns of a mounting glut, its chief executive told Reuters.

titulo: JPMorgan CEO Dimon calls 'bad recession', mulls suspending 2020 dividend
resumo: JPMorgan Chase & Co's top boss, Jamie Dimon , on Monday said he sees a "bad recession" in 2020, and that the largest U.S. bank could suspend its dividend if the coronavirus crisis deepens.

titulo: Peugeot parent secures further 3 billion euros of loans
resumo: Peugeot-owner PSA  said on Monday it had secured a further 3 billion euros ($3.3 billion) of loans, strengthening its financial position in the wake of the hit to the global automotive industry from the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: BMW sees demand falling further after first quarter sales plunge due to coronavirus
resumo: BMW  is expecting a further decline in global demand as the coronavirus outbreak takes its toll, a spokesman said on Monday, after the German carmaker reported a 20.6% drop in first quarter sales to 477,111 vehicles.

titulo: IBM taps former Bank of America CTO to oversee cloud business
resumo: International Business Machines Corp  on Monday named former Bank of America Corp  top technology executive Howard Boville head of its cloud business, as Arvind Krishna takes over from long-time chief Ginni Rometty.

titulo: Total among companies seeking force majeure on EDF contracts: sources
resumo: Energy major Total  is among several power suppliers that tried to declare force majeure on buying nuclear power from EDF, sources said, as the coronavirus outbreak has pushed prices in the French electricity market far below existing contracts.

titulo: As infections balloon, coronavirus squeezes Europe's armed forces
resumo: Military forces across Europe have scaled back operations and imposed stricter rules on personnel to try to stem the spread of the coronavirus among staff who often live and work in close quarters, making them more vulnerable to infection.

titulo: ILO annual gathering of labour ministers postponed for a year
resumo: An annual gathering of the world's labour ministers will not take place this year because of the coronavirus pandemic, but will convene in June 2021, the International Labour Organization (ILO) said on Monday.

titulo: Uber connects out-of-work U.S. ride-hail drivers to delivery, production jobs
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc  said on Monday its app will list job openings in the delivery, food production and grocery industry that its U.S. drivers can access during a slump in ride-hailing demand due to the coronavirus.

titulo: EasyJet founder steps up battle with management over Airbus order
resumo: Stelios Haji-Ioannou, easyJet's  founder and biggest shareholder, on Monday intensified his battle with the airline's management over a huge aircraft order with Airbus  which he says it should cancel.

titulo: EasyJet requests draw down of $500 million credit facility, explores more funding
resumo: EasyJet  said on Monday it has requested to fully draw down its $500 million credit facility, secured against aircraft assets, and will continue to explore funding options due to a possibly prolonged grounding of its jets following the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Norwegian Air's March traffic tumbles 60% amid virus lockdown
resumo: Norwegian Air's  passenger volume fell by 60% year-on-year in March as it grounded planes amid efforts to halt the spread of the new coronavirus, and will book a hedging loss of $102 million as the cost of fuel has plunged.

titulo: BT boss gives salary to health workers, lifts pay for key staff
resumo: The head of Britain's biggest telecoms firm BT  said he would donate his salary to health workers for at least six months and award a pay rise to his frontline staff who are maintaining broadband networks during the COVID-19 shutdown.

titulo: SeaWorld CEO steps down after just five months on the job
resumo: SeaWorld Entertainment Inc  said on Monday Sergio Rivera, the theme park operator's chief executive officer for just five months, has resigned due to disagreements with the board.

titulo: HSBC Hong Kong shareholders mull legal action over dividend suspension
