titulo: Tipping goes virtual as coronavirus decimates U.S. restaurant jobs
resumo: As restaurants and bars across the United States shut their doors and lay off their staff in the wake of the coronavirus outbreak, America’s famed service culture is moving online - with regulars paying their out-of-work waiters, favorite bar staff, and even total strangers via virtual tip jars.

titulo: Mnuchin: Negotiators 'very close' to deal on coronavirus stimulus
resumo: U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin said on Monday that Republican and Democratic senators and Trump administration negotiators were nearing a deal on a coronavirus stimulus bill.

titulo: Fast food closures sweep U.K., as even drive-thrus deemed unsafe
resumo: McDonald's Corp, Yum! Brands Inc's KFC fried chicken chain, Coca-Cola Co unit Costa Coffee and Subway sandwich shops were among restaurants that shuttered all locations in the United Kingdom and Ireland by the end of Monday to help slow the spread of the coronavirus.

titulo: Neiman Marcus in bankruptcy talks with lenders: Bloomberg Law
resumo: Luxury retailer Neiman Marcus Group Inc is in talks with lenders to file for bankruptcy as it struggles to ease its $4.3 billion debt load, Bloomberg reported on Monday, citing people familiar with the matter.

titulo: Trump says won't allow long-lasting damage to economy from virus
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday he will not allow the coronavirus to do long-lasting damage to the U.S. economy and that he would consider how to move forward after a 15-day shutdown ends next week.

titulo: As coronavirus fuels meat demand, processors raise pay for North American farmers, workers
resumo: North American meat processors said on Monday they would pay extra money to farmers and slaughterhouse workers as the companies scramble to meet surging demand from consumers unnerved by the spread of the new coronavirus.

titulo: Mondelez raises hourly wage, hands out weekly bonus as virus worries fuel demand
resumo: Mondelez International Inc  on Monday said it would increase hourly wage by $2 and pay a $125 weekly bonus for its sales representatives as it rushes to meet a surge in demand for its packaged food due to the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Bombardier to suspend business jet production in Canada over coronavirus: source
resumo: Bombardier  will suspend Canadian production of its corporate jets to comply with restrictions imposed by provincial governments aimed at curbing the spread of the coronavirus, a source familiar with the matter said on Monday.

titulo: Fed's money market move lifts Northern Trust fund above key threshold
resumo: Liquidity at a $2.2 billion prime money-market fund run by Northern Trust Corp fell below the key 30% U.S. regulatory threshold twice last week, but rebounded above that level after the U.S. Federal Reserve shored up the industry.

titulo: U.S. Fed aims 'bazooka' to backstop coronavirus-hit economy
resumo: The U.S. Federal Reserve on Monday rolled out an extraordinary array of programs to backstop an economy reeling from sweeping restrictions on commerce that scientists say are needed to slow the coronavirus pandemic and ultimately keep more people safe.

titulo: Nordstrom joins Macy's in suspending dividend amid coronavirus
resumo: Nordstrom Inc  said on Monday it would suspend its quarterly cash dividend and share repurchases as it looks to weather the impact from the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Coronavirus drags car dealers into digital commerce
resumo: Auto retailers have been slow to embrace e-commerce, but the coronavirus pandemic is changing that.

titulo: Fed's big move could help U.S. Treasury liquidity, but effects may not last long
resumo: The Federal Reserve's unparalleled steps to directly backstop the U.S. economy and its willingness to purchase vast sums of U.S. government debt, mortgage-backed securities and even corporate bonds could help boost depleted liquidity in the $17-trillion Treasury market.

titulo: Historic Fed boost fails to stop Wall Street's coronavirus-driven sell-off
resumo: Wall Street's slide deepened on Monday as an unprecedented moves by the U.S. Federal Reserve to shore up credit across the economy proved insufficient to sooth investors' fears about the swiftly-spreading coronavirus.

titulo: Twitter ad sales hit by coronavirus but active users soar
resumo: Twitter Inc  pulled its first-quarter revenue outlook and forecast an operating loss on Monday as the coronavirus outbreak crimped ad sales, but said the pandemic boosted the number of active users on the microblogging platform.

titulo: GM accelerates 'Project V' to build ventilators in Indiana
resumo: General Motors Co  and medical equipment maker Ventec are speeding up efforts under a partnership code-named "Project V" to build ventilators at a GM plant in Kokomo, Indiana, to help combat the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: U.S. trade regulators approve tariff exemption for Apple Watches
resumo: U.S. trade regulators have approved Apple Inc's  request to waive tariffs on China-made Apple Watches, the Office of the United States Trade Representative said in a letter dated Friday.

titulo: What's in the nearly $2 trillion U.S. Senate coronavirus stimulus
resumo: The U.S. Senate on Monday was negotiating a nearly $2 trillion emergency bill that aims to stem some of the economic toll of the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Uber appoints former Mattel CEO Robert Eckert to board
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc  said on Monday it had appointed consumer industry veteran Robert Eckert to its board as an independent director.

titulo: With latest moves, Fed becomes creditor in chief for U.S. business
resumo: In undertaking what will undoubtedly be its largest rescue effort ever, the Federal Reserve on Monday announced programs that represent a never-before-seen intervention by the central bank into the heart of the "real" American economy.

titulo: Explainer: How the Fed's latest move will get money to Main Street
resumo: The U.S. Federal Reserve said on Monday it would backstop an unprecedented range of credit for households, small businesses and major employers in an attempt to stop the economy from sliding into depression as the coronavirus spreads.

titulo: Some U.S. wealth advisers tell clients to stay put as markets fall
resumo: The deepest U.S. stock market sell-off since the 2008 financial crisis is prompting some financial advisers to tell wealthy clients to hold steady or even increase their equity exposure if they have at least 10 years until retirement.

titulo: U.S. junk bonds fall despite unprecedented Fed stimulus
resumo: Prices of the riskiest U.S. corporate bonds fell on Monday as investors bet that the Federal Reserve's pledge to backstop an unprecedented range of debt would not materially change the outlook for less financially stable companies, particularly those at risk of default.

titulo: Market panic lingers despite unprecedented Fed support
resumo: Global equities slid further and safe-haven assets rose on Monday after a massive array of new programs from the U.S. Federal Reserve underscored the "severe" disruptions the coronavirus pandemic poses to a fast-weakening world economy.

titulo: U.S. prosecutors charge PDVSA contractor with money laundering
resumo: U.S. prosecutors have charged a Venezuelan oilfield contractor with money laundering for bribing officials at state oil company Petroleos de Venezuela to win overpriced contracts, and then seeking to hide the proceeds in the United States.

titulo: Airbus sees airlines seeking to defer or cancel orders
resumo: Airbus  said in a stock market filing on Monday that customers could seek to cancel or postpone delivery of airliners and helicopters as the coronavirus crisis continues to escalate.

titulo: Boeing suspending Washington state production due to coronavirus risks
resumo: Boeing Co  said on Monday it would halt production at its Washington state twin-aisle jetliner factory as a temporary measure to help fight the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Fed alone can’t stop coronavirus selloff, investors say, as stocks tumble again
resumo: The Federal Reserve's unprecedented stimulus measures did little to brighten Wall Street's immediate outlook on coronavirus-ravaged stocks, as concerns remained about the lack of a government stimulus package and the ability to contain the pandemic.

titulo: PG&E to plead guilty to 84 involuntary manslaughter counts over 2018 wildfire
resumo: Pacific Gas & Electric has agreed to plead guilty to 84 involuntary manslaughter counts in connection with the 2018 Camp Fire, the most destructive wildfire in California's history.

titulo: U.S. businesses see big hit from coronavirus, Fed survey shows
resumo: U.S. businesses expect a "very large and very negative impact" from the coronavirus pandemic and efforts to slow its spread, according to a survey published on Monday by the Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank.

titulo: GE's aviation unit to cut 10% of U.S. workforce: CEO
resumo: General Electric Co's  aviation unit will cut its U.S. workforce by about 10%, Chief Executive Officer Larry Culp said in a letter to staff on Monday, as a shattered airline industry sought state support to survive the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: U.S. banks hand out bonuses to staff as coronavirus crisis intensifies
resumo: Some U.S. banks on Monday offered employees one-time bonuses to help ease the financial burden of the coronavirus pandemic and as a reward for working through the crisis.

titulo: IMF sees pandemic causing global recession in 2020, recovery in 2021
resumo: The coronavirus pandemic will cause a global recession in 2020 that could be worse than the one triggered by the global financial crisis of 2008-2009, but world economic output should recover in 2021, the International Monetary Fund said on Monday.

titulo: Coronavirus grounds more flights, takes toll on jobs
resumo: Widening travel curbs to contain the spread of the coronavirus prompted more flight cancellations on Monday, with new restrictions spanning India, Australia, New Zealand, the United Arab Emirates (UAE), Hong Kong, Singapore and Taiwan.

titulo: Airlines ground flights, count mounting costs of the coronavirus shock
resumo: Airlines across the globe are feeling the pain as travel demand withers because of the coronavirus outbreak, scrapping flights and ditching financial forecasts.

titulo: Alitalia to relaunch with much smaller fleet: sources
resumo: The Italian government plans to take control of loss-making carrier Alitalia [CAITLA.UL] in a month or so, creating a new company that will start off with a much smaller fleet, union sources said on Monday.

titulo: North American millers, bakers scramble to satisfy bread-buying binge
resumo: North American flour mills and bakeries are rushing to boost production as the spread of the new coronavirus leads to stockpiling of staples like bread and pasta.

titulo: Western supply chains buckle as coronavirus lockdowns spread
resumo: Freight carriers are struggling to deliver goods by land, sea or air as the coronavirus pandemic forces Western governments to impose lockdowns, threatening supplies of vital products including medicines into the most affected areas, such as Italy.

titulo: Shell cuts 2020 spending by $5 billion, suspends share buyback
resumo: Royal Dutch Shell  will lower spending by $5 billion and suspended its vast $25 billion share buyback plan in an effort to weather the recent collapse in oil prices, it said on Monday.

titulo: SoftBank plans $41 billion of asset sales to expand buyback and cut debt
resumo: SoftBank Group Corp  plans to raise as much as $41 billion to buy back shares and reduce debt in an unprecedented move to restore investor confidence as a financial market rout pummels its shares and its portfolio companies.

titulo: Home work triggers demand jump for chips, laptops and network goods
resumo: With more employees working from home to help slow the spread of the coronavirus, demand is surging for laptops and network peripherals as well as components along the supply chain such as chips, as companies rush to build virtual offices.

titulo: Airbus adds 15 billion euro credit line, scraps dividend
resumo: Airbus  boosted its liquidity with a 15 billion euro  ($16 billion) expanded credit facility on Monday while suspending its 2020 outlook in response to the coronavirus crisis that has grounded much of the global airlines fleet.

titulo: Coronavirus fears spark surge in a volatility-focused ETN, but with a downside
resumo: A product that traders use to profit from surges in volatility has soared as coronavirus fears ravaged stocks, prompting concern about a plunge if the market's wild swings subside.

titulo: Instant View: Fed launches unprecedented range of credit support
resumo: The S&P 500  opened lower, reversing a brief bounce in U.S. stock futures after the U.S. Federal Reserve on Monday said it would backstop an unprecedented range of credit for households, small businesses and major employers to offset the "tremendous hardship," caused by coronavirus.

titulo: Central banks deploy record sums to break financial logjam, but may need more
resumo: Central banks have offered trillions of dollars of support to markets in recent days to keep them from freezing up, as investors worried about the economic damage from the coronavirus and made a chaotic dash for the exits.

titulo: Fed: New Main Street program meant to ensure all firms have access to credit
resumo: A Federal Reserve "Main Street" lending program still under development is meant to dovetail with proposals under consideration by Congress to make sure the full range of U.S. companies, from the smallest to the largest, have access to credit during the coronavirus health scare, the Fed said on Monday.

titulo: H&M scraps dividend due to coronavirus as all costs under review
resumo: Sweden's H&M , the world's second largest clothing retailer, said on Monday it had decided to scrap its proposed dividend due to the coronavirus outbreak, and that it was reviewing all parts of its operations, including all costs.

titulo: Walmart ups minimum wage in e-commerce warehouses by $2 as orders surge on virus worries
resumo: Walmart Inc said on Monday it has temporarily raised entry wages for workers in its e-commerce warehouses by $2, following similar moves by rivals, as it attempts to manage a shopping surge brought about by the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Ford decides to halt production in India, South Africa on coronavirus impact
resumo: Ford Motor Co  said on Monday it has decided to temporarily halt vehicle and engine production at its factories in India, South Africa, Thailand and Vietnam in response to the growing impact of the coronavirus.

titulo: Netflix to reduce traffic in Israel by 25% to help ISPs
resumo: Netflix said on Monday it would reduce network traffic in Israel over the next month following a government request to ease data congestion from people staying home due to the coronavirus.

titulo: Fiat Chrysler CEO says group to produce face masks in Asia: union representative
resumo: Fiat Chrysler Chief Executive Mike Manley told employees that the carmaker would help with the production of masks during the coronavirus emergency, a union representative said on Monday.

titulo: Virus hit UK companies told to delay financial statements
resumo: Companies should not publish preliminary financial statements for at least two weeks to better assess how the coronavirus epidemic is affecting their business, Britain's Financial Conduct Authority said on Saturday.

titulo: Factbox: UK companies delay results after regulators relax reporting rules
resumo: British companies on Monday rushed to heed unprecedented calls by regulators to mothball their results so they can better assess how the coronavirus pandemic is affecting their business.

titulo: Nestle CEO tells staff to get ready for coronavirus storm: memo
resumo: Food giant Nestle  told employees to prepare for difficult times ahead and make all the necessary efforts to supply customers with the food and beverages they need, Chief Executive Mark Schneider said in a memorandum seen by Reuters.

titulo: Factbox: Global oil, gas producers cut spending after crude price crash
resumo: Oil and gas companies are cutting spending plans in response to the new coronavirus and a push by Saudi Arabia and Russia to ramp up output. [O/R]

titulo: Factbox: SoftBank's key assets
resumo: SoftBank Group Corp  plans to raise up to 4.5 trillion yen ($41 billion) by selling or monetizing assets to buy back 2 trillion yen of its shares and reduce debt, it said on Monday.

titulo: Panasonic to suspend battery production at Tesla joint venture in Nevada due to coronavirus
resumo: (This story was refiled to say news was earlier reported by the Reno Gazette Journal)

titulo: Apple scraps curbs on online buyers of iPhones amid virus outbreak
resumo: Apple has dropped a two-device limit on online purchases of iPhones, a check of its web stores showed on Monday, just days after changing the checkout procedure amid a coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: Goldman Sachs sees 1% drop in global GDP due to 'coronacrisis'
resumo: Goldman Sachs said it expected global real gross domestic product to contract by about 1% in 2020, a sharper economic decline than in the year following the 2008 global financial crisis.

titulo: Hyundai Motor heir apparent buys $15 million shares in automaker, parts affiliate
resumo: Hyundai Motor Group's heir apparent Euisun Chung bought shares in automaker Hyundai Motor  and parts affiliate Hyundai Mobis  for a combined 19 billion won ($15 million), according to stock exchange filings.

titulo: Senate GOP virus bill aids U.S. transport sector; offers airlines loans, not cash
resumo: A Senate Republican coronavirus stimulus package would provide financial aid to U.S. airports, transit systems and passenger railroad Amtrak, but $50 billion for struggling U.S. airlines would be a loan, not a cash payout, the proposal showed.

titulo: Exxon notifies contractors, vendors of spending cuts over coronavirus
resumo: Exxon Mobil Corp  is notifying contractors and vendors of planned near-term cuts in capital and operating expenses over a coronavirus pandemic, and will announce the plans once they are final, company spokesman Jeremy Eikenberry said on Sunday.

titulo: Boeing worker at Everett plant dies from coronavirus: Seattle Times
resumo: A worker at Boeing Co's  Everett factory outside Seattle who had the coronavirus has died, the Seattle Times reported on Sunday, citing co-workers, a union official and a Facebook post by the man's brother.

titulo: Airbus says plants to reopen at slower production rate
resumo: Airbus  confirmed on Sunday it would resume only partial aircraft production when its French and Spanish factories reopen on Monday after a four-down shutdown to tackle health concerns over the coronavirus.

titulo: Occidental nears deal with activist Icahn on proxy battle: source
resumo: U.S. oil and gas producer Occidental Petroleum Corp  is nearing an agreement with activist investor Carl Icahn to halt a proxy battle in return for board seats, according to a person familiar with the matter.

titulo: Wall Street stares down another bleak week, with coronavirus risks amplified
resumo: Wall Street analysts and investment managers expect another rough week for U.S. markets due to coronavirus fears, with a wild open for futures on Sunday evening as lawmakers battled over an economic aid package in Washington.

titulo: WeWork board's special committee prepares for fight against SoftBank
resumo: The special committee of WeWork's board is preparing for a fight against Japan's SoftBank Group Corp  saying that SoftBank should complete its tender offer promised to the office sharing company's employees and shareholders.

titulo: Embraer puts Brazil-based workers on furlough to protect them from coronavirus
resumo: Brazilian planemaker Embraer  is putting all its Brazil-based employees who cannot work remotely on paid leave until March 31, the company said on Sunday, adding that only "a few essential operations" will be maintained.

titulo: McDonald's to temporarily close all UK, Ireland restaurants amid coronavirus outbreak
resumo: McDonald's Corp  said on Sunday it would temporarily close all its restaurants in the UK and Ireland by Monday evening because of the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Virgin companies to invest $250 million to save jobs after virus outbreak: Branson
resumo: British entrepreneur Richard Branson said on Sunday companies in his Virgin empire will be investing $250 million in coming weeks and months to save jobs from the impact of the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Coronavirus supplies donated by Alibaba's Ma arrive in Africa
resumo: A first planeload of protective and medical equipment donated to Africa by Chinese billionaire and Alibaba co-founder Jack Ma to help it fight the coronavirus arrived in the Ethiopian capital Addis Ababa on Sunday.

titulo: Facebook donates emergency reserve of 720,000 masks due to coronavirus outbreak
resumo: Facebook Inc  Chief Executive Mark Zuckerberg said on Sunday that the social media company has donated its emergency reserve of 720,000 masks to provide health workers with more protective gear.

titulo: Facebook to cut video streaming quality in virus-hit Europe
resumo: Facebook will downgrade video streaming quality on its platform and on Instagram in Europe, the latest U.S. tech giant to respond to an EU call to stave off internet gridlock as thousands work from home due to the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Emirates stops nearly all passenger flights, slashes wages
resumo: Emirates, one of the world's biggest long-haul airlines, said on Sunday it would stop nearly all passenger flights this week and cut staff wages by as much as half because of the coronavirus impact on travel demand.

titulo: Ford, GM, Tesla getting 'go ahead' to make ventilators: Trump
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump said on Sunday that U.S. automakers Ford Motor Co, General Motors Co and Tesla Inc had been given the green light to produce ventilators and other items needed during the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Coronavirus aid bill includes $3,000 for families, $4 trillion liquidity for Fed: Mnuchin
resumo: The coronavirus economic relief bill being finalized by the U.S. Congress will include a one-time $3,000 payment for families and allow the Federal Reserve to leverage up to $4 trillion of liquidity to support the nation's economy, U.S. Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin said on Sunday.

titulo: Fed's Bullard: Coronavirus shutdown not a recession but an investment in survival
resumo: In normal times massive unemployment and a collapse in economic output would be tragic.

titulo: Factbox: Latest on the spread of the coronavirus around the world
resumo: Coronavirus cases ballooned in Europe, prompting more lockdown measures to try to slow the spread. New Jersey's governor followed four other U.S. states that have imposed unprecedented restrictions. Australia announced it was shutting a large array of businesses from Monday.

titulo: Coronavirus deaths in Italy overtake China as economic damage mounts
resumo: The world's richest nations poured unprecedented aid into the  global economy on Thursday as coronavirus cases ballooned in the new epicentre Europe, with the number of deaths in Italy outstripping those in mainland China, where the virus originated.

titulo: Explainer: Hobbled IRS tax agency may need months to put cash into Americans' hands
resumo: In order to put recession-fighting checks into the hands of millions of Americans, President Donald Trump will rely on a tax agency that has fewer workers, a smaller budget, and the same 1960s-era computer systems it had the last time it was asked to do so.

titulo: Netflix to slash traffic across Europe to relieve virus strain on internet providers
resumo: Netflix Inc  said it will cut traffic by 25% on networks across Europe in a relief measure for internet service providers (ISPs) experiencing a surge in usage due to government "shelter in place" orders aimed at slowing the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Airlines appear to come up short in bid to win cash grants in rescue package
resumo: A last-ditch effort by the chief executives of major U.S. airlines to try to win cash grants to weather the coronavirus crisis looked to be unsuccessful, four congressional aides and airline officials said late Saturday.

titulo: U.S. Senate races to agree on massive coronavirus relief package
resumo: Republicans and Democrats in the U.S. Senate on Saturday scrambled to complete a deal on a $1 trillion-plus bill aimed at stemming the coronavirus pandemic's economic fallout for workers, industries and small businesses.

titulo: Amazon raises overtime pay for warehouse workers
resumo: Amazon.com Inc said on Saturday it is raising overtime pay for associates working in its U.S. warehouses as the world's largest online retailer tries to meet the rapidly growing demand for online shopping from consumers stuck at home during the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Musk, Medtronic discuss ventilators amid coronavirus outbreak
resumo: As the United States is combating a shortage of ventilators and respirators amid the coronavirus outbreak, Tesla Chief Executive Officer Elon Musk said he has had a discussion about ventilators with medical device maker Medtronic.

titulo: Volkswagen CEO warns factory closures may drag on for weeks
resumo: Volkswagen AG  CEO Herbert Diess on Saturday warned that the coronavirus crisis may force the company to keep its factories shut for longer than initially planned.

titulo: Shame on you, Britain tells coronavirus panic-buyers
resumo: Britain on Saturday told people who were panic-buying and hoarding food due to the coronavirus outbreak to calm down, pointing to a video on social media showing an exhausted nurse driven to tears by finding shelves bare after her shift.

titulo: Exclusive: Amazon.com to stop shipping non-essentials to consumers in Italy and France
resumo: Amazon.com will stop shipping non-essential products to consumers in Italy and France, the company said on Saturday, representing an escalation in the e-commerce giant's response in regions hard hit by the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Exclusive: Goldman injects $1 billion into own money-market funds after heavy withdrawals
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc  poured more than $1 billion into two of its prime money-market portfolios this week due to heavy investor withdrawals, according to a filing with the U.S. securities regulator.

titulo: Emirates stops flights to New York and other cities as virus crushes demand
resumo: Emirates is suspending flights to cities including New York, Delhi and Melbourne due to the coronavirus outbreak that has severely hit travel demand.

titulo: Exclusive: Agitating bondholders press Argentina to step up efforts
resumo: Agitating bondholders have written to Argentina's government, accusing it of not doing enough to allow the country's crucial debt restructuring negotiations to make progress, according to bondholder sources involved in the process.

titulo: Best Buy withdraws fiscal 2021 financial guidance
resumo: Best Buy Co Inc  said on Saturday that the company withdrew all financial guidance for fiscal 2021, for both the first quarter and full year, due to increased uncertainty related to the potential impacts of the coronavirus.

titulo: LVMH orders 40 million masks from China for France
resumo: LVMH , the world's biggest luxury goods group, is ordering 40 million health masks from a Chinese supplier to help France cope with the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Wells Fargo asks Fed to lift cap on growth to support customers: FT
resumo: Wells Fargo & Co  has asked the U.S. Federal Reserve to remove an asset cap introduced during its accounts scandal in order to allow it to support businesses and customers hit by the coronavirus economic fallout, the Financial Times reported.

titulo: Starbucks closes most U.S., Canada cafes, moves to drive-through
resumo: Starbucks Corp  said on Friday it will close most of its company operated cafes across north America for two weeks, limiting its services to drive-throughs, to help stem the spread of the highly contagious coronavirus.

titulo: Google launches coronavirus website in the United States
resumo: Alphabet Inc's Google said on Saturday it launched a United States-focused website with information about coronavirus guidance and testing, as the country works on slowing the spread of the highly contagious virus.

titulo: Volkswagen to close factories in Mexico to protect public health
resumo: German automaker Volkswagen will temporarily close its factories in Mexico's Puebla and Guanajuato states in a bid to preserve public health, the company's Mexican unit said in a statement on Friday, amid growing worries over the spread of the coronavirus.

titulo: Uber suspends taxi booking option in Saudi Arabia
resumo: Uber Technologies has suspended the option to book regular taxi services through its app in Saudi Arabia until further notice, a company statement said on Friday.

titulo: U.S. seeks comments on tariff cuts for Chinese imports to aid coronavirus battle
resumo: The Trump administration on Friday said it was soliciting public comments on lifting additional tariffs on Chinese imports that could help the United States battle the coronavirus pandemic, showing some flexibility in its trade war against Beijing.

titulo: United Airlines slashes international schedule by 95% for April
resumo: United Airlines Holdings Inc  said on Friday it would slash its international schedule by 95% for April because of government restrictions prohibiting travel in an effort to curb the spread of the coronavirus.

titulo: U.S. airlines call for payroll protections as aid talks continue
resumo: U.S. airlines called on Friday for a government stimulus package to help them weather the coronavirus crisis by providing payroll protections, warning that around 750,000 jobs depend on the airline industry.

titulo: U.S. senators question Amazon on coronavirus safety at warehouses
resumo: Four Democratic U.S. senators on Friday expressed concern in a letter to Amazon.com Inc Chief Executive Jeff Bezos that the world's largest online retailer has not given enough support to warehouse staff during the coronavirus outbreak, according to a copy of the message seen by Reuters.

titulo: Wells Fargo rolls out waivers, aid in response to coronavirus outbreak
resumo: Wells Fargo & Co  said on Friday it is suspending residential property foreclosure sales, evictions and involuntary automobile repossessions due to the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Bank of America boosts hiring, worker pay amid coronavirus outbreak
resumo: Bank of America Corp's consumer division has hired 1,700 people in critical support roles so far this month, a spokesman said on Friday, as the industry faces a surge in customer service demand due to concerns about the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: U.S. auto industry backs tax relief, delaying USMCA start after coronavirus
resumo: Groups representing major automakers and suppliers asked U.S. lawmakers on Friday to consider new tax relief and delay the start of a new trade deal as auto sales decline as a result of the coronavirus pandemic.

titulo: U.S. extends tax filing deadline to July 15: Mnuchin
resumo: The U.S. government will give individuals and companies an extra three months to file their 2019 tax returns, extending the deadline to July 15 amid the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: States rush to prepare for wave of U.S. unemployed
resumo: The U.S. unemployment benefits program, a key part of the safety net for the labor market, is facing its biggest test in more than a decade.

titulo: Lyft drivers can join new delivery service, founders donate salaries
resumo: Lyft Inc on Friday told drivers they could sign up for work driving deliveries under a new service as ride-hailing demand plummets during the rapid spread of coronavirus in the United States.

titulo: Goldman Sachs CEO's 2019 compensation rises 19%
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc  Chief Executive David Solomon's total compensation rose by 19%, or $4 million, for 2019 compared with 2018, according to a regulatory filing released on Friday.

titulo: Wall Street weekahead: Coronavirus uncertainty muddies views on buying opportunities for plunging stocks
resumo: U.S. stock valuations are tumbling in the wake of the coronavirus-fueled market rout, but determining when equities are cheap enough to buy is a tricky proposition.

titulo: Volkswagen tests ventilator output as carmakers join coronavirus fight
resumo: German carmaker Volkswagen  said on Friday it was joining other manufacturers around the world to explore using 3D printing to make hospital ventilators to combat the coronavirus.

titulo: U.S. to send envoy to Saudi Arabia; Texas suggests oil output cuts
resumo: The Trump administration plans to send a special energy envoy to Saudi Arabia to work with the kingdom on stabilizing the global oil market, officials said on Friday, as the U.S. scrambles to deal with a price crash so deep that regulators in Texas considered curbing production there for the first time in nearly 50 years.

titulo: Boeing suspends dividend, CEO foregoes pay after coronavirus-related aid request
resumo: Boeing Co  will cancel CEO pay, suspend its dividend and extend a pause on share buybacks, the U.S. planemaker said on Friday, as companies eager for government aid to curb fallout from the coronavirus face pressure to cut payouts to investors.

titulo: Wall Street dives, ends worst week since 2008, as New York, California impose restrictions
resumo: Wall Street wrapped up its worst week since October 2008, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average and S&P 500 sliding more than 4% on Friday as tough restrictions imposed by New York and California to try to limit the spread of the coronavirus fueled worries about damage to the U.S. economy.

titulo: Panama's Copa Airlines cancels all flights as coronavirus crisis spreads
resumo: Panama's Copa Airlines  on Friday said it will suspend all operations from March 22 until April 21, making it the first Latin American carrier to take such a drastic measure in order to weather the coronavirus crisis.

titulo: Walmart's Mexico unit pulls elderly workers from checkouts on coronavirus concerns
resumo: Grocers including Walmart de Mexico have begun bowing to pressure to pull from stores tens of thousands of elderly workers who pack bags at checkouts as concerns grow about their vulnerability to coronavirus amid panic buying nationwide.

titulo: Coronavirus death toll in Italy's Lombardy jumps some 380 in a day -source
resumo: The death toll from an outbreak of coronavirus in the northern Italian region of Lombardy, which has borne the brunt of a nationwide contagion, has risen by around 380 in a day to some 2,550, a source said on Friday.

titulo: BMW will idle South Carolina plant for two weeks starting April 3
resumo: BMW AG  said on Friday it will temporarily close its South Carolina plant for two weeks starting April 3.

titulo: Ted Baker says 38% of global retail sales hit by coronavirus
resumo: Troubled fashion retailer Ted Baker Plc  said on Friday it has shut businesses that accounted for about 38% of its global retail sales in 2020 due to the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp's Merz stays on as CEO to handle overhaul
resumo: Martina Merz, who took over as interim chief executive of Thyssenkrupp , will remain in the job until 2023 after successfully selling the group's elevator unit in a landmark deal last month, the conglomerate said on Friday.

titulo: Macy's suspends quarterly dividend, withdraws 2020 forecast
resumo: Macy's Inc  said on Friday it would suspend its quarterly dividend and withdrew its 2020 sales and profit forecasts due to ongoing disruptions from store closures on the back of the fast-spreading coronavirus.

resumo: By Silvia Ognibene, Riccardo Bastianello, Silvia Aloisi

resumo: (This corrects paragraph eight of March 4 story to quote UNCTAD report saying Honda's output will be reduced in March, rather than plants closed, and deletes reference to Hyundai.)

titulo: Factbox: How we die: coronavirus in perspective

An index of credit default swaps on 75 high-risk European companies compiled by IHS Markit -- the iTraxx Europe Crossover - has surged to the highest in almost four years. The list is pretty diverse: airlines, retailers, carmakers and others all feature.

titulo: Investors ditch market risk as stocks fall into correction

titulo: Occidental posts quarterly loss on impairment, charges
resumo: Occidental Petroleum Corp  reported a quarterly loss on Thursday, as the oil and gas producer took more than $1.7 billion in impairment and other charges.

titulo: Bed Bath & Beyond to cut about 500 jobs; expects about $26 million charge
resumo: Bed Bath & Beyond Inc  would cut about 500 jobs, including management positions, as part of a restructuring program to turn around its business, the home-furnishing retailer said on Thursday.

titulo: Tellurian stock falls after company extends U.S. LNG sale negotiations with Petronet
resumo: Tellurian Inc  stock dropped on Thursday after the company said it extended the amount of time India's Petronet LNG Ltd  had to finalize an agreement to buy liquefied natural gas (LNG) from Tellurian's Driftwood plant in Louisiana.

titulo: FCC to propose $200 million fines for U.S. cellphone carriers over consumer data disclosures
resumo: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission is set to propose fining four major U.S. mobile phone companies at least $200 million in total for improperly disclosing some consumer real-time location data, two people briefed on the matter said on Thursday.

titulo: Dow suffers record decline in points as Wall Street leads global stocks lower
resumo: The Dow industrials posted the largest-ever points drop on Thursday as Wall Street led stocks across the globe lower, with traders fretting over the economic impact of the spreading coronavirus.

titulo: Oil prices dive to lowest in over a year on coronavirus fears
resumo: Oil prices tumbled for a fifth day on Thursday to their lowest level in more than a year, as new reports of novel coronavirus cases outside China spurred investor fears that the rapidly spreading outbreak could slow the global economy.

titulo: Telecom Italia picks KKR as partner for Italian broadband
resumo: Telecom Italia (TIM) on Thursday picked KKR  as an exclusive partner to develop its ultra-broadband business, saying its board had taken positive note of a non-binding proposal by the U.S. investment firm.

titulo: Financial firms discussing coronavirus contingency plans with regulators: official
resumo: U.S.-based banks and brokers are in discussions with federal regulators about allowing staff to work from home and other business continuity arrangements amid the spread of the coronavirus, the head of a top financial trade group said on Thursday.

titulo: Amazon bars one million products for false coronavirus claims
resumo: Amazon.com Inc has barred more than 1 million products from sale in recent weeks that had inaccurately claimed to cure or defend against the coronavirus, the company told Reuters on  Thursday.

titulo: Opioid companies say lawyers' fee demand threatens settlement talks
resumo: Johnson & Johnson  and other drug companies facing  thousands of lawsuits over their role in the opioid epidemic have warned that settlement talks will be "severely" jeopardized if plaintiffs' lawyers are allowed to assess a fee payment worth billions of dollars.

titulo: Microsoft shares slip after warning of coronavirus hit to supply chain
resumo: Shares of Microsoft Corp  fell 4% on Thursday after the company warned of weakness in its PC business after a hit to its supply chain from the coronavirus outbreak, echoing similar statements from Apple Inc  and HP .

titulo: Factbox: U.S. business forecasts earnings pressure as coronavirus spreads
resumo: As the coronavirus epidemic spreads in China and beyond, U.S. companies will see profits stagnate in 2020, according to a Goldman Sachs report.

titulo: Pfizer warns of hit to financial results on continued coronavirus outbreak
resumo: Pfizer Inc  on Thursday warned that the continued spread of the coronavirus globally could have an adverse impact on its business and financial results.

titulo: U.S. economy grows steadily in fourth-quarter, but coronavirus looms large
resumo: The U.S. economy grew moderately in the fourth quarter, the government confirmed, and is facing a bumpy road in early 2020 amid the fast-spreading coronavirus that has left financial markets fearing a recession.

titulo: U.S. pending home sales jump 5.2% in January
resumo: Contracts to buy previously owned U.S. homes rebounded in January to their second-highest monthly figure in about two years as low mortgage rates boosted sales.

titulo: Thomson Reuters names new CEO, earnings top estimates
resumo: (This February 25 story refiles to delete "organic" from paragraph 8)

titulo: Fed's Evans: too early to cut growth outlook or rates on coronavirus
resumo: Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Charles Evans said on Thursday it was too soon to say if the U.S. growth forecast would be lowered due to the coronavirus epidemic, let alone if the U.S. central bank would need to cut rates. .

titulo: Fed's Evans says above-target inflation 'essential' in future
resumo: Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Charles Evans on Thursday suggested that "extraordinary accommodation" and a period of elevated inflation will be integral to the U.S. central bank's approach to fighting the next severe economic downturn.

titulo: Mastercard says coronavirus to hit first-quarter revenue
resumo: (This February 24 story corrects to say revenue to be hit between "2 and 3 percentage points" not "2% and 3%" in first paragraph)

titulo: Bayer cools prospect of imminent glyphosate settlement
resumo: German drugs and pesticides company Bayer was at pains on Thursday to quell expectations it was nearing an out-of-court settlement laying to rest claims its glyphosate-based weedkillers cause cancer.

titulo: Alphabet still facing questions over data use in its Toronto smart city project proposal
resumo: Alphabet's proposed "smart" city development in Toronto is facing fresh questions over the project's data-gathering technology from a panel advising the Canadian government-mandated body in charge of getting it built.

titulo: Goldman Sachs asks some to skip conference over coronavirus concerns
resumo: Goldman Sachs Group Inc  is asking people not to attend the bank's New York conference next week if they or someone they are close to had recently traveled to China, South Korea or parts of Italy, according to the conference website.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp sells elevator unit for $18.7 billion to Advent-led consortium
resumo: Thyssenkrupp  on Thursday said it agreed to sell its prized elevator division to a private equity consortium including Advent, Cinven and Germany's RAG foundation for 17.2 billion euros ($18.7 billion).

titulo: TIM board views positively KKR's non-binding bid for fiber-optic network
resumo: Telecom Italia (TIM) said on Thursday its board viewed positively a non-binding offer by U.S. investment firm KKR to become the Italian telecoms group's exclusive partner in developing a fiber-optic network.

titulo: Vivendi files appeal against Milan court OK to Mediaset revamp
resumo: France's Vivendi  has filed an appeal against a  decision by a Milan judge to clear the way for a planned reorganization at Mediaset , Italy's biggest commercial broadcaster said on Thursday.

titulo: With West divided, Huawei plans first European 5G factory in France
resumo: Huawei will build its first European manufacturing plant in France, the chairman said on Thursday, as the Chinese telecom giant seeks to ease worldwide concerns stoked by U.S. charges that Beijing could use its equipment for spying.

titulo: China's top container ports unclog backlog as virus curbs ease
resumo: China's top container ports are loosening the backlog of cargoes on their docks as workers return to their jobs after coronavirus travel curbs that kept them away and jammed up global supply chains have been eased.

titulo: Tesla shares slump as coronavirus hits China car registrations
resumo: Shares of Tesla Inc  fell as much as 14% on Thursday on concerns about the impact of the coronavirus on the electric-car maker's vehicle registrations in China.

titulo: DoorDash files to go public in latest IPO test for unprofitable startups
resumo: DoorDash Inc, the loss-making U.S. food delivery startup backed by SoftBank Group Corp , said on Thursday it has filed for an initial public offering (IPO), setting it up to be one of the most high-profile listings of 2020.

titulo: Starbucks' CEO says 85% of stores in China are open
resumo: Starbucks Corp  said on Thursday 85% of its stores in China were open, weeks after the coffee chain shut nearly half of its over 4,000 stores in the world's second-largest economy due to the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Instant View: U.S. stocks confirm correction on pandemic fears
resumo: Wall Street's main indexes tumbled on Thursday, putting them  more than 10% below record highs set earlier this month and indicating that they then entered an intraday correction, as the rapid spread of the coronavirus globally deepened worries about growth and corporate earnings.

titulo: Cargill bans international travel for staff amid coronavirus outbreak
resumo: International grains trader and processor Cargill Inc has banned all non-essential travel for its employees for the next two weeks due to the coronavirus outbreak, company spokeswoman April Nelson told Reuters on Thursday.

titulo: French carmaker PSA unveils mini electric Citroen car
resumo: Citroen, one of French carmaker PSA's brands, unveiled a mini electric car on Thursday aimed at young city dwellers.

titulo: South African unit of Philip Morris welcomes tax stance on cigarette alternatives
resumo: The South African unit of cigarette maker Philip Morris International Inc  welcomed the government's decision to introduce a lower excise tax on heated tobacco products than on regular cigarettes.

titulo: Wall Street set for steep fall on alarm over fast-spreading virus
resumo: Futures indicated that the Dow Jones Industrials index would shed more than 300 points at the open on Thursday after the rapid spread of the coronavirus outside China deepened worries about growth and corporate earnings.

titulo: Taxi group wins right to join Uber's London license appeal case
resumo: A taxi association will be able to participate in Uber's appeal against the loss of its operating license in the British capital city set to be heard in full from July 6, a British judge decided on Thursday.

titulo: Nokia shares rise on report of possible mergers, assets sales
resumo: Shares in Nokia Oyj  rose in a falling market on Thursday, after Bloomberg News reported that the Finnish telecom network equipment maker was considering asset sales and mergers.

titulo: WPP shares slump to lowest since 2012 as sales slide
resumo: WPP  shares plunged to their lowest level in nearly eight years on Thursday after a sharp slowdown in fourth-quarter trading derailed the latest recovery attempt at the world's biggest advertising company.

titulo: Zalando to tap designer labels, vintage to boost sales
resumo: Zalando  plans to offer more premium and luxury ranges and move into the vintage clothing market as the online fashion retailer predicted slower sales growth in 2020 after a strong final quarter to 2019.

titulo: U.S. airlines, hotels extend rebooking options as coronavirus spreads
resumo: (This February 25 story corrects to say that Marriott has issued cancellation waiver until March 15 in paragraph 13)

titulo: Blackstone's Schwarzman says cannot buy Bloomberg while owning Refinitiv
resumo: Blackstone Group Inc  Chief Executive Stephen Schwarzman said the world's biggest private equity firm cannot buy Bloomberg should it come up for sale as long as the firm owns competitor Refinitiv.

titulo: Banks must step up climate risk disclosures, Lagarde says
resumo: Banks must improve transparency on their exposure to climate- change risk and central banks need to pay closer attention as the entire financial sector is at risk, European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said on Thursday.

titulo: AB InBev sees 10% hit to first-quarter profit from coronavirus
resumo: The world's largest brewer Anheuser-Busch InBev  forecast a 10% decline in first-quarter profit on Thursday after the coronavirus outbreak hit beer sales during the Chinese New Year, sending its shares skidding.

titulo: Aston Martin shares slide as losses deepen and finance chief leaves
resumo: Aston Martin  shares slumped to a record low on Thursday after the British luxury carmaker said its losses ballooned last year and its chief financial officer would leave by the end of April.

titulo: StanChart posts strong results but coronavirus, economic headwinds to hamper profit growth
resumo: Standard Chartered  booked a robust 46% jump in annual profit but warned a key earnings target would take longer to meet as the coronavirus epidemic adds to headwinds in its main markets of China and Hong Kong.

titulo: Thai tycoons in fray as Tesco sets bid deadline for $9 billion Asia business: sources
resumo: British retailer Tesco  has asked bidders to submit binding offers for its Asian business by Friday, in a deal that will see Thai billionaires fight for an asset valued at up to $9 billion, sources with direct knowledge of the matter said.

titulo: IKEA reduces climate footprint for the first time
resumo: Carbon emissions throughout the full value chain of IKEA furniture fell for the first time last year, brand owner Inter IKEA said on Thursday citing increased use of renewable energy.

titulo: ABB confirms profit goals for robotics division
resumo: ABB's  robotics division is "well placed" to increase sales by broadening its business beyond the struggling automotive sector, the Swiss engineering company said on Thursday, confirming its profitability goal.

titulo: Microsoft wants to invest in Indonesia data centers, says Indonesian president
resumo: Microsoft Corp is keen to invest in data centers in Indonesia which will soon make regulatory changes to facilitate the move, the country's president said after meeting with the software giant's CEO on Thursday.

titulo: Panera Bread launches $8.99 monthly unlimited coffee subscription
resumo: Panera Bread Co on Thursday is launching an $8.99 per month unlimited coffee subscription as it aims to grow its breakfast traffic and loyalty program membership.

titulo: Delta cutting flights to South Korea; JetBlue waives U.S. change fees
resumo: Delta Air Lines Inc  said on Wednesday it would temporarily cut flights to and from South Korea in half, citing global health concerns related to the coronavirus outbreak as U.S. airlines grapple with lagging travel demand.

titulo: JPMorgan names veteran Gori as new Asia Pacific chief executive
resumo: JPMorgan Chase & Co  on Thursday named veteran Filippo Gori as its new Asia Pacific chief executive officer to oversee strategy and operations of the Wall Street bank across 17 markets in the region.

titulo: Waymo joins backlash against California self-driving data requirement
resumo: Alphabet Inc's Waymo and General Motors Co's Cruise are leading a backlash against a California reporting requirement on self-driving vehicle test data that the companies claim is not relevant or accurate in measuring performance or progress.

titulo: Google defeats conservative nonprofit's YouTube censorship appeal
resumo: Google persuaded a federal appeals court on Wednesday to reject claims that YouTube illegally censors conservative content.

titulo: Facebook to ban misleading ads about coronavirus
resumo: Facebook Inc said on Wednesday it would ban advertisements for products offering any cures or prevention around the coronavirus outbreak, and those that create a sense of urgency around the situation.

titulo: China's CATL aims to raise $2.85 billion for EV battery projects
resumo: CATL , China's top electric vehicle battery maker, said it aims to raise up to 20 billion yuan ($2.85 billion) in a private placement of shares to fund its battery projects and boost working capital.

titulo: Coronavirus clouds Apple's timeline for new iPhones
resumo: Travel restrictions to China because of the coronavirus have come just as Apple Inc's engineers usually jet off to Asia to perfect the production of this fall's new iPhones, former employees and supply chain experts told Reuters.

titulo: Hyundai to decide hydrogen fuel-cell factory location this year - exec
resumo: Hyundai Motor Co  and its affiliates are ready to decide the location of a new hydrogen fuel-cell system factory this year, which could be in South Korea or overseas, an executive said on Wednesday.

titulo: Senator Rubio seeks U.S. review of sale of AT&T unit to Czech-owned conglomerate
resumo: U.S. Senator Marco Rubio on Wednesday asked the Trump administration to review the national security implications of AT&T's Inc's planned sale of its majority stake in Central European Media Group Enterprises (CME) to the Czech-owned conglomerate PPF Group.

titulo: Global stocks outlook still positive, but vulnerable to coronavirus: Reuters poll
resumo: Global stocks have further to run this year, but the likelihood of a correction of 10% or more is high, according to Reuters polls of market strategists, with the severity of the economic hit from the coronavirus outbreak being the biggest risk.

titulo: Exclusive: IMF, World Bank consider 'virtual' Spring Meetings as virus spreads
resumo: Growing concerns inside the International Monetary Fund and the World Bank about the spread of the new coronavirus have prompted the institutions to consider scaling back their Spring Meetings in April or hold them by teleconference, people familiar with the discussions said on Wednesday.

titulo: Apple investor vote sounds 'warning' over China app takedowns
resumo: An Apple Inc shareholder proposal critical of the company's app removals in China received a relatively high level of support at the iPhone maker's annual meeting on Wednesday,  enough to push the company to respond, experts said.

titulo: Canada mulling made-in-Ottawa supplement to Boeing's 737 MAX flight manual
resumo: Transport Canada is mulling a Canadian supplement to the Boeing  737 MAX's U.S.-developed flight manual, as the regulator  weighs going beyond steps taken by the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to end a global grounding of the model after two fatal crashes.

titulo: Wall Street falls more slowly as investors parse coronavirus fears
resumo: The S&P 500 fell for a fifth straight day on Wednesday and while its decline was slower than the last few days, the session was volatile as investors reacted to headlines about coronavirus and sought to gauge its economic fallout.

titulo: Toll Brothers says 11 home sales in California impacted by coronavirus
resumo: Home builder Toll Brothers Inc  said on Wednesday that the coronavirus outbreak in China had led to shortages of lighting fixtures and small appliances, forcing it to delay the sales of 11 homes in California, one of its biggest markets.

titulo: A new calculator sheds light on high-flying U.S. unicorn price tags
resumo: Common shares in many high-flying venture-backed startup companies are worth less than headlines lead some people to believe, and an online calculator that two finance professors debuted on Wednesday can now quantify that discrepancy.

titulo: Microsoft expects Windows unit to miss revenue outlook on coronavirus impact
resumo: Microsoft Inc said on Wednesday it does not expect to meet its quarterly revenue forecast for its Windows and personal computing business as a result of the coronavirus outbreak, sending its shares down more than 1% in after-market trading.

titulo: NMC Health removes CEO amid investigation of UAE firm's finances
resumo: NMC Health  has removed Chief Executive Prasanth Manghat with immediate effect and granted its finance chief extended sick leave, as more details emerge from an investigation into the UAE healthcare firm's finances.

titulo: Stocks, oil fall as coronavirus fears grip markets
resumo: Stocks and oil prices resumed their fall on Wednesday after  authorities said more than 80 people were being monitored for the novel coronavirus in New York state's Long Island, adding to concern over its spread in Europe, Asia and the Middle East.

titulo: U.S. stock exchanges say prepared for coronavirus
resumo: U.S. stock exchanges said on Wednesday they were watching coronavirus-related developments closely and had contingency plans in place to continue running if any of their operations were affected.

titulo: EU open to more spending in countries hit by coronavirus outbreak
resumo: Italy and other euro zone countries hit by the coronavirus outbreak are expected to benefit from waivers under EU fiscal rules that will allow them to spend more to tackle the emergency, the bloc's economics commissioner said on Wednesday.

titulo: Oil prices drop to lowest in more than a year as coronavirus spreads
resumo: Oil prices fell to their lowest in more than a year on Wednesday after hundreds of new coronavirus cases reported in Europe and the Middle East stoked fears that energy demand would decline, and on concerns that the virus could spread across the United States.

titulo: Nokia explores strategic options: Bloomberg News
resumo: Finnish telecom network equipment maker Nokia Oyj is exploring strategic options and is working with advisers to consider potential asset sales and mergers, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

titulo: U.S. Supreme Court allows retirement plan lawsuit against Intel
resumo: The U.S. Supreme Court on Wednesday refused to back stricter deadlines for workers to sue retirement plans over alleged mismanagement, ruling Intel Corp cannot avoid a suit accusing it of unlawfully making high-risk investments that cost retirement plan beneficiaries hundreds of millions of dollars.

titulo: Wells Fargo workers seek Washington's help with internal gripes
resumo: A contingent of aggrieved Wells Fargo & Co workers are taking complaints to Capitol Hill, saying they feel brushed aside by management.

titulo: U.S. consumer spending could see one-two punch from stocks drop, coronavirus
resumo: The coronavirus outbreak has yet to spread meaningfully to U.S. shores, but fears of it alone have already eviscerated some $2 trillion of American stock market value, setting off a market rout that could stymie consumer spending - even before other economic effects of the disease are felt.

titulo: Aston Martin investor blazes green trail for buyout fund
resumo: The top shareholder of carmaker Aston Martin is on a mission to turn investments from theme parks to high-end furniture brands "carbon positive" by the end of 2020, as buyout funds battle to woo investors increasingly focused on tackling global warming.

titulo: Starbucks to launch Beyond Meat plant-based breakfast sandwich in Canada
resumo: Starbucks Corp  said on Wednesday its Canadian stores will start selling a Beyond Meat Inc  plant-based breakfast sandwich next week, the first time the world's biggest coffee chain will offer an imitation meat product.

titulo: Nestle asks staff to stop international travel amid coronavirus epidemic
resumo: Nestle SA  has asked its staff to halt international travel for business purposes through March 15 as a precautionary measure amid the rapid spread of the coronavirus globally, the Swiss company said on Wednesday.

titulo: Bayer chairman quits as Roundup settlement talks progress
resumo: Bayer  Chairman Werner Wenning, one of the architects of a $63 billion takeover deal that has left the German crop protection company fighting costly lawsuits, will step down in April, it said on Wednesday.

titulo: Pressure mounts on Bayer CEO to fix legal problems as chairman quits
resumo: The departure of Bayer  Chairman Werner Wenning will turn up the heat on CEO Werner Baumann to resolve the healthcare to agriculture group's legal problems, two of the firm's biggest German shareholders said on Wednesday.

titulo: SmileDirectClub sell-off gives short sellers reason to grin
resumo: Traders short selling SmileDirectClub had a lot to smile about on Wednesday, earning paper profits of over $115 million as the online dental company's stock plummeted 26% following a disappointing quarterly report.

titulo: Walmart in talks with possible buyers for Asda stake
resumo: Walmart , the world's largest retailer, is in talks with possible buyers of a stake in its British supermarket Asda, which it failed to combine with Sainsbury's  last year.

titulo: Lloyds, Virgin Money cut jobs as UK bank customers shift online
resumo: Lloyds Banking Group  and Virgin Money  pressed ahead with hundreds of job cuts in the face of union opposition, as British banks try to keep up with customers moving online.

titulo: Morgan Stanley deputy chief financial officer to retire
resumo: Morgan Stanley's Deputy Chief Financial Officer Paul Wirth will retire after 15 years with the bank, according to a memo sent to employees seen by Reuters on Wednesday.

titulo: Morgan Stanley conference-goers asked to self-report coronavirus exposure
resumo: Morgan Stanley  is asking people attending its technology, media and telecom conference in San Francisco next week to report if they had visited several countries with reported coronavirus cases in recent weeks.

titulo: Fund investors retreat from U.S. stocks as coronavirus fears weigh
resumo: Investors pulled nearly $3.3 billion out of mutual funds and exchange-traded funds that hold U.S. stocks last week, snapping the longest streak of asset gains since July, according to data released Wednesday by the Investment Company Institute.

titulo: BP to quit main U.S. refining lobby over carbon policy
resumo: BP  will leave the main U.S. refining lobby and two other trade groups as new Chief Executive Bernard Looney spurs some of the oil sector's most ambitious targets for curbing carbon emissions.

titulo: Panasonic to exit solar production at Tesla's New York plant as partnership frays
resumo: Panasonic Corp  said it would exit solar cell production at Tesla Inc's New York plant, the latest sign of strain in a partnership where Panasonic's status as the U.S. electric vehicle (EV) maker's exclusive battery supplier is ending.

titulo: Virgin Money plans to cut 500 jobs and close 52 branches
resumo: British challenger bank Virgin Money  is planning to cut 500 jobs as it presses ahead with cost-cutting after bulking up through the merger of Virgin Money and Clydesdale and Yorkshire Banking Group in 2018, it said on Wednesday.

titulo: Lufthansa and United weigh takeover of Portugal's TAP: Sueddeutsche
resumo: Lufthansa  and United Airlines  are considering taking over TAP-Air Portugal, a German newspaper reported on Wednesday.

titulo: Delta Air waives travel change fees for South Korea as coronavirus spreads
resumo: Delta Air Lines Inc  said on Wednesday it has expanded its travel change fee waiver to include Seoul, South Korea, through April 30, as coronavirus spreads to regions outside China.

titulo: Jury weighs verdict for ex-Barclays bankers in Qatar fee trial
resumo: The jury in the London trial of three former Barclays bankers accused of funneling secret fees to Qatar in return for emergency funding during the financial crisis in 2008, retired to consider its verdict on Wednesday.

titulo: BlackRock Vice Chairman and co-Founder Barbara Novick to step down
resumo: BlackRock Inc  vice chairman and co-founder Barbara Novick will step down from her day-to-day duties at the asset manager, according to an internal memo seen by Reuters on Wednesday.

titulo: Disney shares down 2% after 'surprise' CEO change
resumo: Shares of Walt Disney Co  fell 2% on Wednesday after the media giant's surprise move to replace top boss Bob Iger raised questions on Wall Street if his successor Bob Chapek had sufficient experience in the entertainment business.

titulo: Walt Disney's Iger steps down as CEO, parks head Chapek to take reins
resumo: Walt Disney Co's  Robert Iger will step down as chief executive officer, handing the reins to Disney Parks head Bob Chapek, the company said on Tuesday, ending years of speculation on who will take over Hollywood's most powerful studio.

titulo: Electrolux says implementing Italy travel ban until March 9 due to Coronavirus
resumo: Swedish home appliance maker Electrolux  said on Wednesday it had decided on ban travel by its employees to and from Italy, where it has manufacturing facilities in some regions affected by the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Google to invest over $10 billion in 2020 on U.S. data centers, offices
resumo: Alphabet Inc's  Google said on Wednesday it would invest more than $10 billion in offices and data centers across the United States this year.

titulo: Profit rise lifts Peugeot shares ahead of Fiat merger
resumo: Peugeot maker PSA Group said on Wednesday profitability reached a record high in 2019, results that contrasted with those of many rivals and boosted the group's shares as it beds down a merger with Italy's Fiat Chrysler.

titulo: Ford's incoming COO focuses on cost cuts, launches, change
resumo: Ford Motor Co's  incoming chief operating officer outlined his priorities for the company's turnaround on Wednesday, including cost cuts and more efficient new-vehicle launches in a year in which it will introduce a redesigned F-150 full-sized pickup truck.

titulo: Too early for the ECB to contemplate coronavirus response: Makhlouf
resumo: It is too early for the European Central Bank to assess whether it needs to respond to the coronavirus epidemic with policy, even if the epidemic presents a risk to growth, Irish central bank chief Gabriel Makhlouf said on Wednesday.

titulo: Toyota says Japan plants may be affected by virus-related supply issues
resumo: Toyota Motor Corp  on Wednesday said that operations at its plants in Japan may be affected by supply chain issues linked to the new coronavirus outbreak in the coming weeks, as the global outbreak gathers pace.

titulo: Trump, Modi hope talks lead to phase one of U.S.-India trade deal: White House
resumo: U.S. President Donald Trump and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi have agreed to "promptly" conclude ongoing trade talks that they hope can lead to the first phase of a bilateral U.S.-India trade deal, the White House said.

titulo: PG&E plans to raise up to $25.68 billion by selling securities
resumo: California power producer PG&E Corp said on Tuesday it plans to raise up to $25.68 billion by selling securities, as it works its way out of the bankruptcy process.

titulo: Salesforce co-CEO Keith Block steps down, Marc Benioff to be sole chief
resumo: Salesforce.com Inc  said on Tuesday Keith Block has stepped down as co-chief executive officer, making Marc Benioff the sole CEO of the business software company, sending its shares down nearly 3% in extended trading.

titulo: AI computing startup SambaNova raises $250 million in BlackRock-led funding
resumo: Artificial intelligence computing firm SambaNova Systems said on Tuesday it has raised $250 million in a funding round led by BlackRock Inc to expand its customer base, reflecting investors' appetite for the market.

titulo: Musk's SpaceX rocket production facility approved by Port of Los Angeles
resumo: SpaceX, the private rocket company of high tech entrepreneur Elon Musk, won final approval on Tuesday to build a research and manufacturing plant for its deep-space Starship project on a tract of vacant land at the Port of Los Angeles.

titulo: Mattel receives SEC subpoena on whistleblower letter
resumo: Mattel Inc  had received a subpoena in December from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission seeking documents related to a whistleblower letter and subsequent investigation, the U.S. toymaker said in a filing on Tuesday.

titulo: Economic contagion spreads beyond China to other Asian economies: Reuters poll
resumo: The effects of the coronavirus outbreak are likely to reverberate beyond China as most major economies in the region are expected to either slow down significantly, halt or shrink outright in the current quarter, Reuters polls found.

titulo: Liberian former IMF Africa chief named as deputy managing director
resumo: International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva on Tuesday nominated former IMF official Antoinette Sayeh to become a deputy managing director at the Fund.

titulo: Fed's Kaplan says unclear right now if coronavirus calls for U.S. rate change: WSJ
resumo: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Robert Kaplan said on Tuesday events are still too fluid around the coronavirus outbreak to say the U.S. central bank needs to lower short-term rates, the Wall Street Journal reported on Tuesday.

titulo: Wall Street's sell-off deepens as coronavirus fears intensify
resumo: The Dow and the S&P 500 tumbled 3% on Tuesday in their fourth straight day of losses as the coronavirus spread further around the world and investors offloaded risky assets as they struggled to gauge the economic impact.

titulo: Coronavirus fears pull U.S. chip index into a correction
resumo: Wall Street's main semiconductor benchmark tumbled into correction territory on Tuesday in its deepest four-day rout since the financial crisis, as the coronavirus spread further around the world and deepened fears about the global economy.

titulo: Fiat Chrysler CEO Manley received 13.3 million euros in 2019 compensation: filing
resumo: Fiat Chrysler Automobiles NV (FCA) Chief Executive Mike Manley received compensation of 13.28 million euros ($14.45 million) for 2019, in line with a target set by the company, the Italian-American automaker said in a filing on Tuesday.

titulo: In first public results, Branson's Virgin Galactic posts $73 million quarterly loss
resumo: Billionaire Richard Branson's space tourism company, Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc , said on Tuesday its fourth-quarter net loss widened to $73 million from a year-ago loss of $46 million as it reported its first results as a publicly traded company.

titulo: U.S. trade rep Lighthizer to meet British counterpart as allies gear up for talks
resumo: U.S. Trade Representative Robert Lighthizer will meet with Britain's trade minister Liz Truss this week, British government officials said on Tuesday, as the two allies gear up for post-Brexit trade talks that could prove contentious.

titulo: Tesla and U.S. regulators strongly criticized over role of Autopilot in crash
resumo: The National Transportation Safety Board on Tuesday sharply criticized Tesla Inc.'s lack of system safeguards in a fatal 2018 Autopilot crash in California and called U.S. regulators' approach in overseeing the driver assistance systems "misguided."

titulo: Mallinckrodt agrees to $1.6 billion opioid settlement, unit to seek bankruptcy
resumo: Mallinckrodt Plc on Tuesday said it had agreed to a $1.6 billion settlement proposal in which its generic drug business would file for bankruptcy in order to resolve thousands of lawsuits alleging it helped fuel the U.S. opioid epidemic.

titulo: Three U.S. Senate Democrats propose sweeping reforms after Boeing 737 MAX crashes
resumo: Three Democratic U.S. senators on Tuesday introduced sweeping legislation to reform how new airplanes are certified and overseen by U.S. regulators after two fatal Boeing 737 MAX crashes killed 346 people.

titulo: Once bulletproof, tech stocks now among market's biggest losers
resumo: Technology shares -- a key engine of the stock market's climb to records over the last several months -- are now among those leading Wall Street's plunge on growing concerns over the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Stocks, oil fall further on accelerating coronavirus concerns
