resumo: Finnish telecom networks maker Nokia Oyj said on Wednesday it has agreed to acquire privately-held U.S.-based company Elenion Technologies to boost its optical networking business and broaden its offering to telecoms operators.

titulo: Kuwaiti parliament to investigate Airbus aircraft orders
resumo: Kuwaiti lawmakers agreed on Wednesday to set up a committee to look into whether Airbus's  aircraft orders from the Gulf Arab state involved alleged corruption, the state news agency reported.

titulo: U.S. urges EU to use 5G by Ericsson, Nokia, Samsung, seen on par with Huawei
resumo: EU countries have no reason to use 5G mobile technology from Huawei because Sweden's Ericsson , Finland's Nokia  and South Korea's Samsung  are on par with the Chinese group in the field, a senior U.S. diplomat said.

titulo: Air France faces new strikes over domestic network cuts
resumo: Air France  unions have called for strikes at its domestic carrier Hop as pilots seek improved pay and conditions and ground staff press for job guarantees, union officials said.

titulo: Exclusive: Vodafone, Telecom Italia offer rivals access to some sites to ease EU concerns - EU paper
resumo: Vodafone  and Telekom Italia  have offered to allow rivals access to sites in some cities for up to nine years, a proposal aimed at allaying EU antitrust concerns over the creation of Italy’s largest mobile tower company, according to an EU document seen by Reuters.

titulo: Qatar Airways spends $600 million to lift stake in BA owner to 25%
resumo: Qatar Airways has bought a $600 million stake in British Airways-owner IAG  to increase its holding to 25.1%, strengthening its position ahead of a change in IAG management.

titulo: Their market shut, traders in China's Silicon Valley do business from bags
resumo: While coronavirus fears curtail much of China's bustle, traders are getting back to business at the world's largest electronics market, buying and selling from bags and suitcases on the streets outside their shut premises.

titulo: Adidas, Puma warn of coronavirus hit to China business
resumo: German sportswear makers Adidas  and Puma  have both warned that the coronavirus outbreak was hurting their business in China due to store closures and fewer Chinese tourists travelling and shopping in other markets.

titulo: UniCredit CEO says no M&A deal after Intesa's surprise UBI bid
resumo: The head of UniCredit  repeated in a letter to staff on Wednesday that Italy's biggest bank had no intention of engaging in merger and acquisition deals following Intesa's  blitz move to buy rival UBI Banca .

titulo: General Motors to lay off all 1,500 plant workers in Thailand following sale
resumo: General Motors will begin laying off around 1,500 employees in Thailand in June, after announcing the sale of its production plants in the country, a government official said on Wednesday.

titulo: Citi, Deutsche start talks to sell $9 billion Dubai port company debt: sources
resumo: Citi  and Deutsche Bank  have started talks with other banks to sell roughly $9 billion in debt Dubai raised to take full control of DP World  and refinance borrowings of Dubai World, sources familiar with the matter said on Wednesday.

titulo: Renault shares fall after Moody's cuts its debt to 'junk' status
resumo: Renault's  shares fell on Wednesday after Moody's cut its rating on the French carmaker's debt to "junk" status, citing weaker profitability as the company restructures and grapples with falling demand.

titulo: BlackRock bolsters European management as part of regional expansion: memo
resumo: BlackRock , the world's biggest asset manager, has appointed two executives to oversee its operations in continental Europe as part of efforts to expand in the region, a memo to staff on Wednesday seen by Reuters showed.

titulo: Merged T-Mobile U.S. to close valuation gap on Big Two: Deutsche Telekom CEO
resumo: The merger between T-Mobile US  and Sprint  is within reach, the head of its main owner Deutsche Telekom said, forecasting that the combined business would quickly close a valuation gap on market leaders AT&T  and Verizon .

titulo: Puma warns of hit to sales and profits from coronavirus
resumo: German sportswear group Puma  on Wednesday said it expected China's coronavirus outbreak to hit its sales and profits in the first quarter but it still hopes to reach its targets for 2020.

titulo: Hyundai Motor chairman to give up board seat as succession looms
resumo: Hyundai Motor Group  Chairman Mong-Koo Chung plans to give up his board seat, in the latest sign that the octogenarian patriarch of South Korea's second-largest conglomerate is preparing to hand the reins over to his son.

titulo: Exclusive: Unilever, 3M on list of firms eligible for China loans to ease coronavirus crisis - sources
resumo: Shanghai has compiled a list of firms, including local units of multi-nationals Unilever PLC  and 3M Co , as eligible for millions of dollars in subsidized loans to ease any blow from the coronavirus outbreak, according to bankers and documents seen by Reuters.

titulo: SoftBank to borrow $4.5 billion pledging domestic telco's shares
resumo: SoftBank Group Corp  said on Wednesday it plans to borrow up to 500 billion yen ($4.5 billion) from 16 domestic and foreign financial institutions using almost a third of its stake in telco SoftBank Corp  as collateral.

titulo: Exclusive: India antitrust investigators see no evidence of collusion by IndiGo, SpiceJet and others - sources
resumo: Investigators with India's antitrust watchdog have found no evidence that the country's biggest airline, IndiGo , and four rival carriers colluded to fix ticket prices, three sources with direct knowledge of the matter told Reuters.

titulo: Metro Bank names interim boss Dan Frumkin CEO
resumo: Metro Bank  on Wednesday named interim Chief Executive Dan Frumkin to the role permanently, tasking him with leading the British lender past an accounting scandal that plunged it into crisis last year.

titulo: Uber closing office in downtown Los Angeles
resumo: Uber Technologies Inc  is closing its office in downtown Los Angeles, where the ride-hailing company employs customer support staff, to focus on its bigger locations.

titulo: Hormel joins Tyson Foods, JBS in removing drug banned by China from hog supply
resumo: Spam-maker Hormel Foods Corp  is eliminating a growth drug banned by China from its hog supply, the company said on Tuesday, joining rivals that are seeking to increase meat sales to Chinese buyers grappling with a pork shortage.

titulo: U.S. judge rejects Huawei challenge to federal law restricting its business
resumo: A federal judge in Texas on Tuesday rejected Chinese telecommunications equipment maker Huawei Technologies Co Ltd's constitutional challenge to a U.S. law that restricted its ability to do business with federal agencies and their contractors.

titulo: China races to contain job losses as coronavirus batters economy
resumo: When cameraman Mark Xia returned to his job this month after holidays, the Shanghai video production house where he worked told him to take three months of leave with no pay, as a coronavirus epidemic takes a toll of China's businesses.

titulo: Oil near flat; virus impact offsets Libya supply disruptions
resumo: Oil prices were near flat on Tuesday, pressured by concerns over the impact on crude demand from the coronavirus outbreak in China, but prices drew support from a reduction in supply from Libya.

titulo: Samsung poised to benefit from China virus woes afflicting Apple, other rivals
resumo: Samsung Electronics stands to be a major beneficiary of the China production problems announced by rival Apple Inc on Monday, reaping the rewards of a decade-long bet on low-cost smartphone manufacturing in Vietnam.

titulo: Trump blasts proposed U.S. restrictions on sale of jet parts to China
resumo: President Donald Trump objected on Tuesday to U.S. proposals that would prevent companies from supplying jet engines and other components to China's aviation industry and suggested he had instructed his administration not to implement them.

titulo: Stocks slide on Apple's virus warning, euro near three-year low
resumo: Gold rose and global equity markets slid on Tuesday after Apple Inc  said it was unlikely to meet its sales guidance because of the coronavirus outbreak in China, a warning highlighting the epidemic's threat to global growth and corporate profits.

titulo: Huawei accuses U.S. of overlooking HSBC misconduct to go after Chinese firm
resumo: U.S. prosecutors overlooked apparent violations of U.S. sanctions against Iran by HSBC Holdings in exchange for the British bank's cooperation with a government investigation of Huawei Technologies, lawyers for the Chinese telecoms giant said.

titulo: HSBC to slash investment bank, 35,000 jobs in strategy overhaul
resumo: HSBC Holdings PLC said on Tuesday it would shed $100 billion in assets, shrink its investment bank and revamp its U.S. and European businesses in a drastic overhaul that will mean 35,000 jobs cut over three years.

titulo: HSBC eyes $600 million in losses if Coronavirus persists
resumo: HSBC could be forced to take $600 million in additional provisions against loan losses if the Coronavirus outbreak persists into the second half of this year, Chief Financial Officer Ewan Stevenson said on Tuesday.

titulo: Boy Scouts of America files bankruptcy in wake of abuse lawsuits
resumo: The Boy Scouts of America said on Tuesday it had filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy amid a flood of lawsuits over allegations of child sexual abuse stretching back decades.

titulo: Coronavirus threatens Apple supply chain, sales; shares drop
resumo: Shares of Apple Inc  fell 2 % on Tuesday and dragged the stocks of its suppliers across the globe lower, after the iPhone maker warned of lower sales in the current quarter acknowledging that the coronavirus outbreak was pressuring its supply chain.

titulo: Dow, S&P 500 decline after Apple's sales warning
resumo: The Dow and S&P 500 fell on Tuesday after a sales warning from Apple left investors assessing the impact of the coronavirus outbreak on U.S. companies.

titulo: Alstom, Bombardier shares fall after $6.7 billion rail deal
resumo: Shares of Alstom SA and Montreal-based Bombardier Inc fell on Tuesday after the French firm agreed to buy its Canadian rival's rail division for up to 6.2 billion euros ($6.7 billion), a deal likely to draw scrutiny from competition regulators and unions concerned about job cuts.

titulo: Consumer groups, unions oppose fix for planned merger of AbbVie and Allergan
resumo: A group of unions and consumer groups wrote to U.S. antitrust enforcers on Tuesday to oppose a proposed remedy that could lead to U.S. approval for AbbVie Inc's  planned purchase of Allergan Plc .

titulo: Virgin Galactic's stock soars, fueled by retail investors
resumo: Virgin Galactic Holdings Inc  shares surged 24% on Tuesday, extending a rally since early December to over 400% and evoking a warning from an analyst who likes the space tourism company but warns it has become overbought.

titulo: Coronavirus dents bookings for Holiday Inn owner IHG
resumo: InterContinental Hotels (IHG) warned on Tuesday that fewer travellers are booking its rooms in China because of the coronavirus outbreak.

titulo: Walmart sees slower online sales growth after tepid holiday quarter
resumo: Walmart Inc  on Tuesday forecast online sales growth to slow this year after posting lower-than-expected holiday quarter results, revealing the pressure traditional retailers are facing to keep pace with consumers who are increasingly shopping online.

titulo: U.S. mulls cutting Huawei off from global chip suppliers, with TSMC in crosshairs
resumo: The Trump administration is considering changing U.S. regulations to allow it to block shipments of chips to Huawei Technologies [HWT.UL] from companies such as Taiwan's TSMC , the world's largest contract chipmaker, two sources familiar with the matter said.

titulo: Exclusive: Vodafone, Telecom Italia offer rivals access to some sites to ease EU concerns – document
resumo: Vodafone  and Telecom Italia (TIM)  have offered to give rivals access to some of their infrastructure for up to nine years to allay EU antitrust concerns over plans to merge their tower units, according to a document seen by Reuters.

titulo: Jaguar Land Rover's UK output at risk from coronavirus-hit supplies
resumo: Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) has flown Chinese parts in suitcases to Britain to maintain production and could run out after two weeks as the impact of the coronavirus outbreak hits firms across countries and industries.

titulo: Hedge funds use DIY strategies to gauge coronavirus impact
resumo: As investors crunch numbers to determine how the coronavirus will hit China’s economy, hedge fund manager Nathaniel Polachek has tied much of his outlook to the fate of a ship anchored near Weihai, China.

titulo: Shades of Detroit? Germany's auto heartlands in peril as 'golden age' fades
resumo: When Kristin and Thomas Schmitt took out a mortgage and bought a house last summer, the German couple's dream looked as if it was coming true. Two months later, they learned that the tire factory where both work would be shut down early next year.

titulo: Dell to sell cybersecurity unit for $2.08 billion
resumo: Dell Technologies Inc  said on Tuesday it would sell its cybersecurity unit RSA for $2.08 billion to a consortium led by Symphony Technology Group, Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan Board and AlpInvest Partners.

titulo: 20 years after dot-com peak, tech dominance keeps investors on edge
resumo: As Wall Street approaches the 20th anniversary of the piercing of the dot-com bubble, today's decade-old rally led by a few small players shows some similarities that cautious investors are keeping an eye on.

titulo: Exclusive: JPMorgan shakes up investment bank in leadership makeover - sources
resumo: JPMorgan  is reshuffling senior management at the top of its investment bank, naming two new global co-heads and shifting some of its most senior dealmakers into new jobs focused purely on bringing in business, two sources told Reuters.

titulo: Factbox: JPMorgan names new global leaders at investment bank - sources
resumo: JPMorgan  has shifted some of its top dealmakers to a newly-created executive committee of global chairs of investment banking to deepen ties with major clients and mentor the next generation of star bankers, two sources told Reuters.

titulo: Breen to return as DuPont CEO
resumo: DuPont  has brought back management veteran Edward Breen as its chief executive officer, in a move that the industrial materials maker is hoping will help lift sluggish sales since its split from DowDuPont Inc.

titulo: Exclusive: Samsung wins 5-nanometer modem chip contract from Qualcomm - sources

titulo: Exclusive: Tesla in talks to use CATL's cobalt-free batteries in China-made cars - sources
resumo: Tesla  is in advanced stages of talks to use batteries from CATL  that contain no cobalt - one of the most expensive metals in electric vehicle (EV) batteries - in cars made at its China plant, people familiar with the matter said.

titulo: Amazon's Bezos pledges $10 billion to climate change fight
resumo: Amazon  Chief Executive Officer Jeff Bezos will commit $10 billion to fund scientists, activists, nonprofits and other groups fighting to protect the environment and counter the effects of climate change, he said on Monday.

titulo: StanChart commits $75 billion toward sustainable development goals
resumo: Standard Chartered on Tuesday said it will provide $75 billion in financing by 2024 toward sustainable development and clean energy projects, as it expands on its aspirations to support the Paris Agreement on climate change.

titulo: Nissan CEO tells angry shareholders he is ready to be sacked if no turnaround
resumo: Nissan's  new chief executive said on Tuesday he would accept being fired if he fails to turn around Japan's second biggest automaker which is grappling with plunging sales in the aftermath of the scandal surrounding ex-chairman Carlos Ghosn.

titulo: If Apple is hurting due to the coronavirus, its suppliers and rivals likely are too
resumo: Apple Inc's  surprise warning that it will likely fall short of this quarter's sales target due to the coronavirus epidemic points to much pain for its chip and other suppliers as well as for rivals who also rely on China to build their products.

titulo: French finance minister warns Renault against job cuts, factory closures
resumo: French Finance Minister Bruno Le Maire warned Renault  on Tuesday against shutting factories in France and cutting jobs there after the carmaker announced "no taboos" cost cuts last week.

titulo: Recession risk forces BOJ to row back on inflation goal

titulo: BTG Pactual founder Esteves returns to center stage amid speculation on role
resumo: Banco BTG Pactual SA  founder Andre Esteves will be the marquee speaker at a wide-ranging event on Tuesday sponsored by the Brazilian investment bank, marking a return to the limelight for one of the country's best-known bankers.

titulo: China to grant tariff exemptions on 696 U.S. goods to support purchases

titulo: SoftBank spends $2.5 billion to get second Vision Fund off the ground: sources

titulo: European new car sales down 7.4% in January: ACEA

titulo: France's Accor partners with Visa under loyalty program

titulo: DP World returns to full state ownership, takes on $8.1 billion debt
resumo: (This 17th Feb. story corrects final paragraph to "raised" not "valued")

titulo: GM shuts Australia, NZ operations; sells Thai plant to Great Wall
resumo: General Motors Co  said it would wind down its Australian and New Zealand operations and sell a Thai plant in the latest restructuring of its global business, costing the U.S. auto maker $1.1 billion.

titulo: Alstom to buy Bombardier rail unit for up to $6.7 billion
resumo: France's Alstom SA has agreed to buy the rail division of Canada's Bombardier Inc for up to 6.2 billion euros ($6.7 billion) to create the world's No. 2 train manufacturer and better take on Chinese leader CRRC Corp.

titulo: Timeline: Bombardier's journey from Ski-Doo maker to business jet maker
resumo: Alstom SA on Monday agreed to buy Bombardier Inc's train business for up to 6.2 billion euros ($6.7 billion), days after Airbus SE agreed to buy out Bombardier's remaining stake in the A220 passenger jet program, helping the company improve its financial position.

titulo: Xerox courts HP shareholders as takeover battle heats up
resumo: U.S. printer maker Xerox Holdings Corp  is hosting a dinner for HP Inc  shareholders this week as it seeks investor support to overcome the personal computer maker's resistance to its $35 billion takeover bid, according to people familiar with the matter.

titulo: EU industry chief tells Facebook to adapt to EU, not other way round

titulo: Euro zone ministers discuss fiscal boost options as virus worries weigh
resumo: Euro zone finance ministers discussed ways to pursue a more growth-friendly fiscal policy mix on Monday, in a potential nudge to Germany and the Netherlands to spend more as fears of a downturn grow in the wake of the coronavirus epidemic.

titulo: Oil edges up as output cut hopes offset coronavirus concern
resumo: Oil prices inched up on Monday as concerns over the economic fallout from the coronavirus outbreak in China were offset by expectations that potential production cuts from major producers could tighten global crude supply.

titulo: French government to ensure Alstom-Bombardier deal creates value: minister
resumo: The French government will make sure the acquisition of Bombardier's train business by Alstom creates value for the European rail industry and the staff of both companies, Finance Minister Bruno Le Maire said on Monday.

titulo: Exclusive: Italian government leaning towards third term for Eni CEO - sources
resumo: The Italian government is leaning toward reappointing Claudio Descalzi as CEO of state-controlled oil firm Eni, provided he will work with a new board to speed up efforts to cut carbon emissions, four senior political sources told Reuters.

titulo: New York drops fight against T-Mobile-Sprint merger
resumo: New York on Sunday dropped its fight against the $40 billion merger of U.S. wireless carriers T-Mobile US Inc  and Sprint Corp , saying the state would not appeal a judge's approval of the deal.

titulo: Thyssenkrupp shortlists buyout firms for elevator unit, Kone out
resumo: By Christoph Steitz, Tom Käckenhoff, Arno Schuetze and Edward Taylor

titulo: EU industry chief warns of more onerous tech rules by year end
resumo: The European Union will decide by the end of the year whether to impose rules reining in the market power of U.S. tech giants, the bloc's industry chief said on Monday as he criticised Facebook's proposals as insufficient to address EU concerns.

titulo: Chinese support measures buoy world stocks
resumo: Global shares were buoyant on Monday as the promise of further policy stimulus from China to counteract the economic hit from a coronavirus outbreak calmed nervous investors.

titulo: WTO gives somber goods trade outlook, sees virus threat
resumo: Growth of global trade in goods is likely to remain weak in early 2020, the World Trade Organization (WTO) said on Monday, adding that the below-trend performance could become even worse due to the new coronavirus.

titulo: Alibaba offers $2.86 billion in loans to firms hit by coronavirus outbreak
resumo: Alibaba Group Holdings  said on Monday its affiliate Ant Financial's MYBank unit would offer 20 billion yuan ($2.86 billion) in loans to companies in China in light of the coronavirus outbreak, with preferential terms for Hubei firms.

titulo: Cathay Pacific flags 'significant' drop in first-half profit, capacity cuts due to coronavirus
resumo: Cathay Pacific Airways  is expecting a significant drop in its first-half results and has also cut capacity due to the coronavirus outbreak, it said on Monday.

titulo: NMC Health founder Shetty resigns as turmoil deepens

titulo: Timeline: NMC Health's expansion and ailments
resumo: NMC Health  said on Monday its founder BR Shetty had resigned as joint non-executive chairman, months after the UAE healthcare group was thrown into turmoil by doubts over its finances.

titulo: Japan on brink of recession as economy contracts, virus heightens risk
resumo: Japan's economy shrank at the fastest pace in almost six years in the December quarter as a sales tax hike hit consumer and business spending, raising the risk of a recession as China's coronavirus outbreak chills global activity.

titulo: William Hill names DS Smith's Adrian Marsh as CFO
resumo: British bookmaker William Hill  on Monday named cardboard maker DS Smith's  Chief Financial Officer Adrian Marsh as its finance head.

titulo: U.S. firms in China report staff shortages, say coronavirus hitting global operations: AmCham
resumo: Nearly half of the U.S. companies in China say their global operations are already seeing an impact from business shutdowns due to the coronavirus epidemic, according to a poll by Shanghai's American Chamber of Commerce (AmCham).

titulo: BOJ's Kuroda flags more easing if virus impact worsens: Sankei
resumo: Bank of Japan Governor Haruhiko Kuroda said the central bank would consider additional rapid easing if the coronavirus outbreak significantly threatened Japan's economy and price trends, the Sankei newspaper reported on Monday.

titulo: Timeline: General Motors streamlines its international operations
resumo: General Motors Co  is retreating from more markets outside the United States and China, saying on Sunday that it will wind down its Australian and New Zealand operations, while selling a plant in Thailand.

titulo: Climate change could make premiums unaffordable: QBE Insurance
resumo: Australia's QBE Insurance Group  said on Monday climate change could make some premiums unaffordable, especially for customers exposed to extreme weather events, and was a "material risk" for its operations across the globe.

titulo: UK digital bank Monzo plans to hire 500 and relaunch paid accounts
resumo: Fast-growing British digital bank Monzo plans to hire up to 500 people and forecasts it will get 5.5 million users this year, as it prepares to have another crack at charging some customers to turn a profit.

titulo: Japan ready to take steps vs virus impact on tourism: Economy Minister
resumo: Japanese Economy Minister Yasutoshi Nishimura said on Monday the government was ready to take all necessary steps flexibly with an eye on the impact of the coronavirus outbreak on the economy and tourism.

titulo: Great Wall says to buy GM's Thailand car plant
resumo: China's Great Wall Motor  said on Monday it had signed a binding agreement to purchase a car plant from General Motors  in Thailand.

titulo: Clock's ticking for Nissan boss Uchida to show he has a plan: sources
resumo: Nissan's new CEO Makoto Uchida doesn't have time to work his way into the job. He is effectively on probation and has a matter of months to show he can revive the ailing automaker, according to three people familiar with the thinking of some on the company's board.

titulo: British Airways COO and director of people to leave
resumo: British Airways said on Sunday its Chief Operating Officer, Klaus Goersch, and its Director of People, Angela Williams, were leaving the company.

titulo: U.S. peach grower awarded $265 million from Bayer, BASF in weedkiller lawsuit
resumo: A Missouri jury's $265 million award to peach grower Bill Bader in his lawsuit against herbicide providers Bayer and BASF has raised the stakes for the two companies as at least 140 similar cases head to U.S. courts later this year.

titulo: Alstom nears deal to buy Bombardier's train unit: source
resumo: France's Alstom SA  is close to clinching a deal to buy Bombardier Inc's  train business which will give the unit an enterprise value of $7 billion, according to a source familiar with the matter.

titulo: Tesla ordered by German court to stop cutting down trees for Gigafactory
resumo: A German court on Sunday ordered Tesla Inc to stop clearing forest land near the capital Berlin to build its first European car and battery factory, a victory for local environmental activists.

titulo: Lebanon's MEA reverses move to charge in dollars
resumo: Lebanon's state-owned carrier Middle East Airlines (MEA) reversed a decision to accept payment only in U.S. dollars after a backlash on Sunday, Lebanese media and an MEA source said.

titulo: Amazon, Flipkart seek rollback of new Indian tax on online sellers
resumo: Amazon and Walmart's Flipkart are among online retailers demanding that India scale back a proposed tax on third-party sellers on their platforms, saying the burden of compliance will hurt the fledgling industry, according to documents seen by Reuters.

titulo: Carrefour to buy 30 stores in Brazil from rival Makro
resumo: The Brazilian unit of France's Carrefour SA  has agreed to buy 30 stores from rival retailer Makro's Brazilian subsidiary for 1.95 billion reais ($453.69 million), the French retailer said in a securities filing on Sunday.

titulo: Regulators should allow RTL and ProSieben to merge: Rabe
resumo: The head of publisher Bertelsmann  said its TV arm RTL  should be allowed to merge with German rival ProSiebenSat.1 , to give them a fighting chance against U.S. streaming giants.

titulo: Treat us like something between a telco and a newspaper, says Facebook's Zuckerberg
resumo: Online content should be regulated with a system somewhere between the existing rules used for the telecoms and media industries, Facebook  CEO Mark Zuckerberg told global leaders and security chiefs on Saturday.

titulo: U.S. weighs blocking GE engine sales for China's new airplane: sources
resumo: The U.S. government is considering whether to stop General Electric Co from continuing to supply engines for a new Chinese passenger jet, according to people familiar with the matter, casting uncertainty over China's efforts to enter the civil aviation market.

titulo: Toyota to resume China output at three plants next week
resumo: Toyota Motor Corp  said on Saturday it plans to resume output at three of its four main auto plants in China next week.

titulo: Factbox: Carmakers look to resume China output after virus forced closures
resumo: Automakers including Daimler, Ford and Tesla are looking to resume production in China after being forced to shut factories following the outbreak of a new coronavirus.

titulo: Rebuffed by UK, U.S. pitches 'big tent' for Huawei rivals in Europe
resumo: The United States is seeking to rally European support for competitors to Huawei Technologies following disappointment in Washington over Britain's decision to use 5G equipment made by the Chinese company.

titulo: Vodafone assesses payment to India in dispute over dues
resumo: Vodafone Idea Ltd said on Saturday it was assessing how much it would pay the Indian government as part of dues owed and said it proposed making a payment in the next few days.

titulo: From wind power to cow manure: oil traders seek new profit recipe

titulo: Factbox: Big oil traders start investing in renewables
resumo: The world's big oil trading firms are investing heavily in environmentally-friendly projects as they face increasing  pressure from investors, governments, activists and financiers to find a sustainable business model.

titulo: Investment bank loss mars Thiam's final act at Credit Suisse
resumo: Credit Suisse posted its highest annual profit in nearly a decade on Thursday, but volatile earnings at its investment bank and trading divisions muted outgoing Chief Executive Tidjane Thiam's swan song.

titulo: Activists in dinghy urge Credit Suisse off climate 'collision course'
resumo: Climate activists clad in Titanic-era costumes gathered in a rubber dinghy outside Credit Suisse's headquarters on Thursday, to urge incoming chief executive Thomas Gottstein to steer the bank off a climate "collision course".

titulo: U.S. accuses Huawei of stealing trade secrets, assisting Iran

titulo: Huawei cyber security chief says no operator gives it access to intercept equipment
resumo: Huawei's cyber security chief said on Friday that he was not aware of any mobile operator ever having given the Chinese company access to the equipment used to intercept calls when required to do so by security services.

titulo: Univision in talks with investor group for possible sale: source
resumo: Spanish-language broadcaster Univision Communications Inc is in sale talks with a consortium of investors, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters on Friday.

titulo: U.S. SEC Commissioner defends proposal on shareholder voting rules
resumo: A top official at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday defended an agency shareholder voting proposal amid "misconception" received from the SEC's public consultation process over new limits to vote on thorny issues like climate change disclosure and executive pay.

titulo: AllianzGI pushes for more climate data ahead of AGM season
resumo: German fund manager Allianz Global Investors is pushing every company it invests in to improve their climate-related disclosures ahead of the season for annual shareholder meetings.

titulo: Airbus defense division to start talks on job cuts
resumo: The head of the defense business of Airbus  said he would start talks with labor representatives next week on job cuts, as the German-based group retrenches following setbacks with its A400M military transporter.

titulo: Airbus regrets U.S. tariffs, hopes for change when WTO authorizes EU retaliation
resumo: European aerospace group Airbus  said on Saturday it deeply regretted the U.S. decision to increase tariffs on aircraft imported from the European Union and said it would hurt U.S. airlines and their customers.
